```text
     1→# Public Distribution Product & Delivery Plan (V5a)
     2→
     3→Recorded: 2026-03-15
     4→
     5→Status: Consolidated implementation-ready specification
     6→
     7→Scope: Unify the product-output naming and layout contract (V4a), the delivery
     8→mechanics (V4b), and the publication architecture (V3) into one actionable
     9→document with phased implementation, explicit rollback gates, and a complete
    10→rework map of every file that must change.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## 1. Why V5a Exists
    15→
    16→V3, V4a, and V4b each advanced the plan, but they left three problems
    17→unresolved:
    18→
    19→1. **Contradictory public-tree models.** V3 still published the corpus inside
    20→   an `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper with a separate "distribution shell"
    21→   above it. V4a eliminated wrappers and moved to a direct-root `release-tree/`
    22→   where modules sit at root. The two specs describe different public trees and
    23→   different URL contracts. V5a resolves this: the V4a direct-root model wins.
    24→
    25→2. **No per-file rework map.** V4a listed categories of scripts that "clearly
    26→   need updates" but did not map the exact old-contract strings inside each
    27→   file, the exact replacement strings, or the order of safe changes. Without
    28→   that map, a developer must survey the whole codebase before writing a single
    29→   line. V5a provides the map.
    30→
    31→3. **No rollback-safe phase gates.** V4b described a recommended sequence but
    32→   did not define what condition must be true before advancing to the next
    33→   phase, or what happens if one phase fails. V5a adds explicit go/no-go
    34→   checkpoints so that a failed phase leaves the pipeline in a known-good
    35→   state.
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## 2. Consolidated Locked Decisions
    40→
    41→These decisions are final. They supersede any conflicting statement in V1
    42→through V4b.
    43→
    44→| # | Decision | Source |
    45→| --- | --- | --- |
    46→| D1 | The public release tree is direct-root. No `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper in any public surface. | V4a §9, supersedes V2/V3 |
    47→| D2 | Every public module folder uses `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`, and optional `types/`. | V4a §7.5 |
    48→| D3 | Module names are not repeated in child filenames inside the module folder. | V4a §13 |
    49→| D4 | The pipeline main output root contains `release-tree/` and `support-tree/`. Only `release-tree/` is publishable. | V4a §9 + V4b §2 |
    50→| D5 | `release-inputs/release-include/` adds or overrides files after generation, before validation. | V4a §10 + V4b §3 |
    51→| D6 | The former top-level public `L2-semantic/` is replaced by per-module `chunked-reference/`. | V4a §9.3 |
    52→| D7 | All public output names come from shared config or lib constants, not scattered string literals. | V4a §6 goal 9, V4b §4 |
    53→| D8 | The GitHub organization is `openwrt-docs4ai`. Two target repos: `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` (Pages) and `corpus` (Releases). | V3 §3, §5 |
    54→| D9 | The ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` (project-named, not `corpus/`). | V3 §7 |
    55→| D10 | Authentication uses a GitHub App with installation tokens, not long-lived PATs. | V2 §6, V3 §14 |
    56→| D11 | Git history on target repos is append-only. No force-push. | V2 §9.2 |
    57→| D12 | Same-day reruns update the existing release and replace the dated ZIP asset. | V2 §9.3, V3 §15.2 |
    58→| D13 | Existing source-repo deploy behavior must remain intact even if new distribution steps fail. | REVIEW §2.D, V2 §9.4 |
    59→| D14 | The `openwrt-condensed-docs` name is internal to the source repo only; it must never appear in any public path, URL, or visible label. | V3 §4.1, V4a §7.8 |
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## 3. Resolved Conflicts
    64→
    65→### 3.1 V3 wrapper vs V4a direct-root
    66→
    67→V3 §6 defined a public tree with `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `CHANGES.md`,
    68→`changelog.json`, `cross-link-registry.json`, `repo-manifest.json`, and
    69→`signature-inventory.json` at product root.
    70→
    71→V4a §2, §9 eliminated all of those from the public tree.
    72→
    73→**Resolution:** V4a wins. The public release tree contains only:
    74→
    75→```text
    76→release-tree/
    77→├── README.md
    78→├── AGENTS.md
    79→├── llms.txt
    80→├── llms-full.txt
    81→├── index.html
    82→├── {module}/
    83→│   ├── llms.txt
    84→│   ├── map.md
    85→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
    86→│   ├── chunked-reference/
    87→│   │   └── {topic}.md ...
    88→│   └── types/ (optional)
    89→│       └── {module}.d.ts
    90→└── ... (more modules)
    91→```
    92→
    93→The items V3 listed at root that V4a removed are relocated:
    94→
    95→| V3 root item | V5a location |
    96→| --- | --- |
    97→| `L1-raw/` | `support-tree/raw/` |
    98→| `L2-semantic/` | redistributed into `{module}/chunked-reference/` |
    99→| `CHANGES.md` | `support-tree/telemetry/CHANGES.md` |
   100→| `changelog.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/changelog.json` |
   101→| `cross-link-registry.json` | `support-tree/manifests/cross-link-registry.json` |
   102→| `repo-manifest.json` | `support-tree/manifests/repo-manifest.json` |
   103→| `signature-inventory.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/signature-inventory.json` |
   104→
   105→### 3.2 V3 delivery-root overlays vs V4a release-include
   106→
   107→V3 §8 defined `delivery-root/common/`, `delivery-root/pages/`, and
   108→`delivery-root/release/` as source-controlled overlay directories.
   109→
   110→V4a §10 defined `release-inputs/release-include/` as the single overlay point.
   111→
   112→**Resolution:** Merge both. Use one overlay parent directory with
   113→target-specific children:
   114→
   115→```text
   116→release-inputs/
   117→├── release-include/          (common overlay, applied to all surfaces)
   118→├── pages-include/            (Pages-only overlay, e.g. .nojekyll)
   119→└── release-repo-include/    (release-repo-only overlay, if ever needed)
   120→```
   121→
   122→This preserves the V3 concern about target-specific files (`.nojekyll` for
   123→Pages) while keeping the V4a single-overlay concept.
   124→
   125→### 3.3 Module-local README.md
   126→
   127→V4a §14 question 1 asked whether module-local `README.md` should be in the
   128→first fixed-schema contract.
   129→
   130→**Resolution:** Not in the first pass. The `llms.txt` + `map.md` routing pair
   131→is sufficient. A module `README.md` can be added later if proven necessary.
   132→
   133→### 3.4 Oversized module strategy
   134→
   135→V4a §14 question 4 asked how `bundled-reference.md` handles oversized modules.
   136→
   137→**Resolution:** For the first pass, keep the existing sharding behavior:
   138→
   139→- `bundled-reference.md` remains the stable index filename.
   140→- If a module exceeds the token limit, `bundled-reference.md` becomes an index
   141→  that points to `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` files.
   142→- `chunked-reference/` topic files are always present regardless of sharding.
   143→- The sharded parts sit alongside `bundled-reference.md`, not inside
   144→  `chunked-reference/`.
   145→
   146→### 3.5 Support-tree persistence
   147→
   148→V4a §14 question 2 asked whether `support-tree/` stays persistent.
   149→
   150→**Resolution:** `support-tree/` is ephemeral during CI runs and may be uploaded
   151→as a CI artifact for debugging. It is not a persistent on-disk contract. Its
   152→internal layout may change without notice.
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## 4. Complete Rework Map
   157→
   158→This section maps every file in the current codebase that contains
   159→old-contract names, the exact strings that must change, and what they become.
   160→
   161→### 4.1 Shared configuration — `lib/config.py`
   162→
   163→Current state: defines `OUTDIR`, `L1_RAW_WORKDIR`, `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR`, and
   164→`REPO_MANIFEST_PATH`. All paths are relative to `OUTDIR` which defaults to
   165→`openwrt-condensed-docs`.
   166→
   167→Required changes:
   168→
   169→| Current | New | Notes |
   170→| --- | --- | --- |
   171→| `OUTDIR` defaults to `openwrt-condensed-docs` | `OUTDIR` becomes the main output root (formerly "working output" only) | Internal stages still write here |
   172→| - | Add `RELEASE_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / release-tree` | New constant |
   173→| - | Add `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / support-tree` | New constant |
   174→| - | Add `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR` = `release-inputs/release-include` | New constant, relative to repo root |
   175→| - | Add public-name constants: `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`, `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME` | Central ownership of `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference`, `types` |
   176→| `L1_RAW_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L1-raw` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/raw` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   177→| `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L2-semantic` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/semantic-pages` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   178→
   179→**Risk reduction:** Do NOT change `OUTDIR` semantics for early stages. Early
   180→stages continue to write `L1-raw/` and `L2-semantic/` under the existing
   181→`OUTDIR`. Only the late assembly stage reads from those locations and writes
   182→into `release-tree/`.
   183→
   184→### 4.2 Assembly stage — `05a-assemble-references.py`
   185→
   186→Current hard-coded strings and their required changes:
   187→
   188→| Line range | Current string | Required change |
   189→| --- | --- | --- |
   190→| Line 6-8 (docstring) | `OUTDIR/{module}/{module}-complete-reference.md`, `{module}-skeleton.md` | Update docstring to new names |
   191→| Line 36 | `L2_DIR = os.path.join(OUTDIR, "L2-semantic")` | Keep for reading; assembly output paths change |
   192→| Line 157 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` | `f"bundled-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` |
   193→| Line 330 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.md"` | `"bundled-reference.md"` (from config constant) |
   194→| Line 331 | `f"{module}-skeleton.md"` | `"map.md"` (from config constant) |
   195→| Line 327 | `out_mod_dir = os.path.join(OUTDIR, module)` | `os.path.join(RELEASE_TREE_DIR, module)` |
   196→| Line 202 | `f"# {module} Complete Reference"` | `f"# {module} Bundled Reference"` |
   197→| Line 293 | `f"# {module} (Skeleton Semantic Map)"` | `f"# {module} Navigation Map"` |

   205→2. Write `map.md` (renamed from `{module}-skeleton.md`)
   206→3. Copy or link each L2 topic page into `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md`

   208→This is the most significant new code in the rework.

   303→

   333→
   334→## 5. Phased Implementation With Rollback Gates
   335→
   336→Each phase has a go/no-go gate. If the gate fails, the previous phase's
   337→state is the safe rollback point. The pipeline continues to produce the
   338→current old-contract output until the phase that switches public output
   339→is explicitly activated.
   340→
   341→### Phase 0: Add shared configuration constants
   342→
   343→**Goal:** Centralize all public output names in `lib/config.py`.
   344→
   345→**Changes:**
   346→- Add constants for `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`, `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`,
   347→  `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`,
   348→  `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME`,
   349→  `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR`, `PAGES_INCLUDE_DIR`
   350→
   351→**Gate:** All existing tests pass with no behavioral change. The new constants
   352→are defined but not yet consumed by any stage.
   353→
   354→**Rollback:** Revert the `lib/config.py` additions. Zero risk.
   355→
   356→---
   357→
   358→### Phase 1: Add release assembly stage (dual output, feature-flagged)
   359→
   360→**Goal:** Create a new late-stage script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` that
   361→reads from the existing pipeline output and writes the V5a `release-tree/`.
   362→
   363→**Changes:**
   364→- New script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   365→  - Creates `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   366→  - Creates `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`
   367→  - For each module:
   368→    - Copies/renames `{module}-complete-reference.md` → `bundled-reference.md`
   369→    - Copies/renames `{module}-skeleton.md` → `map.md`
   370→    - Copies L2 topic pages into `chunked-reference/`
   371→    - Copies optional `types/` files
   372→    - Generates module `llms.txt` with new paths
   373→  - Copies root files (`llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`,
   374→    `index.html`) with path rewrites
   375→  - Moves support-only artifacts to `support-tree/`
   376→- Feature flag: `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` env var (default `false`)
   377→- When flag is off, existing stages still produce old-contract output
   378→
   379→**Why a new script instead of modifying 05a:** This is the single most
   380→important risk-reduction decision. Script `05a` currently works. Inserting
   381→rename logic into it creates a change where a bug can break the existing
   382→pipeline. A new script that runs after `05a` and reads its output means:
   383→
   384→- `05a` continues to produce known-good old-contract files
   385→- `05e` produces the new contract files alongside them
   386→- Both can be validated before any consumer switches
   387→- If `05e` is broken, deleting it restores the old pipeline
   388→
   389→**Gate:** When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`:
   390→- `release-tree/` contains the full V5a layout
   391→- `support-tree/` contains L1, L2, telemetry, and manifests
   392→- All old-contract output still exists unchanged under `OUTDIR/`
   393→- All existing tests pass (they still check `OUTDIR/`)
   394→- A new parallel test suite validates `release-tree/`
   395→
   396→**Rollback:** Set `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false`. The script is skipped. Old
   397→output is unchanged.
   398→
   399→---
   400→
   401→### Phase 2: Add release-tree validation
   402→
   403→**Goal:** Create new tests and a validator that assert the V5a contract on
   404→`release-tree/`.
   405→
   406→**Changes:**
   407→- New test file:
   408→  `tests/pytest/pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py`
   409→  - Asserts every module has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`,
   410→    `chunked-reference/`
   411→  - Asserts no `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere
   412→    in `release-tree/`
   413→  - Asserts bundled-vs-chunked parity for at least one module
   414→  - Asserts `release-tree/` contains no `support-tree/` items
   415→- Extend `08-validate-output.py` with a `release-tree` validation mode
   416→  (behind feature flag)
   417→
   418→**Gate:** All new tests pass against the `release-tree/` produced by Phase 1.
   419→All existing tests still pass against old `OUTDIR/` output.
   420→
   421→**Rollback:** Delete the new test file and validation mode. Zero risk.
   422→
   423→---
   424→
   425→### Phase 3: Add release-include merge
   426→
   427→**Goal:** Implement the overlay merge from `release-inputs/release-include/`.
   428→
   429→**Changes:**
   430→- Create `release-inputs/release-include/` in the source repo (initially
   431→  empty or with a `README.md` explaining its purpose)
   432→- Create `release-inputs/pages-include/.nojekyll`
   433→- Create `release-inputs/release-repo-include/` (empty, reserved)
   434→- Add merge logic to `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   435→  after generating `release-tree/`, copy `release-include/` contents on top
   436→- Add merge-precedence test
   437→
   438→**Gate:** Include merge produces expected results. A test file placed in
   439→`release-include/` appears in the merged `release-tree/`. A generated file
   440→replaced by an include-override contains the override content. Existing tests
   441→pass.
   442→
   443→**Rollback:** Remove the merge logic from `05e`. The `release-include/`
   444→directory stays inert.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→### Phase 4: Switch late stages to write into release-tree
   449→
   450→**Goal:** Modify scripts `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` to consume
   451→`RELEASE_TREE_DIR` when the feature flag is on.
   452→
   453→**Changes:**
   454→- `05a`: output paths use config constants; filenames use new names
   455→- `05b`: text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   456→- `06`: routing text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   457→- `07`: path labels and links updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   458→- `08`: validation logic updated per §4.6
   459→- Script `05e` is retired (its functionality is absorbed into the modified
   460→  stages)
   461→
   462→This is the riskiest phase because it modifies working scripts.
   463→
   464→**Risk mitigation:**
   465→- Feature flag controls whether stages write to old or new paths
   466→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false` proves old contract
   467→  is intact
   468→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` proves new contract
   469→  works
   470→- Only after both pass green does the flag default change
   471→
   472→**Gate:** Full smoke tests pass in both flag states. The `release-tree/`
   473→output matches what Phase 1's `05e` produced. The old `OUTDIR/` output matches
   474→what the pipeline produced before Phase 4.
   475→
   476→**Rollback:** Reset feature flag to `false`. All stages revert to old output
   477→paths.
   478→
   479→---
   480→
   481→### Phase 5: Update test suite to new contract
   482→
   483→**Goal:** Modify `smoke_pipeline_support.py`, `pytest_02`, and `pytest_06`
   484→to assert the new contract as the primary contract.
   485→
   486→**Changes:**
   487→- Update all assertions per §4.7, §4.8, §4.9
   488→- Old-contract assertions are removed
   489→- Feature flag default changes to `true`
   490→
   491→**Gate:** Full test suite passes with the new contract as primary. Manual
   492→inspection of `release-tree/` output confirms correctness.
   493→
   494→**Rollback:** Restore old assertions and set flag to `false`.
   495→
   496→---
   497→
   498→### Phase 6: Provision public infrastructure and deploy
   499→
   500→**Goal:** Set up the external GitHub organization, repos, and deploy workflow.
   501→
   502→**Changes:**
   503→1. Create `openwrt-docs4ai` GitHub organization
   504→2. Create `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` repo (Pages)
   505→3. Create `corpus` repo (Releases)
   506→4. Create and install GitHub App
   507→5. Store secrets in source repo
   508→6. Add deploy workflow steps per §4.10
   509→7. First live publish
   510→
   511→**Gate:** Live Pages site serves direct-root content. Release repo has a
   512→fresh commit. ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` with the V5a layout.
   513→No `openwrt-condensed-docs`, `L1-raw`, or `L2-semantic` visible anywhere.
   514→
   515→**Rollback:** Disable the new workflow steps. Source-repo deploy continues
   516→unchanged.
   517→
   518→---
   519→
   520→### Phase 7: Remove old contract and feature flag
   521→
   522→**Goal:** Clean up the codebase after live validation.
   523→
   524→**Changes:**
   525→- Remove `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` feature flag
   526→- Remove old-contract code paths
   527→- Retire script `05e` if not already retired in Phase 4
   528→- Update `DEVELOPMENT.md` and architecture docs
   529→
   530→**Gate:** Full test suite, CI pipeline, and live deploy all green without the
   531→old contract code paths.
   532→
   533→**Rollback:** Restore the feature flag and old paths. This is the only phase
   534→with a moderate rollback cost, which is why it is last.
   535→
   536→---
   537→
   538→## 6. Rework Summary By Risk Level
   539→
   540→| Risk | Phase | Files affected | What can go wrong | Mitigation |
   541→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
   542→| **None** | 0 | `lib/config.py` | Nothing; additive only | Constants unused until Phase 1 |
   543→| **Low** | 1 | New `05e` script | New script bugs produce bad `release-tree/` | Does not touch existing output; feature-flagged off |
   544→| **Low** | 2 | New test file, `08` extension | Bad test logic gives false confidence | Tests run against Phase 1 output; manual cross-check |
   545→| **Low** | 3 | `release-inputs/`, `05e` merge logic | Include merge overwrites wrong file | Merge is additive; test asserts exact behavior |
   546→| **High** | 4 | `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` | Bug in modified script breaks existing pipeline | Feature flag; dual smoke test; rollback to flag=false |
   547→| **Medium** | 5 | Test support, pytest files | Wrong assertions pass bad output | Diff old vs new assertions for parity |
   548→| **Medium** | 6 | Workflow YAML, external infra | Deploy fails or publishes wrong tree | Deploy is additive after existing deploy; gatekeeper checks |
   549→| **Low** | 7 | Cleanup only | Premature removal of fallback | Only after live validation |
   550→
   551→---
   552→
   553→## 7. Final Release Tree Contract
   554→
   555→When all phases are complete, the published product tree looks like this:
   556→
   557→```text
   558→(repository root or ZIP root openwrt-docs4ai/)

   585→├── ucode/

   596→
   597→### 7.1 Items guaranteed absent from the public tree
   598→
   599→- `L1-raw/`
   600→- `L2-semantic/`
   601→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
   602→- `corpus/`

   608→- `changelog.json`
   609→- `CHANGES.md`
   610→- Any `.meta.json` sidecar
   611→
   612→---
   613→
   614→## 8. Delivery Surfaces
   615→
   616→| Surface | Root | Source |
   617→| --- | --- | --- |
   618→| Pages site | `https://openwrt-docs4ai.github.io/` | Validated `release-tree/` + `pages-include/` |
   619→| Release repo | `https://github.com/openwrt-docs4ai/corpus` | Validated `release-tree/` + `release-repo-include/` |
   620→| ZIP download | `openwrt-docs4ai-YYYY-MM-DD.zip` → `openwrt-docs4ai/` | Validated `release-tree/` (no target-specific overlays) |
   621→
   622→All three surfaces share the same validated `release-tree/` as their content
   623→source. Target-specific overlays (`.nojekyll` for Pages) are applied after.
   624→
   625→---
   626→
   627→## 9. Gatekeeper Rules
   628→
   629→Before any cross-repo publish, the pipeline must assert:
   630→
   631→| Check | Assertion |
   632→| --- | --- |
   633→| Root router exists | `release-tree/llms.txt` exists and is > 512 bytes |
   634→| Root index exists | `release-tree/index.html` exists |

   637→| Fixed schema | Every module dir has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/` |
   638→| Chunked non-empty | Every module's `chunked-reference/` has at least one `.md` file |
   639→
   640→Failure of any check blocks publish and fails the job visibly.

```text
     1→# openwrt-docs4ai Architecture
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This repository is a documentation production pipeline, not an application runtime. Its job is to collect OpenWrt documentation from multiple upstream sources, normalize it into stable intermediate layers, and publish compact outputs that are useful to humans, IDE tooling, and LLM workflows.
     6→
     7→The primary engineering goal for the current stage is maintainable correctness on Windows with verified remote publication behavior. Local validation remains the first gate, and GitHub Actions is now a confirmed verification and deployment layer rather than an unproven future target.
     8→
     9→The repository also has a dual-role documentation boundary: maintainer guidance lives in the source tree under `docs/`, while the generated AI-facing navigation surface lives under `openwrt-condensed-docs/`. The generated-corpus routing contract is authoritative for `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, module `llms.txt`, and `AGENTS.md`; a source-repo root `llms.txt` remains explicitly deferred.
    10→
    11→## Repository Zones
    12→
    13→| Path | Role | Notes |
    14→| --- | --- | --- |
    15→| `.github/scripts/` | Numbered pipeline scripts | `00` is workflow orchestration. Whole numbers denote stage boundaries, and letter suffixes denote sibling scripts within a stage family. A bare stage id cannot coexist with same-family lettered siblings. |
    16→| `.github/workflows/` | GitHub Actions workflows | Remote execution only. Current stabilization work does not assume GitHub behavior is already verified. |
    17→| `lib/` | Shared Python support code | Shared config, file writing, extraction helpers, and other reusable logic. |
    18→| `tools/` | Non-numbered maintainer support tools | Local-only operator CLIs and helpers that are not part of the numbered hosted pipeline surface. |
    19→| `tests/` | Local deterministic tests and smoke runners | Local verification is required before remote workflow testing. |
    20→| `docs/specs/v12/` | Active v12 technical specifications | These are the current engineering references. |
    21→| `docs/archive/v12/` | Archived planning and review material | Historical context only. Not authoritative for implementation. |
    22→| `openwrt-condensed-docs/` | Published output root | Stable output tree for generated artifacts and local inspection. |
    23→| `tmp/` | Ephemeral working area | Never authoritative. Safe to delete between runs. |
    24→| `templates/` | Static content templates | Only keep templates that are actually consumed by the live pipeline. |
    25→
    26→## Layer Model
    27→
    28→| Layer | Meaning | Primary Location | Persistence |
    29→| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    30→| `L0` | Untouched upstream source inputs | `tmp/repo-*` | Ephemeral |
    31→| `L1` | Raw normalized markdown plus sidecar metadata | `tmp/L1-raw/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/` for inspection | Generated |
    32→| `L2` | Semantic markdown with YAML frontmatter and cross-links | `tmp/L2-semantic/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/` for inspection | Generated |
    33→| `L3` | Navigational and operational outputs | `openwrt-condensed-docs/` and module subdirectories | Published |
    34→| `L4` | Complete-reference index files plus optional sharded parts | `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/` | Published |
    35→| `L5` | Telemetry and drift outputs | `openwrt-condensed-docs/` | Published |
    36→
    37→## Naming Conventions
    38→
    39→### Script numbering
    40→
    41→- `00` denotes orchestration or workflow entry points.
    42→- Whole numbers denote dependency boundaries and high-level stage order.
    43→- A letter suffix such as `05a` or `05d` denotes sibling scripts inside the same stage family.
    44→- A bare stage id such as `04` cannot coexist with `04a`, `04b`, or other same-family siblings.
    45→- Non-pipeline helpers must live outside the numbered stage surface, typically under `tools/`.
    46→- Stage-family siblings may run sequentially locally and may be parallelized in deployment when their direct dependencies are satisfied.
    47→- Local smoke tests may still run all scripts sequentially for simplicity and debuggability.
    48→
    49→### Directory names
    50→
    51→- Use `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic` without leading dots.
    52→- Avoid hidden directory naming for active pipeline outputs because the current project targets Windows development and public GitHub use.
    53→- Use lowercase, hyphenated filenames for human-authored specs in `docs/specs/v12/`.
    54→
    55→### Output philosophy
    56→
    57→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the stable output root for generated documentation.
    58→- `docs/` is for maintainers and project documentation only.
    59→- `tmp/` is scratch space and must never be treated as durable state.
    60→
    61→## Execution Contract
    62→
    63→1. `01` prepares local source inputs and manifests.
    64→2. `02a` is an independent wiki extractor and can run in parallel with `01` in hosted workflow execution.
    65→3. `02b` through `02h` extract source-specific content into L1 and remain gated on `01` because they consume cloned repositories.
    66→4. `03` normalizes L1 into L2 and promotes stable intermediates into the output tree.
    67→5. `04` optionally enriches staged L2 files with AI summary metadata and performs its own AI-store preflight.
    68→6. `05a` assembles the publishable skeletons and complete-reference outputs, sharding oversized modules into smaller part files while preserving the stable index filename.
    69→7. `05b`, `05c`, and `05d` generate companion publication artifacts from the stabilized post-`03` snapshot.
    70→8. `06` generates routing indexes after `05a` has produced the publishable reference assets.
    71→9. `07` generates the HTML landing page directly from the staged publish tree, grouping files by top-level product area instead of deriving its structure from `llms.txt`.
    72→10. `08` validates the entire output tree.
    73→
    74→Current Option B hardening adds per-extractor status manifests, disables matrix fail-fast for repo-backed extractors, and emits extract plus pipeline summary artifacts for faster triage.
    75→
    76→## Local-First Verification Model
    77→
    78→- The required first-stage verification path is local and sequential.
    79→- Deterministic fixture tests are the core protection against accidental regressions.
    80→- Local smoke tests should isolate `WORKDIR` and `OUTDIR` so the repository is not corrupted during development.
    81→- GitHub Actions remains a second-stage verification target after local proof, and it is now also the normal publication path for generated outputs.
    82→
    83→## Remote Promotion Contract
    84→
    85→- The `process` job builds generated artifacts into `staging/` (`OUTDIR`) and uploads that tree as `final-staging`.
    86→- The `deploy` job promotes `final-staging` into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` with `rsync -a --delete`.
    87→- Generated-output commits use the `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD` format and are written by the GitHub Actions bot only when the staged tree changed.
    88→- The `deploy` job then mirrors `openwrt-condensed-docs/` into the dedicated `gh-pages` branch and writes `.nojekyll` there so GitHub Pages serves the same packaged tree, including `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic`, without Jekyll rewriting.
    89→
    90→## Active Documents
    91→
    92→- `README.md`: short external overview.
    93→- `DEVELOPMENT.md`: maintainer quick start and local workflow reference.
    94→- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: durable architecture and naming contract.
    95→- `docs/specs/v12/`: active v12 specifications, current status, execution map, active bug log, and stabilization plan.
    96→
    97→## Archive Policy
    98→
    99→Documents under `docs/archive/v12/` remain useful as historical design context, but they do not control implementation. If an archived document conflicts with an active spec or with verified code behavior, the active spec wins.
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# Development Guide
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This file is the maintainer quick start for local development. The current engineering priority is to keep the Windows development path and the sequential smoke-test path reliable while preserving the now-verified GitHub Actions publication flow.
     6→
     7→## Prerequisites
     8→
     9→| Tool | Version | Purpose |
    10→| --- | --- | --- |
    11→| Python | 3.11+ | All pipeline scripts and local tests |
    12→| Node.js | 20+ | `jsdoc-to-markdown` and JavaScript syntax checks |
    13→| pandoc | 3.0+ | Wiki conversion |
    14→| git | 2.25+ | Repo cloning and versioned refactors |
    15→| jsdoc-to-markdown | current npm release | API doc extraction |
    16→
    17→## Quick Start on Windows
    18→
    19→```powershell
    20→pip install -r .github/scripts/requirements.txt
    21→npm install -g jsdoc-to-markdown
    22→winget install --id JohnMacFarlane.Pandoc
    23→```
    24→
    25→After dependencies are installed, use the local tests in `tests/` rather than relying on GitHub Actions behavior.
    26→
    27→## Recommended Local Commands
    28→
    29→```powershell
    30→python tests/run_pytest.py
    31→python tests/run_smoke.py
    32→python tests/run_smoke.py --run-ai
    33→python tests/run_smoke.py --include-extractors
    34→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py
    35→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py --run-ai --keep-temp
    36→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py
    37→python tests/check_linting.py
    38→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review
    39→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option full --keep-scratch
    40→```
    41→
    42→The `--run-ai` path is cache-backed for local verification, so it can validate the placement and mutation behavior of script `04` without requiring a live model token.
    43→
    44→For real AI-summary generation, use `python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review` or the detailed scratch-first workflow in [docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md](docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md). Do not treat `--run-ai` as proof of live API generation or permanent AI-store promotion.
    45→
    46→For one-off terminal invocations, either activate `.venv` once before testing or call the workspace interpreter directly via `C:/Users/MC/Documents/AirSentinel/openwrt-docs4ai-v12-copilot/.venv/Scripts/python.exe`. Do not assume the system `python` on `PATH` is the repo interpreter.
    47→
    48→## Validation Runners
    49→
    50→The maintained local validation surface is now Python-first and cross-platform.
    51→See `tests/README.md` for the full layout and direct entry points.
    52→
    53→- `tests/run_pytest.py` runs the focused suites in `tests/pytest/` and forwards any extra CLI arguments to pytest.
    54→- `tests/run_smoke.py` runs the maintained smoke lane in serial order. It supports `--run-ai`, `--keep-temp`, `--include-extractors`, the individual `--skip-*` switches, and `--result-root`.
    55→- `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` is the maintained one-command sequential validation path. It runs the focused pytest lane first, then the enabled smoke stages, and writes per-stage logs plus `summary.json` under `tmp/ci/local-validation/`.
    56→- `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py` runs one pytest lane and one smoke lane in parallel. Use it only for that supported split; do not treat it as permission to run multiple smoke scripts concurrently.
    57→- `tests/check_linting.py` performs a read-only Ruff, strict Pyright, and actionlint review and writes its bundle under `tmp/ci/lint-review/`.
    58→
    59→These runners are intentionally local-first. Remote GitHub Actions validation still depends on a pushed commit and the run-pinning procedure in `CI Operations`.
    60→
    61→## Terminal Testing Discipline
    62→
    63→When validating this project from the terminal, prefer deterministic, bounded commands over ad hoc shell control flow.
    64→
    65→1. Start in the repo root with the workspace virtual environment active, or use the explicit `.venv` interpreter path when running a single isolated command.
    66→2. Run the smallest proof first: `python tests/run_pytest.py`, then `python tests/run_smoke.py`, then remote workflow validation.
    67→3. When you want the full preferred local progression in one command, use `python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` instead of rebuilding the sequence ad hoc.
    68→4. Keep one purpose per command. Prefer a direct command invocation over inline retry loops or multi-purpose shell snippets.
    69→5. Capture durable local evidence when it matters, typically under `tests/test-results-*.txt` or `tmp/ci/`, so reruns can be compared without re-reading terminal scrollback.
    70→6. For GitHub Actions validation, pin the run to the pushed commit SHA, identify the matching `databaseId`, and wait on that exact run with `gh run watch <run_id> --exit-status --interval 15`.
    71→7. After the run finishes, inspect `pipeline-summary`, `process-summary`, and `extract-summary` artifacts before opening raw failed-job logs.
    72→8. Avoid open-ended polling loops, mid-run log scraping, and assumptions about "the latest run". They create noisy output and make it easy to validate the wrong workflow run.
    73→
    74→## Pre-Change Checklist
    75→
    76→Before editing pipeline scripts, generated outputs, or workflow behavior:
    77→
    78→1. Decide whether the change affects maintainer docs, generated-corpus contracts, workflow execution, or only local tests.
    79→2. Read this file, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/specs/v12/schema-definitions.md`, and `docs/specs/v12/execution-map.md` before changing numbered scripts or the workflow.
    80→3. If the change touches scripts `05b` through `08`, inspect the currently generated AI-facing outputs first: `openwrt-condensed-docs/llms.txt`, `openwrt-condensed-docs/llms-full.txt`, `openwrt-condensed-docs/AGENTS.md`, and at least one representative `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/llms.txt`.
    81→4. If the change touches `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`, map the intended behavior to a specific trigger path: push on `main`, monthly schedule, or `workflow_dispatch` with explicit inputs.
    82→5. Run the smallest local proof first. Only use remote GitHub Actions runs after local validation passes.
    83→
    84→## Dual-Role LLM Surfaces
    85→
    86→This repository has two LLM-relevant surfaces and they should not be conflated:
    87→
    88→- The source repository is the implementation and maintainer-doc surface. Its authoritative docs live under `docs/`, `README.md`, and `DEVELOPMENT.md`.
    89→- The generated corpus under `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the published AI navigation surface consumed by downstream tools and models.
    90→
    91→The strict routing contract for generated `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, module `llms.txt`, and `AGENTS.md` lives in `docs/specs/v12/schema-definitions.md`.
    92→
    93→A source-repo root `llms.txt` remains intentionally out of scope for the current maintenance tranche. Do not create one opportunistically while working on generated output behavior.
    94→
    95→## Repository Rules
    96→
    97→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the stable generated output root.
    98→- `tmp/` is ephemeral and never authoritative.
    99→- `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic` are the standard intermediate layer names.
   100→- Script numbering denotes stage families and dependency boundaries. Letter suffixes denote sibling scripts inside the same stage family.
   101→- A bare stage id such as `04` cannot coexist with `04a`, `04b`, or other lettered siblings. A stage is either singular or lettered, never both.
   102→- Non-pipeline support tools belong outside the numbered stage surface, under `tools/`.
   103→- Local smoke runs may still execute the lettered scripts sequentially.
   104→- Local-only references that are out of scope for this repo (for example third-party bug notes) should stay under `tmp/legacy-backup/` so they remain durable locally and gitignored.
   105→
   106→## Script Families
   107→
   108→| Script | Role |
   109→| --- | --- |
   110→| `openwrt-docs4ai-01-clone-repos.py` | Prepare L0 source inputs and manifests |
   111→| `openwrt-docs4ai-02a` through `02h` | Source-specific extraction into L1 |
   112→| `openwrt-docs4ai-03-normalize-semantic.py` | L1 to L2 normalization and promotion |
   113→| `openwrt-docs4ai-04-generate-ai-summaries.py` | Optional AI summary enrichment |
   114→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05a-assemble-references.py` | Assemble publishable L3 skeletons and L4 monoliths |
   115→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py` | Generate AGENTS.md and the root generated README |
   116→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py` | Generate ucode IDE schema output |
   117→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py` | Generate API drift telemetry against the baseline inventory |
   118→| `openwrt-docs4ai-06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py` | Generate llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and module routing indexes |
   119→| `openwrt-docs4ai-07-generate-web-index.py` | HTML landing page generation |
   120→| `openwrt-docs4ai-08-validate-output.py` | Whole-output validation gate |
   121→
   122→## Local Tests
   123→
   124→- `tests/pytest/` contains the focused pytest suites for import-safe helpers, workflow contracts, fixture routing, corpus sanity, and wiki scraper behavior.
   125→- `tests/smoke/` contains the direct smoke runners; use `python tests/run_smoke.py` for the maintained serial sequence.
   126→- `tests/check_linting.py` produces read-only local review bundles for Ruff, strict Pyright, and actionlint.
   127→
   128→These entry points are maintained as first-class engineering assets. Use `tests/README.md` when you need the exact folder contract or runner/output mapping.
   129→
   130→## AI Summary Operations
   131→
   132→Real AI-summary work is intentionally AI-store first and scratch first.
   133→
   134→- `data/base/` and `data/override/` are the authoritative AI-summary surfaces.
   135→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is downstream generated evidence.
   136→- The permanent workflow lives in [docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md](docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md).
   137→- The only numbered AI stage is `.github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-04-generate-ai-summaries.py`.
   138→- Script `04` performs its own library-backed preflight against the selected AI store before applying or generating summaries.
   139→- The maintained one-command helper is `tools/manage_ai_store.py`.
   140→- `--option review` prepares scratch data, runs script `04`, then runs the same library-backed validation and audit checks against the scratch store.
   141→- `--option promote` copies reviewed scratch JSON into `data/base/` and immediately reruns validation and audit against the permanent store.
   142→- `--option full` runs the full review-plus-promotion path and can remove scratch data automatically unless `--keep-scratch` is set.
   143→
   144→Use the cache-backed smoke paths for regression proof, and use the runbook plus `tools/manage_ai_store.py` for real generation, review, and promotion.
   145→
   146→## Remote Publish Policy
   147→
   148→- The workflow builds generated artifacts into `staging/` first and only promotes them in the `deploy` job.
   149→- Hosted extraction now runs `02a` in parallel with `01`, while `02b` through `02h` remain clone-gated.
   150→- On push, schedule, and manual runs, the deploy job syncs `staging/` into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` with `rsync -a --delete`.
   151→- If the promoted tree changed, GitHub Actions writes a bot-authored commit in the form `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD`.
   152→- GitHub Pages publishes a `public/` copy of staging that excludes `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic`.
   153→- Workflow diagnostics now include `extract-summary`, `process-summary`, and `pipeline-summary` artifacts for first-stop triage.
   154→- Avoid hand-editing generated outputs if the next workflow run is expected to republish them.
   155→
   156→## Workflow Triggers And Manual Inputs
   157→
   158→- `push` runs only on `main` and follows the normal publish path.
   159→- `schedule` runs on the first day of each month at `13:00 UTC`.
   160→- `workflow_dispatch` exists for targeted verification and controlled reruns.
   161→- Manual dispatch supports four inputs: `skip_wiki`, `skip_buildroot`, `skip_ai`, and `max_ai_files`.
   162→- Manual dispatch can target a specific ref. Use `gh workflow run "openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml" --ref <branch>` when you need remote proof for a non-`main` branch.
   163→- Treat `workflow_dispatch` as the preferred remote test path for pipeline changes because it makes the intended skip knobs explicit in run history.
   164→
   165→## Dependency Policy
   166→
   167→- `.github/scripts/requirements.txt` is intentionally kept as a small direct dependency list rather than a blanket fully pinned lockfile.
   168→- Add new direct dependencies only when they materially simplify the pipeline or improve output reliability.
   169→- Do not add exact pins by default. Pin or lock only after verifying a concrete reproducibility or breakage problem.
   170→- When a dependency-related failure appears, record the exact failing version in the investigation notes and then decide whether targeted pinning is justified.
   171→- Keep local and CI bootstrap lightweight enough that a low-touch maintainer can rebuild the environment without a separate dependency-management project.
   172→
   173→## Optional Workflow Linting
   174→
   175→If you edit `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`, run `actionlint` locally when it is available on your machine. This is intentionally optional and is not a mandatory bootstrap dependency for the repo.
   176→
   177→## CI Operations
   178→
   179→### Waiting for a GitHub Actions run and reading its logs
   180→
   181→This pipeline runs CI frequently. Follow the procedure below every time you push a commit and need to verify the result. Do not improvise — the anti-pattern list below is based on real mistakes that have caused wasted debug cycles in this project.
   182→
   183→**Best method: pin-then-triage**
   184→
   185→**Phase A — wait for the right run to complete**
   186→
   187→1. Capture the exact commit SHA you pushed:
   188→
   189→   ```powershell
   190→   git rev-parse HEAD
   191→   ```
   192→
   193→2. List recent workflow runs and find the one that matches that SHA (never assume "latest" is yours — a deploy auto-update commit may have started a newer run):
   194→
   195→   ```powershell
   196→   gh run list --workflow "openwrt-docs4ai pipeline (v12)" --limit 20 --json databaseId,headSha,status,conclusion,url
   197→   ```
   198→
   199→3. Copy the `databaseId` of the matching entry and wait on that specific run with a bounded poll interval:
   200→
   201→   ```powershell
   202→   gh run watch <run_id> --exit-status --interval 15
   203→   ```
   204→
   205→   `--exit-status` returns exit code 1 on failure so the caller detects it without manual logic. `--interval 15` polls every 15 seconds. Block here until it exits — do not proceed to Phase B while the run is still in progress.
   206→
   207→**Phase B — triage from summary artifacts before opening raw logs**
   208→
   209→4. Download and inspect the structured pipeline summary artifact first:
   210→
   211→   ```powershell
   212→   gh run download <run_id> -n pipeline-summary -D tmp/ci/pipeline-summary
   213→   Get-Content tmp/ci/pipeline-summary/*.json | ConvertFrom-Json
   214→   ```
   215→
   216→5. If that implicates a specific extractor, also pull the per-extractor status bundle:
   217→
   218→   ```powershell
   219→   gh run download <run_id> -n extract-summary -D tmp/ci/extract-summary
   220→   ```
   221→
   222→6. Only if the summary artifacts indicate a failure you still cannot explain structurally, open raw failed-job logs:
   223→
   224→   ```powershell
   225→   gh run view <run_id> --log-failed
   226→   ```
   227→
   228→Quick whole-run status check:
   229→
   230→```powershell
   231→gh run view <run_id> --json jobs,conclusion,url
   232→```
   233→
   234→### Things to avoid when waiting for CI
   235→
   236→- **Do not poll "the latest run"** — always pin to the SHA of your push. After a successful deploy the workflow writes a bot-authored `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD` commit which triggers a new run; the "latest" ID is no longer yours.
   237→- **Do not run an open-ended polling loop** — an unbounded `while ($true)` loop in a chat session consumes tokens, hides intermediate output, and looks like an infinite hang to both the user and the model. Always use `gh run watch` with `--interval` instead.
   238→- **Do not pull full logs before the run finishes** — logs are noisy and incomplete mid-run; wait for `gh run watch` to exit cleanly before any log inspection.
   239→- **Do not skip the artifact triage phase** — `pipeline-summary`, `process-summary`, and `extract-summary` exist precisely to avoid log forensics; read them first and fall back to raw logs only for unexplained failures.
   240→- **Do not assume a deploy auto-commit is your commit** — verify by checking `headSha` on the run, not by run order.
   241→
   242→## Environment Variables
   243→
   244→Defaults below are the direct local defaults from `lib/config.py` and the local
   245→AI tooling. The hosted workflow overrides `WORKDIR` and `OUTDIR`, and manual
   246→dispatch exposes `skip_ai=false` by default.
   247→
   248→| Variable | Default | Purpose |
   249→| --- | --- | --- |
   250→| `WORKDIR` | `tmp` | Scratch area for cloned repos and intermediate layers |
   251→| `OUTDIR` | `openwrt-condensed-docs` | Stable output root for generated artifacts |
   252→| `SKIP_WIKI` | `false` | Skip wiki extraction |
   253→| `SKIP_AI` | `true` | Disable optional AI enrichment by default |
   254→| `WRITE_AI` | `true` | Allow script `04` to create missing base records when AI is enabled |
   255→| `WIKI_MAX_PAGES` | `300` | Limit wiki traversal depth |
   256→| `MAX_AI_FILES` | `40` | Limit local or remote AI summary volume |
   257→| `VALIDATE_MODE` | `hard` | Validation severity mode |
   258→| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | empty | Remote-only integrations and telemetry fallback retrieval |
   259→| `LOCAL_DEV_TOKEN` | empty | Local override token for optional AI enrichment |
   260→| `AI_DATA_BASE_DIR` | `data/base` | Base AI summary store root |
   261→| `AI_DATA_OVERRIDE_DIR` | `data/override` | Override AI summary store root |
   262→
   263→## Documentation And Reporting Conventions
   264→
   265→- Use relative Markdown links for navigational cross-references between maintainer documents and generated outputs.
   266→- When a file or path reference is meant to be opened by the reader, prefer a Markdown link instead of an inline code span.
   267→- Keep inline code for commands, environment variables, symbol names, filenames used as literals, and short syntax fragments.
   268→- Do not inject or rewrite cross-links inside code fences or inline code spans.
   269→
   270→## Adding or Changing a Scraper
   271→
   272→If a new extractor is added or a current extractor is materially changed:
   273→
   274→1. Keep it in `.github/scripts/` with the numbered naming convention.
   275→2. Make sure it writes only to `WORKDIR/L1-raw/{module}/`.
   276→3. Write `.meta.json` sidecars through shared helper logic instead of ad hoc metadata code.
   277→4. Update the deterministic tests and the sequential smoke runner.
   278→5. Update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and active v12 specs if the contract changed.
   279→
   280→## Logging Convention
   281→
   282→Scripts should emit concise line-buffered messages using the numbered prefix convention, for example:
   283→
   284→```text
   285→[02a] OK: scraped 15 pages
   286→[05d] INFO: Baseline lacks module metadata; suppressing module drift sections
   287→[04] SKIP: AI enrichment disabled
   288→[08] FAIL: missing llms.txt
   289→```
   290→
   291→## Deferred Bugs
   292→
   293→- `luci-app-dockerman` still produces one truthful non-blocking standalone `ucode` validation warning in `docker_rpc.uc`. Keep it soft until a higher-fidelity LuCI runtime validation context exists.
   294→- Mermaid template promotion is still deferred. The repository has Mermaid source templates, but the exact publication targets and insertion rules are not constrained tightly enough to inject diagrams automatically without risking incorrect output.
   295→
   296→## Deferred Features
   297→
   298→- Extreme A3 renaming remains deferred. If revisited, prefer fully descriptive stage-prefixed or category-first filenames only after the current moderate stage-family contract is stable across the workflow, tests, and docs.
   299→- `signature-inventory.json` still does not emit explicit module metadata. The current `05d` fix suppresses false module drift against legacy baselines; richer schema expansion remains a later compatibility decision.
   300→- Curated example extraction still warns on partial read failures instead of failing the run. Hard-failing partial omissions is deferred until the curated app and file inventory is formalized.
   301→- Broader validation coverage beyond the current targeted fixes is deferred until the renamed stage contract and baseline expectations are stable.
   302→
   303→## Windows Notes
   304→
   305→- Windows is a required development environment for this project.
   306→- Path logic must remain cross-platform, but local development is the first validation target.
   307→- A small number of Linux-only checks may exist later for remote workflow validation, but they must be documented explicitly.
   308→
   309→## Current Focus
   310→
   311→The pipeline reached a stable, fully-tested state on 2026-03-11. All stabilization phases are complete.
   312→
   313→Ongoing monitored items:
   314→
   315→1. The `luci-app-dockerman` ucode warning (`REMOTE-008`) remains a deferred soft warning — intentionally preserved as a truthful signal, not suppressed.
   316→2. Mermaid diagram template promotion remains deferred until a concrete consumer exists.
   317→
   318→The pipeline is otherwise healthy: local tests pass, CI runs succeed with 0 hard failures, and published outputs are generated and committed correctly on every run. When returning to active development, run `python tests/run_pytest.py` locally first, then use `python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` before following the CI Operations procedure above.
   319→

     1→# openwrt-docs4ai v12 Schema Definitions
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This document defines the active filesystem and data contracts for the v12 pipeline.
     6→
     7→## Top-Level Paths
     8→
     9→- `WORKDIR` defaults to `tmp`
    10→- `OUTDIR` defaults to `openwrt-condensed-docs`
    11→- `docs/` is for maintainer documentation only
    12→
    13→## Dual-Role Documentation Boundary
    14→
    15→The repository has two distinct documentation surfaces:
    16→
    17→- The source tree is the maintainer and implementation surface. Its authoritative material lives in `README.md`, `DEVELOPMENT.md`, and `docs/specs/v12/`.
    18→- The generated corpus under `OUTDIR` is the published LLM and IDE navigation surface.
    19→
    20→This document defines the generated-corpus contract only. A source-repo root `llms.txt` is intentionally deferred and is not part of the active v12 contract.
    21→
    22→The active publication policy keeps L1 and L2 under `openwrt-condensed-docs` for inspection, debugging, and AI-context use. L0 remains unpublished because raw clones and raw fetched inputs are materially larger and are not needed as durable outputs.
    23→
    24→## Layer Contracts
    25→
    26→### L0
    27→
    28→- Location: `tmp/repo-*`
    29→- Contents: untouched upstream clones or raw fetched inputs
    30→- Publication: none; treat as transient local or CI-only build state
    31→
    32→### L1
    33→
    34→- Location during build: `tmp/L1-raw/{module}/`
    35→- Retained output location: `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/{module}/`
    36→- File naming: `{origin_type}-{slug}.md`
    37→- Sidecar naming: `{origin_type}-{slug}.meta.json`
    38→- Rules:
    39→  - markdown only
    40→  - no YAML frontmatter
    41→  - no injected cross-links
    42→  - code blocks must carry explicit language identifiers
    43→
    44→#### L1 sidecar fields
    45→
    46→Required or expected fields:
    47→
    48→- `extractor`
    49→- `origin_type`
    50→- `module`
    51→- `slug`
    52→- `content_hash`
    53→
    54→Recommended fields when known:
    55→
    56→- `upstream_path`
    57→- `original_url`
    58→- `language`
    59→- `fetch_status`
    60→- `extraction_timestamp`
    61→
    62→### L2
    63→
    64→- Location during build: `tmp/L2-semantic/{module}/`
    65→- Retained output location: `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/{module}/`
    66→- Format: markdown with YAML frontmatter
    67→- Rules:
    68→  - YAML must be parsed and written safely
    69→  - cross-links must not be injected inside code fences or inline code
    70→  - cross-links must remain relative Markdown links that resolve within the published output tree
    71→  - file or path references intended as navigation aids should be emitted as Markdown links rather than inline code spans
    72→  - inline code remains appropriate for commands, environment variables, symbol names, and syntax literals
    73→  - token counts describe body content, not frontmatter
    74→
    75→#### Required L2 frontmatter fields
    76→
    77→- `title`
    78→- `module`
    79→- `origin_type`
    80→- `token_count`
    81→- `version`
    82→
    83→#### Recommended L2 frontmatter fields
    84→
    85→- `source_file`
    86→- `upstream_path`
    87→- `language`
    88→- `description`
    89→- `last_pipeline_run`
    90→
    91→#### Optional AI fields
    92→
    93→- `ai_summary`
    94→- `ai_when_to_use`
    95→- `ai_related_topics`
    96→
    97→### L3
    98→
    99→- Location: `openwrt-condensed-docs/` root and module subdirectories
   100→- Primary outputs:
   101→  - `llms.txt`
   102→  - `llms-full.txt`
   103→  - `AGENTS.md`
   104→  - `README.md`
   105→  - `index.html`
   106→  - `*-skeleton.md`
   107→  - `*.d.ts`
   108→
   109→### L3 LLM Routing Contract
   110→
   111→The generated corpus is both a published documentation product and a navigation surface for tools and models. Its LLM-facing outputs must therefore be deterministic, link-safe, and self-describing.
   112→
   113→#### Root `llms.txt`
   114→
   115→Required characteristics:
   116→
   117→- single H1 title line for the routing index
   118→- introductory blockquote lines that point to `llms-full.txt`, state the version banner, and state the total underlying L2 token count
   119→- one or more H2 category sections
   120→- category bullet lines that point only to module indexes
   121→- bullet format:
   122→
   123→```text
   124→- [<module>](./<module>/llms.txt): <short description> (~<module_l2_tokens> tokens)
   125→```
   126→
   127→Rules:
   128→
   129→- root `llms.txt` is a routing tree, not the exhaustive catalog
   130→- the displayed token count represents underlying L2 content for the module rather than duplicated L3 or L4 helper files
   131→- descriptions should prefer `ai_summary`, then `description`, then a first-sentence body fallback
   132→- placeholder descriptions such as `No description` are not valid output
   133→
   134→#### Root `llms-full.txt`
   135→
   136→Required characteristics:
   137→
   138→- single H1 title line for the flat catalog
   139→- optional introductory blockquote lines are allowed
   140→- flat bullet list sorted by relative path
   141→- catalog entries must include all generated AI-facing helper files and all L2 markdown documents that belong to the published corpus
   142→- required catalog coverage:
   143→  - `AGENTS.md`
   144→  - generated `README.md`
   145→  - each module `llms.txt`
   146→  - each `*-skeleton.md`
   147→  - each `*-complete-reference.md`
   148→  - each `*-complete-reference.part-*.md` when sharding is present
   149→  - each published `.d.ts`
   150→  - every `L2-semantic/{module}/*.md`
   151→- bullet format:
   152→
   153→```text
   154→- [<relative-path>](./<relative-path>): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, <kind>)
   155→```
   156→
   157→Typical `kind` values include `l2-source`, `l3-agent-guide`, `l3-generated-readme`, `l3-module-index`, `l3-skeleton`, `l3-ide-schema`, `l4-monolith`, and `l4-monolith-part`.
   158→
   159→#### Module `llms.txt`
   160→
   161→Required characteristics:
   162→
   163→- H1 title in the form `# <module> module`
   164→- introductory blockquote lines for module description and total underlying L2 token count
   165→- deterministic H2 section order
   166→- allowed sections:
   167→  - `Recommended Entry Points`
   168→  - `Tooling Surfaces`
   169→  - `Source Documents`
   170→- empty sections must be omitted
   171→
   172→Preferred section semantics:
   173→
   174→- `Recommended Entry Points` lists the module skeleton, the stable complete-reference index, and any sharded part files when present
   175→- `Tooling Surfaces` lists generated `.d.ts` or other IDE-oriented helper files when present
   176→- `Source Documents` lists each L2 document for the module
   177→
   178→Bullet formats:
   179→
   180→```text
   181→- [<module>-skeleton.md](./<module>-skeleton.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-skeleton)
   182→- [<module>-complete-reference.md](./<module>-complete-reference.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith)
   183→- [<module>-complete-reference.part-01.md](./<module>-complete-reference.part-01.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith-part)
   184→- [<module>.d.ts](./<module>.d.ts): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-ide-schema)
   185→- [<filename>.md](../L2-semantic/<module>/<filename>.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l2-source)
   186→```
   187→
   188→#### `AGENTS.md`
   189→
   190→`AGENTS.md` must remain consistent with the routing contract above.
   191→
   192→Required guidance points:
   193→
   194→- begin at root `llms.txt`
   195→- prefer module `llms.txt` once the target subsystem is known
   196→- prefer `*-skeleton.md` before complete-reference indexes or part files when context is tight
   197→- treat generated module indexes, skeletons, complete-reference indexes, part files, and `.d.ts` files as published navigation surfaces
   198→- avoid implying that a separate source-repo root `llms.txt` already exists
   199→
   200→### L4
   201→
   202→- Location: `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/`
   203→- Format: one stable complete-reference index per module plus optional sharded part files for oversized modules
   204→- Rule: one top-level YAML block per generated L4 file, not repeated L2 frontmatter sections
   205→
   206→### L5
   207→
   208→- Location: `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
   209→- Outputs:
   210→  - `CHANGES.md`
   211→  - `changelog.json`
   212→  - `signature-inventory.json`
   213→
   214→## Enumerated Origin Types
   215→
   216→- `c_source`
   217→- `js_source`
   218→- `wiki_page`
   219→- `makefile_meta`
   220→- `readme`
   221→- `uci_schema`
   222→- `hotplug_event`
   223→- `example_app`
   224→- `header_api`
   225→
   226→## Stakeholder Mapping
   227→
   228→| Stakeholder | Primary layers |
   229→| --- | --- |
   230→| Raw analyst | L1, L2 |

   234→| Drift and telemetry tooling | L5 |

```text
     1→# openwrt-docs4ai v12 Execution Map
     2→
     3→## Layer Locations
     4→
     5→- `L0`: `tmp/repo-*`
     6→- `L1`: `tmp/L1-raw/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/{module}/`
     7→- `L2`: `tmp/L2-semantic/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/{module}/`
     8→- `L3`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/` root and per-module subdirectories
     9→- `L4`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/`
    10→- `L5`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
    11→
    12→## Ordered Script Flow
    13→
    14→1. `01-clone-repos.py`
    15→2. `02a-scrape-wiki.py` (independent branch in hosted workflow)
    16→3. `02b` through `02h` (repo-backed extractors, gated on `01`)
    17→4. `03-normalize-semantic.py`
    18→5. `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` when AI enrichment is enabled; it performs its own AI-store preflight before enrichment
    19→6. `05a-assemble-references.py`
    20→7. `05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py`
    21→8. `05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py`
    22→9. `05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py`
    23→10. `06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py`
    24→11. `07-generate-web-index.py`
    25→12. `08-validate-output.py`
    26→
    27→Letter suffixes denote scripts that are parallelizable in deployment. Local smoke tests may still execute them sequentially.
    28→
    29→`tools/manage_ai_store.py` is the maintained local scratch-first helper.
    30→
    31→Hosted Option B topology now runs `02a` in a separate job without waiting on `initialize`, while repo-backed `02b` through `02h` remain in a matrix job that depends on `initialize`.
    32→
    33→## Execution Contract Matrix
    34→
    35→This matrix defines placement rationale, mutability boundaries, dependencies, and failure power.
    36→
    37→| Step | Mutability & atomicity | Primary dependencies | Failure mode in current workflow | Deploy impact |
    38→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    39→| `01` | Writes L0 clones and `repo-manifest.json` under `WORKDIR`; isolated to initialize job | Git checkout, network access | Hard fail (`initialize` fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    40→| `02a` | Writes only wiki L1 outputs and wiki cache under `WORKDIR` | Internet/wiki availability, `WORKDIR` | Hard fail (`extract_wiki` job fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    41→| `02b`-`02h` | Write module L1 outputs under `WORKDIR`; no publish writes | L0 clone artifacts from `01`, toolchain deps | Hard fail (repo-backed matrix leg fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    42→| `03` | Converts L1 to L2 and promotes staged intermediates inside `OUTDIR`; atomic within process job sandbox | L1 artifacts, metadata sidecars | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    43→| `04` | Optional in-place L2 enrichment in staged `OUTDIR`; no direct publish writes | `SKIP_AI`, cache availability, optional token | If `SKIP_AI=true`, skipped cleanly. If enabled and it fails, hard fail in `process` | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    44→| `05a`-`05d` | Deterministic generation of publish companions inside `OUTDIR` | Staged L2 and registry outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    45→| `06` | Builds routing/index artifacts inside `OUTDIR` | L2 metadata and reference outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    46→| `07` | Generates `index.html` inside `OUTDIR` | Prior index inputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    47→| `08` | Validation gate over full staged `OUTDIR`; no external writes | Complete staged corpus | Hard fail on hard checks | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    48→| `deploy` | `rsync -a --delete` from staging into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` and optional bot commit | Successful `process` job and `final-staging` artifact | Hard fail in deploy only | Only step that can change published output |
    49→
    50→## Return-State Guarantees
    51→
    52→1. If any `process`-phase script fails, `final-staging` is not promoted and deploy does not run.
    53→2. Because promotion is isolated to the deploy job, partially generated outputs from `03` through `08` stay sandboxed in runner staging.
    54→3. This means a failed `08` cannot publish a mismatched `07` web index to the committed output tree.
    55→4. `SKIP_AI=true` is the explicit optional path for AI enrichment; AI failure is not ignored once the AI step is enabled.
    56→
    57→## Key Handoffs
    58→
    59→- `01` produces cloned repositories and any repo manifest data required by downstream stages.
    60→- `02*` scripts write L1 markdown plus `.meta.json` sidecars.
    61→- `02b` now exposes import-safe helper surfaces for fenced-block normalization and example fixups so targeted tests can import it directly.
    62→- `03` reads only L1 plus metadata and writes L2 plus a cross-link registry before promoting stable intermediates into `OUTDIR`.
    63→- `03` is also the correct place for bounded module-specific cleanup that should not mutate L1 raw retention; the current live example is wiki-only cleanup for residual DokuWiki and pandoc artifacts.
    64→- `04` mutates staged L2 files in place when enabled.
    65→- `05` consumes staged L2 files and emits skeletons plus monolithic references.
    66→- `06*` scripts consume staged L2 metadata and generated references to emit maps, agent instructions, IDE schemas, and telemetry.
    67→- `07` consumes the map outputs and writes the HTML landing page.
    68→- `08` validates the full `OUTDIR` tree and now exposes import-safe parsing helpers for direct unit coverage.
    69→
    70→## Observability Baseline
    71→
    72→Current hosted logs expose started/completed timestamps per job and per step via `gh run view --json jobs`.
    73→
    74→Example timing snapshot from successful run `22901854476`:
    75→
    76→- `initialize`: ~31s total, with `Clone repositories` ~9s
    77→- `extract_wiki (02a-scrape-wiki.py)`: ~211s
    78→- `process`: ~56s
    79→- `deploy`: ~18s
    80→
    81→Current workflow observability artifacts now include:
    82→
    83→- extractor-level status manifests (`extract-status-*`)
    84→- an always-generated `extract-summary` artifact
    85→- a `process-summary` staging contract artifact
    86→- an always-generated `pipeline-summary` artifact
    87→
    88→These artifacts are intended to be the first-stop troubleshooting surface before deep log forensics.
    89→
    90→## Scope Note
    91→
    92→The current AI-alignment slice keeps `04` as the single numbered AI stage and
    93→moves local review, audit, validation, and promotion into `tools/manage_ai_store.py`.
    94→Deeper model-selection and quality-scoring work remains deferred.
    95→
    96→## High-Value Debugging Paths
    97→
    98→- If the issue is stale or malformed wiki prose in committed outputs, inspect `02a` for source/cache behavior and `03` for semantic cleanup.
    99→- If the issue is misclassified ucode examples or false syntax warnings, inspect `02b` and `08` together because the extractor and validator now share regression coverage boundaries.
   100→- If the issue is missing, stale, or structurally invalid AI summaries, inspect `04`, `tools/manage_ai_store.py`, and the `data/base/` plus `data/override/` store roots together.
   101→- If the issue is missing or overwritten generated outputs, inspect the workflow `process` to `deploy` handoff because `staging/` is now the authoritative promotion source.
   102→- If the issue is only visible in committed `openwrt-condensed-docs/`, compare it against the focused corpus sanity snapshot from `tests/pytest/pytest_03_wiki_corpus_sanity_test.py` to decide whether the repo tree is stale or the normalization logic is still wrong.
   103→
   104→## Required Environment Variables
   105→
   106→- `WORKDIR`
   107→- `OUTDIR`
   108→- `SKIP_WIKI`
   109→- `SKIP_AI`
   110→- `WIKI_MAX_PAGES`
   111→- `MAX_AI_FILES`
   112→- `VALIDATE_MODE`
   113→
   114→## Required Schema Facts
   115→
   116→- L1 files are pure markdown and must not begin with YAML frontmatter.
   117→- L1 sidecars are `.meta.json` files stored beside the markdown file.
   118→- L2 files require YAML frontmatter with at least `title`, `module`, `origin_type`, `token_count`, and `version`.
   119→- L4 files use one top-level YAML block for the full monolith rather than reusing L2 frontmatter inline.
   120→
   121→## Verified Execution Priority
   122→
   123→The first required execution proof remains local and sequential. GitHub Actions is now a verified second-stage execution and publication layer, so pipeline debugging should distinguish between local logic regressions and remote promotion or cache-state effects.
   124→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→# Tests
     2→
     3→This directory holds the maintained local validation surface for the repository.
     4→Only `tests/pytest/` and `tests/smoke/` contain runnable test code. Everything
     5→else under `tests/` is support material, committed sample data, durable
     6→artifacts, or planning notes.
     7→
     8→## Layout
     9→
    10→| Path | Purpose |
    11→| --- | --- |
    12→| `tests/pytest/` | Focused pytest suites for helper logic, workflow contracts, fixture routing, corpus sanity, and wiki scraper behavior |
    13→| `tests/smoke/` | Maintained smoke runners that exercise the local pipeline without pytest discovery |
    14→| `tests/support/` | Shared helpers used by pytest modules, smoke scripts, and root-level runner scripts |
    15→| `tests/sample-inputs/` | Small committed repro inputs used by the fixture-backed smoke path |
    16→| `tests/artifacts/` | Intentionally committed test artifacts that are useful to inspect in Git history |
    17→| `tests/proposals/` | Historical and draft test planning material |
    18→| `tests/run_pytest.py` | Maintained focused pytest entry point |
    19→| `tests/run_smoke.py` | Maintained serial smoke entry point |
    20→| `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` | Maintained sequential validation runner |
    21→| `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py` | Supported two-lane runner: one pytest lane plus one smoke lane |
    22→| `tests/check_linting.py` | Read-only Ruff, Pyright, and actionlint review runner |
    23→
    24→## Recommended Commands
    25→
    26→```powershell
    27→python tests/run_pytest.py
    28→python tests/run_smoke.py
    29→python tests/run_smoke.py --run-ai --include-extractors
    30→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py
    31→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py --run-ai --keep-temp
    32→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py
    33→python tests/check_linting.py
    34→```
    35→
    36→All root-level Python runners accept `--result-root` if you want to override the
    37→default output directory. `tests/run_pytest.py` forwards any extra arguments to
    38→pytest, so commands such as `python tests/run_pytest.py -k wiki -q` are valid.
    39→
    40→## Direct Entry Points
    41→
    42→Use the root-level runners for normal maintenance. Run the underlying test files
    43→directly when you need a narrower proof.
    44→
    45→```powershell
    46→python -m pytest tests/pytest/pytest_04_wiki_scraper_test.py -q
    47→python -m pytest tests/pytest/pytest_03_wiki_corpus_sanity_test.py -s -q
    48→python tests/smoke/smoke_00_post_extract_pipeline.py --keep-temp
    49→python tests/smoke/smoke_01_full_local_pipeline.py --include-extractors
    50→python tests/smoke/smoke_02_ai_store_contract.py
    51→```
    52→
    53→The smoke scripts are intentionally named `smoke_...` so pytest will not collect
    54→them by mistake.
    55→
    56→## Result Bundles
    57→
    58→The maintained runners write durable bundles under `tmp/ci/`:
    59→
    60→| Runner | Default bundle root |
    61→| --- | --- |
    62→| `tests/run_pytest.py` | `tmp/ci/pytest/<timestamp>/` |
    63→| `tests/run_smoke.py` | `tmp/ci/smoke/<timestamp>/` |
    64→| `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` | `tmp/ci/local-validation/<timestamp>/` |
    65→| `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py` | `tmp/ci/local-validation-parallel/<timestamp>/` |
    66→| `tests/check_linting.py` | `tmp/ci/lint-review/<timestamp>/` |
    67→
    68→Each bundle contains per-stage text logs and a `summary.json` file.
    69→
    70→## Parallel Rules
    71→
    72→- Supported: one pytest lane and one smoke lane running together through `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py`.
    73→- Unsupported: running multiple smoke scripts against the same worktree at once.
    74→- Unsupported: launching duplicate full-local smoke runners in parallel.
    75→- Unsupported: treating the smoke stages as order-independent. They are still maintained as a serial lane.
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→# openwrt-docs4ai
     2→
     3→OpenWrt documentation collection and condensation pipeline for humans, tooling, and LLM workflows.
     4→
     5→## What This Repository Does
     6→
     7→This repository pulls documentation from multiple OpenWrt-related sources, normalizes it into stable intermediate layers, and generates compact outputs intended for:
     8→
     9→- targeted human lookup
    10→- LLM context ingestion
    11→- IDE and tooling support
    12→- long-term monthly refresh automation
    13→
    14→The current development stage is verified stabilization and operational hardening. Local-first validation remains the engineering baseline, and GitHub Actions is now a confirmed remote execution and publication path rather than an unproven future target.
    15→
    16→## Source Families
    17→
    18→- OpenWrt wiki pages
    19→- `jow-/ucode`
    20→- `openwrt/luci`
    21→- `openwrt/openwrt`
    22→- OpenWrt-adjacent API and example sources extracted from those repositories
    23→
    24→## Output Model
    25→
    26→Generated artifacts live under `openwrt-condensed-docs/`.
    27→
    28→- `L1-raw/` contains normalized raw markdown plus sidecar metadata.
    29→- `L2-semantic/` contains semantic markdown with YAML frontmatter and cross-links.
    30→- Root and per-module outputs contain maps, monoliths, skeletons, HTML landing pages, and telemetry.
    31→- GitHub Pages mirrors that same generated product tree from `gh-pages` rather than publishing a filtered subset.
    32→
    33→The exact output set is defined in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and `docs/specs/v12/`.
    34→
    35→## Repository Guidance
    36→
    37→- Read `DEVELOPMENT.md` for local setup and testing.
    38→- Read `tests/README.md` for the test-tree layout, supported entry points, and result bundle locations.
    39→- Use `python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` for the scripted maintainer validation path. It runs the focused pytest lane plus the maintained smoke stages in order and stores durable logs under `tmp/ci/local-validation/`.
    40→- Use `python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review` for the maintained scratch-first AI-store workflow.
    41→- Read `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` for repository structure and naming rules.
    42→- Read `docs/specs/v12/` for the active v12 technical specifications.
    43→- Treat `docs/archive/v12/` as historical context only.
    44→
    45→## Status
    46→
    47→The repository now has both local and remote v12 verification from the 2026-03-09 stabilization pass and the follow-up wiki hardening runs.
    48→
    49→- local deterministic smoke coverage is passing
    50→- the sequential local runner is passing
    51→- remote GitHub Actions runs are verified through warm-cache wiki run `22877413563`, with `0` hard failures and `1` soft warning
    52→- push, schedule, and manual workflow runs auto-promote staged generated outputs back into `openwrt-condensed-docs/`; GitHub Pages mirrors that same tree from `gh-pages` with `.nojekyll` branch publishing
    53→- the remaining work is concentrated in one deferred dockerman ucode soft warning, bounded wiki-derived cleanup, and deciding whether extra corpus telemetry is worth the complexity
    54→
    55→## License
    56→
    57→Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. See `LICENSE` for details. Generated documentation derives from upstream OpenWrt project sources and retains their respective upstream licensing context.
    58→
```
