<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `C:\Users\MC\Documents\AirSentinel\openwrt-docs4ai-v12-copilot\docs\plans\v12\public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`

```text
     1→# Public Distribution Product & Delivery Plan (V5a)
     2→
     3→Recorded: 2026-03-15
     4→
     5→Status: Consolidated implementation-ready specification
     6→
     7→Scope: Unify the product-output naming and layout contract (V4a), the delivery
     8→mechanics (V4b), and the publication architecture (V3) into one actionable
     9→document with phased implementation, explicit rollback gates, and a complete
    10→rework map of every file that must change.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## 1. Why V5a Exists
    15→
    16→V3, V4a, and V4b each advanced the plan, but they left three problems
    17→unresolved:
    18→
    19→1. **Contradictory public-tree models.** V3 still published the corpus inside
    20→   an `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper with a separate "distribution shell"
    21→   above it. V4a eliminated wrappers and moved to a direct-root `release-tree/`
    22→   where modules sit at root. The two specs describe different public trees and
    23→   different URL contracts. V5a resolves this: the V4a direct-root model wins.
    24→
    25→2. **No per-file rework map.** V4a listed categories of scripts that "clearly
    26→   need updates" but did not map the exact old-contract strings inside each
    27→   file, the exact replacement strings, or the order of safe changes. Without
    28→   that map, a developer must survey the whole codebase before writing a single
    29→   line. V5a provides the map.
    30→
    31→3. **No rollback-safe phase gates.** V4b described a recommended sequence but
    32→   did not define what condition must be true before advancing to the next
    33→   phase, or what happens if one phase fails. V5a adds explicit go/no-go
    34→   checkpoints so that a failed phase leaves the pipeline in a known-good
    35→   state.
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## 2. Consolidated Locked Decisions
    40→
    41→These decisions are final. They supersede any conflicting statement in V1
    42→through V4b.
    43→
    44→| # | Decision | Source |
    45→| --- | --- | --- |
    46→| D1 | The public release tree is direct-root. No `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper in any public surface. | V4a §9, supersedes V2/V3 |
    47→| D2 | Every public module folder uses `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`, and optional `types/`. | V4a §7.5 |
    48→| D3 | Module names are not repeated in child filenames inside the module folder. | V4a §13 |
    49→| D4 | The pipeline main output root contains `release-tree/` and `support-tree/`. Only `release-tree/` is publishable. | V4a §9 + V4b §2 |
    50→| D5 | `release-inputs/release-include/` adds or overrides files after generation, before validation. | V4a §10 + V4b §3 |
    51→| D6 | The former top-level public `L2-semantic/` is replaced by per-module `chunked-reference/`. | V4a §9.3 |
    52→| D7 | All public output names come from shared config or lib constants, not scattered string literals. | V4a §6 goal 9, V4b §4 |
    53→| D8 | The GitHub organization is `openwrt-docs4ai`. Two target repos: `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` (Pages) and `corpus` (Releases). | V3 §3, §5 |
    54→| D9 | The ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` (project-named, not `corpus/`). | V3 §7 |
    55→| D10 | Authentication uses a GitHub App with installation tokens, not long-lived PATs. | V2 §6, V3 §14 |
    56→| D11 | Git history on target repos is append-only. No force-push. | V2 §9.2 |
    57→| D12 | Same-day reruns update the existing release and replace the dated ZIP asset. | V2 §9.3, V3 §15.2 |
    58→| D13 | Existing source-repo deploy behavior must remain intact even if new distribution steps fail. | REVIEW §2.D, V2 §9.4 |
    59→| D14 | The `openwrt-condensed-docs` name is internal to the source repo only; it must never appear in any public path, URL, or visible label. | V3 §4.1, V4a §7.8 |
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## 3. Resolved Conflicts
    64→
    65→### 3.1 V3 wrapper vs V4a direct-root
    66→
    67→V3 §6 defined a public tree with `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `CHANGES.md`,
    68→`changelog.json`, `cross-link-registry.json`, `repo-manifest.json`, and
    69→`signature-inventory.json` at product root.
    70→
    71→V4a §2, §9 eliminated all of those from the public tree.
    72→
    73→**Resolution:** V4a wins. The public release tree contains only:
    74→
    75→```text
    76→release-tree/
    77→├── README.md
    78→├── AGENTS.md
    79→├── llms.txt
    80→├── llms-full.txt
    81→├── index.html
    82→├── {module}/
    83→│   ├── llms.txt
    84→│   ├── map.md
    85→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
    86→│   ├── chunked-reference/
    87→│   │   └── {topic}.md ...
    88→│   └── types/ (optional)
    89→│       └── {module}.d.ts
    90→└── ... (more modules)
    91→```
    92→
    93→The items V3 listed at root that V4a removed are relocated:
    94→
    95→| V3 root item | V5a location |
    96→| --- | --- |
    97→| `L1-raw/` | `support-tree/raw/` |
    98→| `L2-semantic/` | redistributed into `{module}/chunked-reference/` |
    99→| `CHANGES.md` | `support-tree/telemetry/CHANGES.md` |
   100→| `changelog.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/changelog.json` |
   101→| `cross-link-registry.json` | `support-tree/manifests/cross-link-registry.json` |
   102→| `repo-manifest.json` | `support-tree/manifests/repo-manifest.json` |
   103→| `signature-inventory.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/signature-inventory.json` |
   104→
   105→### 3.2 V3 delivery-root overlays vs V4a release-include
   106→
   107→V3 §8 defined `delivery-root/common/`, `delivery-root/pages/`, and
   108→`delivery-root/release/` as source-controlled overlay directories.
   109→
   110→V4a §10 defined `release-inputs/release-include/` as the single overlay point.
   111→
   112→**Resolution:** Merge both. Use one overlay parent directory with
   113→target-specific children:
   114→
   115→```text
   116→release-inputs/
   117→├── release-include/          (common overlay, applied to all surfaces)
   118→├── pages-include/            (Pages-only overlay, e.g. .nojekyll)
   119→└── release-repo-include/    (release-repo-only overlay, if ever needed)
   120→```
   121→
   122→This preserves the V3 concern about target-specific files (`.nojekyll` for
   123→Pages) while keeping the V4a single-overlay concept.
   124→
   125→### 3.3 Module-local README.md
   126→
   127→V4a §14 question 1 asked whether module-local `README.md` should be in the
   128→first fixed-schema contract.
   129→
   130→**Resolution:** Not in the first pass. The `llms.txt` + `map.md` routing pair
   131→is sufficient. A module `README.md` can be added later if proven necessary.
   132→
   133→### 3.4 Oversized module strategy
   134→
   135→V4a §14 question 4 asked how `bundled-reference.md` handles oversized modules.
   136→
   137→**Resolution:** For the first pass, keep the existing sharding behavior:
   138→
   139→- `bundled-reference.md` remains the stable index filename.
   140→- If a module exceeds the token limit, `bundled-reference.md` becomes an index
   141→  that points to `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` files.
   142→- `chunked-reference/` topic files are always present regardless of sharding.
   143→- The sharded parts sit alongside `bundled-reference.md`, not inside
   144→  `chunked-reference/`.
   145→
   146→### 3.5 Support-tree persistence
   147→
   148→V4a §14 question 2 asked whether `support-tree/` stays persistent.
   149→
   150→**Resolution:** `support-tree/` is ephemeral during CI runs and may be uploaded
   151→as a CI artifact for debugging. It is not a persistent on-disk contract. Its
   152→internal layout may change without notice.
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## 4. Complete Rework Map
   157→
   158→This section maps every file in the current codebase that contains
   159→old-contract names, the exact strings that must change, and what they become.
   160→
   161→### 4.1 Shared configuration — `lib/config.py`
   162→
   163→Current state: defines `OUTDIR`, `L1_RAW_WORKDIR`, `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR`, and
   164→`REPO_MANIFEST_PATH`. All paths are relative to `OUTDIR` which defaults to
   165→`openwrt-condensed-docs`.
   166→
   167→Required changes:
   168→
   169→| Current | New | Notes |
   170→| --- | --- | --- |
   171→| `OUTDIR` defaults to `openwrt-condensed-docs` | `OUTDIR` becomes the main output root (formerly "working output" only) | Internal stages still write here |
   172→| - | Add `RELEASE_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / release-tree` | New constant |
   173→| - | Add `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / support-tree` | New constant |
   174→| - | Add `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR` = `release-inputs/release-include` | New constant, relative to repo root |
   175→| - | Add public-name constants: `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`, `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME` | Central ownership of `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference`, `types` |
   176→| `L1_RAW_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L1-raw` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/raw` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   177→| `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L2-semantic` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/semantic-pages` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   178→
   179→**Risk reduction:** Do NOT change `OUTDIR` semantics for early stages. Early
   180→stages continue to write `L1-raw/` and `L2-semantic/` under the existing
   181→`OUTDIR`. Only the late assembly stage reads from those locations and writes
   182→into `release-tree/`.
   183→
   184→### 4.2 Assembly stage — `05a-assemble-references.py`
   185→
   186→Current hard-coded strings and their required changes:
   187→
   188→| Line range | Current string | Required change |
   189→| --- | --- | --- |
   190→| Line 6-8 (docstring) | `OUTDIR/{module}/{module}-complete-reference.md`, `{module}-skeleton.md` | Update docstring to new names |
   191→| Line 36 | `L2_DIR = os.path.join(OUTDIR, "L2-semantic")` | Keep for reading; assembly output paths change |
   192→| Line 157 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` | `f"bundled-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` |
   193→| Line 330 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.md"` | `"bundled-reference.md"` (from config constant) |
   194→| Line 331 | `f"{module}-skeleton.md"` | `"map.md"` (from config constant) |
   195→| Line 327 | `out_mod_dir = os.path.join(OUTDIR, module)` | `os.path.join(RELEASE_TREE_DIR, module)` |
   196→| Line 202 | `f"# {module} Complete Reference"` | `f"# {module} Bundled Reference"` |
   197→| Line 293 | `f"# {module} (Skeleton Semantic Map)"` | `f"# {module} Navigation Map"` |
   198→
   199→**New responsibility:** Script `05a` must also write `chunked-reference/`
   200→topic files. Currently, L2 semantic pages exist under `OUTDIR/L2-semantic/
   201→{module}/` but are not explicitly copied into a module-local page folder. The
   202→assembly stage must:
   203→
   204→1. Write `bundled-reference.md` (renamed from `{module}-complete-reference.md`)
   205→2. Write `map.md` (renamed from `{module}-skeleton.md`)
   206→3. Copy or link each L2 topic page into `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md`
   207→
   208→This is the most significant new code in the rework.

   302→### 4.10 Workflow — `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`
   303→
   304→Required new workflow steps (added after existing deploy steps):
   305→
   306→1. Create dual output root (`release-tree/` + `support-tree/`)
   307→2. Run late assembly into `release-tree/`
   308→3. Apply `release-inputs/release-include/`
   309→4. Apply `release-inputs/pages-include/` (for Pages target)
   310→5. Run gatekeeper validation on merged `release-tree/`
   311→6. Mint GitHub App installation token
   312→7. Push to `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io`
   313→8. Push to `corpus`
   314→9. Create/update dated release and ZIP on `corpus`
   315→
   316→Existing steps 1–5 (source-repo promotion, `gh-pages` mirror) remain unchanged
   317→per D13.
   318→

   320→
   321→These early-stage scripts do not need public-contract changes:
   322→
   323→- `01-clone-repos.py` — clones upstream, no public names
   324→- `02a` through `02h` — extractors write to `WORKDIR/L1-raw/`, internal only
   325→- `03-normalize-semantic.py` — writes to `WORKDIR/L2-semantic/`, internal only
   326→- `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` — enriches L2 frontmatter, no public names
   327→
   328→These scripts write into the existing `WORKDIR` which becomes part of
   329→`support-tree/`. They do not need renaming because their output paths are
   330→never public.
   331→
   332→---
   333→
   334→## 5. Phased Implementation With Rollback Gates
   335→
   336→Each phase has a go/no-go gate. If the gate fails, the previous phase's
   337→state is the safe rollback point. The pipeline continues to produce the
   338→current old-contract output until the phase that switches public output
   339→is explicitly activated.
   340→
   341→### Phase 0: Add shared configuration constants
   342→
   343→**Goal:** Centralize all public output names in `lib/config.py`.
   344→
   345→**Changes:**
   346→- Add constants for `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`, `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`,
   347→  `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`,
   348→  `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME`,
   349→  `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR`, `PAGES_INCLUDE_DIR`
   350→
   351→**Gate:** All existing tests pass with no behavioral change. The new constants
   352→are defined but not yet consumed by any stage.
   353→
   354→**Rollback:** Revert the `lib/config.py` additions. Zero risk.
   355→
   356→---
   357→
   358→### Phase 1: Add release assembly stage (dual output, feature-flagged)
   359→
   360→**Goal:** Create a new late-stage script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` that
   361→reads from the existing pipeline output and writes the V5a `release-tree/`.
   362→
   363→**Changes:**
   364→- New script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   365→  - Creates `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   366→  - Creates `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`
   367→  - For each module:
   368→    - Copies/renames `{module}-complete-reference.md` → `bundled-reference.md`
   369→    - Copies/renames `{module}-skeleton.md` → `map.md`
   370→    - Copies L2 topic pages into `chunked-reference/`
   371→    - Copies optional `types/` files
   372→    - Generates module `llms.txt` with new paths
   373→  - Copies root files (`llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`,
   374→    `index.html`) with path rewrites
   375→  - Moves support-only artifacts to `support-tree/`
   376→- Feature flag: `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` env var (default `false`)
   377→- When flag is off, existing stages still produce old-contract output
   378→
   379→**Why a new script instead of modifying 05a:** This is the single most
   380→important risk-reduction decision. Script `05a` currently works. Inserting
   381→rename logic into it creates a change where a bug can break the existing
   382→pipeline. A new script that runs after `05a` and reads its output means:
   383→
   384→- `05a` continues to produce known-good old-contract files
   385→- `05e` produces the new contract files alongside them
   386→- Both can be validated before any consumer switches
   387→- If `05e` is broken, deleting it restores the old pipeline
   388→
   389→**Gate:** When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`:
   390→- `release-tree/` contains the full V5a layout
   391→- `support-tree/` contains L1, L2, telemetry, and manifests
   392→- All old-contract output still exists unchanged under `OUTDIR/`
   393→- All existing tests pass (they still check `OUTDIR/`)
   394→- A new parallel test suite validates `release-tree/`
   395→
   396→**Rollback:** Set `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false`. The script is skipped. Old
   397→output is unchanged.
   398→
   399→---
   400→
   401→### Phase 2: Add release-tree validation
   402→
   403→**Goal:** Create new tests and a validator that assert the V5a contract on
   404→`release-tree/`.
   405→
   406→**Changes:**
   407→- New test file:
   408→  `tests/pytest/pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py`
   409→  - Asserts every module has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`,
   410→    `chunked-reference/`
   411→  - Asserts no `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere
   412→    in `release-tree/`
   413→  - Asserts bundled-vs-chunked parity for at least one module
   414→  - Asserts `release-tree/` contains no `support-tree/` items
   415→- Extend `08-validate-output.py` with a `release-tree` validation mode
   416→  (behind feature flag)
   417→
   418→**Gate:** All new tests pass against the `release-tree/` produced by Phase 1.
   419→All existing tests still pass against old `OUTDIR/` output.
   420→
   421→**Rollback:** Delete the new test file and validation mode. Zero risk.
   422→
   423→---
   424→
   425→### Phase 3: Add release-include merge
   426→
   427→**Goal:** Implement the overlay merge from `release-inputs/release-include/`.
   428→
   429→**Changes:**
   430→- Create `release-inputs/release-include/` in the source repo (initially
   431→  empty or with a `README.md` explaining its purpose)
   432→- Create `release-inputs/pages-include/.nojekyll`
   433→- Create `release-inputs/release-repo-include/` (empty, reserved)
   434→- Add merge logic to `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   435→  after generating `release-tree/`, copy `release-include/` contents on top
   436→- Add merge-precedence test
   437→
   438→**Gate:** Include merge produces expected results. A test file placed in
   439→`release-include/` appears in the merged `release-tree/`. A generated file
   440→replaced by an include-override contains the override content. Existing tests
   441→pass.
   442→
   443→**Rollback:** Remove the merge logic from `05e`. The `release-include/`
   444→directory stays inert.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→### Phase 4: Switch late stages to write into release-tree
   449→
   450→**Goal:** Modify scripts `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` to consume
   451→`RELEASE_TREE_DIR` when the feature flag is on.
   452→
   453→**Changes:**
   454→- `05a`: output paths use config constants; filenames use new names
   455→- `05b`: text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   456→- `06`: routing text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   457→- `07`: path labels and links updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   458→- `08`: validation logic updated per §4.6
   459→- Script `05e` is retired (its functionality is absorbed into the modified
   460→  stages)
   461→
   462→This is the riskiest phase because it modifies working scripts.
   463→
   464→**Risk mitigation:**
   465→- Feature flag controls whether stages write to old or new paths
   466→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false` proves old contract
   467→  is intact
   468→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` proves new contract
   469→  works
   470→- Only after both pass green does the flag default change
   471→
   472→**Gate:** Full smoke tests pass in both flag states. The `release-tree/`
   473→output matches what Phase 1's `05e` produced. The old `OUTDIR/` output matches
   474→what the pipeline produced before Phase 4.
   475→
   476→**Rollback:** Reset feature flag to `false`. All stages revert to old output
   477→paths.
   478→
   479→---
   480→
   481→### Phase 5: Update test suite to new contract
   482→
   483→**Goal:** Modify `smoke_pipeline_support.py`, `pytest_02`, and `pytest_06`
   484→to assert the new contract as the primary contract.
   485→
   486→**Changes:**
   487→- Update all assertions per §4.7, §4.8, §4.9
   488→- Old-contract assertions are removed
   489→- Feature flag default changes to `true`
   490→
   491→**Gate:** Full test suite passes with the new contract as primary. Manual
   492→inspection of `release-tree/` output confirms correctness.
   493→
   494→**Rollback:** Restore old assertions and set flag to `false`.
   495→
   496→---
   497→
   498→### Phase 6: Provision public infrastructure and deploy
   499→
   500→**Goal:** Set up the external GitHub organization, repos, and deploy workflow.
   501→
   502→**Changes:**
   503→1. Create `openwrt-docs4ai` GitHub organization
   504→2. Create `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` repo (Pages)
   505→3. Create `corpus` repo (Releases)
   506→4. Create and install GitHub App
   507→5. Store secrets in source repo
   508→6. Add deploy workflow steps per §4.10
   509→7. First live publish
   510→
   511→**Gate:** Live Pages site serves direct-root content. Release repo has a
   512→fresh commit. ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` with the V5a layout.
   513→No `openwrt-condensed-docs`, `L1-raw`, or `L2-semantic` visible anywhere.
   514→
   515→**Rollback:** Disable the new workflow steps. Source-repo deploy continues
   516→unchanged.
   517→
   518→---
   519→
   520→### Phase 7: Remove old contract and feature flag
   521→
   522→**Goal:** Clean up the codebase after live validation.
   523→
   524→**Changes:**
   525→- Remove `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` feature flag
   526→- Remove old-contract code paths
   527→- Retire script `05e` if not already retired in Phase 4
   528→- Update `DEVELOPMENT.md` and architecture docs
   529→
   530→**Gate:** Full test suite, CI pipeline, and live deploy all green without the
   531→old contract code paths.
   532→
   533→**Rollback:** Restore the feature flag and old paths. This is the only phase
   534→with a moderate rollback cost, which is why it is last.
   535→
   536→---
   537→
   538→## 6. Rework Summary By Risk Level
   539→
   540→| Risk | Phase | Files affected | What can go wrong | Mitigation |
   541→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
   542→| **None** | 0 | `lib/config.py` | Nothing; additive only | Constants unused until Phase 1 |
   543→| **Low** | 1 | New `05e` script | New script bugs produce bad `release-tree/` | Does not touch existing output; feature-flagged off |
   544→| **Low** | 2 | New test file, `08` extension | Bad test logic gives false confidence | Tests run against Phase 1 output; manual cross-check |
   545→| **Low** | 3 | `release-inputs/`, `05e` merge logic | Include merge overwrites wrong file | Merge is additive; test asserts exact behavior |
   546→| **High** | 4 | `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` | Bug in modified script breaks existing pipeline | Feature flag; dual smoke test; rollback to flag=false |
   547→| **Medium** | 5 | Test support, pytest files | Wrong assertions pass bad output | Diff old vs new assertions for parity |
   548→| **Medium** | 6 | Workflow YAML, external infra | Deploy fails or publishes wrong tree | Deploy is additive after existing deploy; gatekeeper checks |
   549→| **Low** | 7 | Cleanup only | Premature removal of fallback | Only after live validation |
   550→
   551→---
   552→
   553→## 7. Final Release Tree Contract
   554→
   555→When all phases are complete, the published product tree looks like this:
   556→
   557→```text
   558→(repository root or ZIP root openwrt-docs4ai/)
   559→├── README.md
   560→├── AGENTS.md
   561→├── llms.txt
   562→├── llms-full.txt
   563→├── index.html
   564→├── luci/
   565→│   ├── llms.txt
   566→│   ├── map.md
   567→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   568→│   └── chunked-reference/
   569→│       ├── javascript-api-cbi.md
   570→│       └── ...
   571→├── luci-examples/
   572→│   ├── llms.txt
   573→│   ├── map.md
   574→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   575→│   └── chunked-reference/
   576→│       └── ...
   577→├── openwrt-core/
   578→│   └── ...
   579→├── openwrt-hotplug/
   580→│   └── ...
   581→├── procd/
   582→│   └── ...
   583→├── uci/
   584→│   └── ...
   585→├── ucode/
   586→│   ├── llms.txt
   587→│   ├── map.md
   588→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   589→│   ├── chunked-reference/
   590→│   │   └── ...
   591→│   └── types/
   592→│       └── ucode.d.ts
   593→└── wiki/
   594→    └── ...
   595→```
   596→
   597→### 7.1 Items guaranteed absent from the public tree
   598→
   599→- `L1-raw/`
   600→- `L2-semantic/`
   601→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
   602→- `corpus/`
   603→- `*-skeleton.md`
   604→- `*-complete-reference.md`
   605→- `cross-link-registry.json`
   606→- `repo-manifest.json`
   607→- `signature-inventory.json`
   608→- `changelog.json`
   609→- `CHANGES.md`
   610→- Any `.meta.json` sidecar
   611→
   612→---
   613→
   614→## 8. Delivery Surfaces
   615→
   616→| Surface | Root | Source |
   617→| --- | --- | --- |
   618→| Pages site | `https://openwrt-docs4ai.github.io/` | Validated `release-tree/` + `pages-include/` |
   619→| Release repo | `https://github.com/openwrt-docs4ai/corpus` | Validated `release-tree/` + `release-repo-include/` |
   620→| ZIP download | `openwrt-docs4ai-YYYY-MM-DD.zip` → `openwrt-docs4ai/` | Validated `release-tree/` (no target-specific overlays) |
   621→
   622→All three surfaces share the same validated `release-tree/` as their content
   623→source. Target-specific overlays (`.nojekyll` for Pages) are applied after.
   624→
   625→---
   626→
   627→## 9. Gatekeeper Rules
   628→
   629→Before any cross-repo publish, the pipeline must assert:
   630→
   631→| Check | Assertion |
   632→| --- | --- |
   633→| Root router exists | `release-tree/llms.txt` exists and is > 512 bytes |
   634→| Root index exists | `release-tree/index.html` exists |
   635→| Module count | At least 4 module directories exist |
   636→| No legacy leakage | No `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere in `release-tree/` |
   637→| Fixed schema | Every module dir has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/` |
   638→| Chunked non-empty | Every module's `chunked-reference/` has at least one `.md` file |
   639→
   640→Failure of any check blocks publish and fails the job visibly.
   641→
   642→---
   643→
   644→## 10. Document Supersession
   645→
   646→| Document | Status after V5a |
   647→| --- | --- |
   648→| V1 (`public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-14.md`) | Historical; fully superseded |
   649→| REVIEW (`-REVIEW.md`) | Historical; all concerns addressed |
   650→| V2 (`-V2.md`) | Historical; auth and deploy details carried forward |
   651→| V3 (`-V3.md`) | Partially superseded; delivery architecture preserved, public tree changed |
   652→| V4a (`-V4a.md`) | Incorporated; naming contract locked |
   653→| V4b (`-V4b.md`) | Incorporated; mechanics absorbed |
   654→| **V5a (this document)** | **Active; implementation-ready specification** |
   655→
   656→---
   657→
   658→## 11. Open Questions (Non-Blocking)
   659→
   660→These do not block implementation but should be revisited after Phase 5:
   661→
   662→1. Should the root `llms.txt` carry an explicit "router" title line as
   663→   suggested in V4a §8?
   664→2. Should `release-inputs/pages-include/` support a Pages-specific
   665→   `index.html` override for the site landing page?
   666→3. What is the long-term telemetry and changelog strategy for the public tree
   667→   once the support files are excluded?
   668→
```

```text
     1→# openwrt-docs4ai v12 Execution Map
     2→
     3→## Layer Locations
     4→
     5→- `L0`: `tmp/repo-*`
     6→- `L1`: `tmp/L1-raw/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/{module}/`
     7→- `L2`: `tmp/L2-semantic/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/{module}/`
     8→- `L3`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/` root and per-module subdirectories
     9→- `L4`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/`
    10→- `L5`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
    11→
    12→## Ordered Script Flow
    13→
    14→1. `01-clone-repos.py`
    15→2. `02a-scrape-wiki.py` (independent branch in hosted workflow)
    16→3. `02b` through `02h` (repo-backed extractors, gated on `01`)
    17→4. `03-normalize-semantic.py`
    18→5. `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` when AI enrichment is enabled; it performs its own AI-store preflight before enrichment
    19→6. `05a-assemble-references.py`
    20→7. `05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py`
    21→8. `05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py`
    22→9. `05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py`
    23→10. `06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py`
    24→11. `07-generate-web-index.py`
    25→12. `08-validate-output.py`
    26→
    27→Letter suffixes denote scripts that are parallelizable in deployment. Local smoke tests may still execute them sequentially.
    28→
    29→`tools/manage_ai_store.py` is the maintained local scratch-first helper.
    30→
    31→Hosted Option B topology now runs `02a` in a separate job without waiting on `initialize`, while repo-backed `02b` through `02h` remain in a matrix job that depends on `initialize`.
    32→
    33→## Execution Contract Matrix
    34→
    35→This matrix defines placement rationale, mutability boundaries, dependencies, and failure power.
    36→
    37→| Step | Mutability & atomicity | Primary dependencies | Failure mode in current workflow | Deploy impact |
    38→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    39→| `01` | Writes L0 clones and `repo-manifest.json` under `WORKDIR`; isolated to initialize job | Git checkout, network access | Hard fail (`initialize` fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    40→| `02a` | Writes only wiki L1 outputs and wiki cache under `WORKDIR` | Internet/wiki availability, `WORKDIR` | Hard fail (`extract_wiki` job fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    41→| `02b`-`02h` | Write module L1 outputs under `WORKDIR`; no publish writes | L0 clone artifacts from `01`, toolchain deps | Hard fail (repo-backed matrix leg fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    42→| `03` | Converts L1 to L2 and promotes staged intermediates inside `OUTDIR`; atomic within process job sandbox | L1 artifacts, metadata sidecars | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    43→| `04` | Optional in-place L2 enrichment in staged `OUTDIR`; no direct publish writes | `SKIP_AI`, cache availability, optional token | If `SKIP_AI=true`, skipped cleanly. If enabled and it fails, hard fail in `process` | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    44→| `05a`-`05d` | Deterministic generation of publish companions inside `OUTDIR` | Staged L2 and registry outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    45→| `06` | Builds routing/index artifacts inside `OUTDIR` | L2 metadata and reference outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    46→| `07` | Generates `index.html` inside `OUTDIR` | Prior index inputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    47→| `08` | Validation gate over full staged `OUTDIR`; no external writes | Complete staged corpus | Hard fail on hard checks | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    48→| `deploy` | `rsync -a --delete` from staging into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` and optional bot commit | Successful `process` job and `final-staging` artifact | Hard fail in deploy only | Only step that can change published output |
    49→
    50→## Return-State Guarantees
    51→
    52→1. If any `process`-phase script fails, `final-staging` is not promoted and deploy does not run.
    53→2. Because promotion is isolated to the deploy job, partially generated outputs from `03` through `08` stay sandboxed in runner staging.
    54→3. This means a failed `08` cannot publish a mismatched `07` web index to the committed output tree.
    55→4. `SKIP_AI=true` is the explicit optional path for AI enrichment; AI failure is not ignored once the AI step is enabled.
    56→
    57→## Key Handoffs
    58→
    59→- `01` produces cloned repositories and any repo manifest data required by downstream stages.
    60→- `02*` scripts write L1 markdown plus `.meta.json` sidecars.
    61→- `02b` now exposes import-safe helper surfaces for fenced-block normalization and example fixups so targeted tests can import it directly.
    62→- `03` reads only L1 plus metadata and writes L2 plus a cross-link registry before promoting stable intermediates into `OUTDIR`.
    63→- `03` is also the correct place for bounded module-specific cleanup that should not mutate L1 raw retention; the current live example is wiki-only cleanup for residual DokuWiki and pandoc artifacts.
    64→- `04` mutates staged L2 files in place when enabled.
    65→- `05` consumes staged L2 files and emits skeletons plus monolithic references.
    66→- `06*` scripts consume staged L2 metadata and generated references to emit maps, agent instructions, IDE schemas, and telemetry.
    67→- `07` consumes the map outputs and writes the HTML landing page.
    68→- `08` validates the full `OUTDIR` tree and now exposes import-safe parsing helpers for direct unit coverage.
    69→
    70→## Observability Baseline
    71→
    72→Current hosted logs expose started/completed timestamps per job and per step via `gh run view --json jobs`.
    73→
    74→Example timing snapshot from successful run `22901854476`:
    75→
    76→- `initialize`: ~31s total, with `Clone repositories` ~9s
    77→- `extract_wiki (02a-scrape-wiki.py)`: ~211s
    78→- `process`: ~56s
    79→- `deploy`: ~18s
    80→
    81→Current workflow observability artifacts now include:
    82→
    83→- extractor-level status manifests (`extract-status-*`)
    84→- an always-generated `extract-summary` artifact
    85→- a `process-summary` staging contract artifact
    86→- an always-generated `pipeline-summary` artifact
    87→
    88→These artifacts are intended to be the first-stop troubleshooting surface before deep log forensics.
    89→
    90→## Scope Note
    91→
    92→The current AI-alignment slice keeps `04` as the single numbered AI stage and
    93→moves local review, audit, validation, and promotion into `tools/manage_ai_store.py`.
    94→Deeper model-selection and quality-scoring work remains deferred.
    95→
    96→## High-Value Debugging Paths
    97→
    98→- If the issue is stale or malformed wiki prose in committed outputs, inspect `02a` for source/cache behavior and `03` for semantic cleanup.
    99→- If the issue is misclassified ucode examples or false syntax warnings, inspect `02b` and `08` together because the extractor and validator now share regression coverage boundaries.
   100→- If the issue is missing, stale, or structurally invalid AI summaries, inspect `04`, `tools/manage_ai_store.py`, and the `data/base/` plus `data/override/` store roots together.
   101→- If the issue is missing or overwritten generated outputs, inspect the workflow `process` to `deploy` handoff because `staging/` is now the authoritative promotion source.
   102→- If the issue is only visible in committed `openwrt-condensed-docs/`, compare it against the focused corpus sanity snapshot from `tests/pytest/pytest_03_wiki_corpus_sanity_test.py` to decide whether the repo tree is stale or the normalization logic is still wrong.
   103→
   104→## Required Environment Variables
   105→
   106→- `WORKDIR`
   107→- `OUTDIR`
   108→- `SKIP_WIKI`
   109→- `SKIP_AI`
   110→- `WIKI_MAX_PAGES`
   111→- `MAX_AI_FILES`
   112→- `VALIDATE_MODE`
   113→
   114→## Required Schema Facts
   115→
   116→- L1 files are pure markdown and must not begin with YAML frontmatter.
   117→- L1 sidecars are `.meta.json` files stored beside the markdown file.
   118→- L2 files require YAML frontmatter with at least `title`, `module`, `origin_type`, `token_count`, and `version`.
   119→- L4 files use one top-level YAML block for the full monolith rather than reusing L2 frontmatter inline.
   120→
   121→## Verified Execution Priority
   122→
   123→The first required execution proof remains local and sequential. GitHub Actions is now a verified second-stage execution and publication layer, so pipeline debugging should distinguish between local logic regressions and remote promotion or cache-state effects.
   124→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `C:\Users\MC\Documents\AirSentinel\openwrt-docs4ai-v12-copilot\docs\plans\v12\public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`

```text
     1→# Public Distribution Product & Delivery Plan (V5a)
     2→
     3→Recorded: 2026-03-15
     4→
     5→Status: Consolidated implementation-ready specification
     6→
     7→Scope: Unify the product-output naming and layout contract (V4a), the delivery
     8→mechanics (V4b), and the publication architecture (V3) into one actionable
     9→document with phased implementation, explicit rollback gates, and a complete
    10→rework map of every file that must change.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## 1. Why V5a Exists
    15→
    16→V3, V4a, and V4b each advanced the plan, but they left three problems
    17→unresolved:
    18→
    19→1. **Contradictory public-tree models.** V3 still published the corpus inside
    20→   an `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper with a separate "distribution shell"
    21→   above it. V4a eliminated wrappers and moved to a direct-root `release-tree/`
    22→   where modules sit at root. The two specs describe different public trees and
    23→   different URL contracts. V5a resolves this: the V4a direct-root model wins.
    24→
    25→2. **No per-file rework map.** V4a listed categories of scripts that "clearly
    26→   need updates" but did not map the exact old-contract strings inside each
    27→   file, the exact replacement strings, or the order of safe changes. Without
    28→   that map, a developer must survey the whole codebase before writing a single
    29→   line. V5a provides the map.
    30→
    31→3. **No rollback-safe phase gates.** V4b described a recommended sequence but
    32→   did not define what condition must be true before advancing to the next
    33→   phase, or what happens if one phase fails. V5a adds explicit go/no-go
    34→   checkpoints so that a failed phase leaves the pipeline in a known-good
    35→   state.
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## 2. Consolidated Locked Decisions
    40→
    41→These decisions are final. They supersede any conflicting statement in V1
    42→through V4b.
    43→
    44→| # | Decision | Source |
    45→| --- | --- | --- |
    46→| D1 | The public release tree is direct-root. No `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper in any public surface. | V4a §9, supersedes V2/V3 |
    47→| D2 | Every public module folder uses `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`, and optional `types/`. | V4a §7.5 |
    48→| D3 | Module names are not repeated in child filenames inside the module folder. | V4a §13 |
    49→| D4 | The pipeline main output root contains `release-tree/` and `support-tree/`. Only `release-tree/` is publishable. | V4a §9 + V4b §2 |
    50→| D5 | `release-inputs/release-include/` adds or overrides files after generation, before validation. | V4a §10 + V4b §3 |
    51→| D6 | The former top-level public `L2-semantic/` is replaced by per-module `chunked-reference/`. | V4a §9.3 |
    52→| D7 | All public output names come from shared config or lib constants, not scattered string literals. | V4a §6 goal 9, V4b §4 |
    53→| D8 | The GitHub organization is `openwrt-docs4ai`. Two target repos: `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` (Pages) and `corpus` (Releases). | V3 §3, §5 |
    54→| D9 | The ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` (project-named, not `corpus/`). | V3 §7 |
    55→| D10 | Authentication uses a GitHub App with installation tokens, not long-lived PATs. | V2 §6, V3 §14 |
    56→| D11 | Git history on target repos is append-only. No force-push. | V2 §9.2 |
    57→| D12 | Same-day reruns update the existing release and replace the dated ZIP asset. | V2 §9.3, V3 §15.2 |
    58→| D13 | Existing source-repo deploy behavior must remain intact even if new distribution steps fail. | REVIEW §2.D, V2 §9.4 |
    59→| D14 | The `openwrt-condensed-docs` name is internal to the source repo only; it must never appear in any public path, URL, or visible label. | V3 §4.1, V4a §7.8 |
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## 3. Resolved Conflicts
    64→
    65→### 3.1 V3 wrapper vs V4a direct-root
    66→
    67→V3 §6 defined a public tree with `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `CHANGES.md`,
    68→`changelog.json`, `cross-link-registry.json`, `repo-manifest.json`, and
    69→`signature-inventory.json` at product root.
    70→
    71→V4a §2, §9 eliminated all of those from the public tree.
    72→
    73→**Resolution:** V4a wins. The public release tree contains only:
    74→
    75→```text
    76→release-tree/
    77→├── README.md
    78→├── AGENTS.md
    79→├── llms.txt
    80→├── llms-full.txt
    81→├── index.html
    82→├── {module}/
    83→│   ├── llms.txt
    84→│   ├── map.md
    85→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
    86→│   ├── chunked-reference/
    87→│   │   └── {topic}.md ...
    88→│   └── types/ (optional)
    89→│       └── {module}.d.ts
    90→└── ... (more modules)
    91→```
    92→
    93→The items V3 listed at root that V4a removed are relocated:
    94→
    95→| V3 root item | V5a location |
    96→| --- | --- |
    97→| `L1-raw/` | `support-tree/raw/` |
    98→| `L2-semantic/` | redistributed into `{module}/chunked-reference/` |
    99→| `CHANGES.md` | `support-tree/telemetry/CHANGES.md` |
   100→| `changelog.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/changelog.json` |
   101→| `cross-link-registry.json` | `support-tree/manifests/cross-link-registry.json` |
   102→| `repo-manifest.json` | `support-tree/manifests/repo-manifest.json` |
   103→| `signature-inventory.json` | `support-tree/telemetry/signature-inventory.json` |
   104→
   105→### 3.2 V3 delivery-root overlays vs V4a release-include
   106→
   107→V3 §8 defined `delivery-root/common/`, `delivery-root/pages/`, and
   108→`delivery-root/release/` as source-controlled overlay directories.
   109→
   110→V4a §10 defined `release-inputs/release-include/` as the single overlay point.
   111→
   112→**Resolution:** Merge both. Use one overlay parent directory with
   113→target-specific children:
   114→
   115→```text
   116→release-inputs/
   117→├── release-include/          (common overlay, applied to all surfaces)
   118→├── pages-include/            (Pages-only overlay, e.g. .nojekyll)
   119→└── release-repo-include/    (release-repo-only overlay, if ever needed)
   120→```
   121→
   122→This preserves the V3 concern about target-specific files (`.nojekyll` for
   123→Pages) while keeping the V4a single-overlay concept.
   124→
   125→### 3.3 Module-local README.md
   126→
   127→V4a §14 question 1 asked whether module-local `README.md` should be in the
   128→first fixed-schema contract.
   129→
   130→**Resolution:** Not in the first pass. The `llms.txt` + `map.md` routing pair
   131→is sufficient. A module `README.md` can be added later if proven necessary.
   132→
   133→### 3.4 Oversized module strategy
   134→
   135→V4a §14 question 4 asked how `bundled-reference.md` handles oversized modules.
   136→
   137→**Resolution:** For the first pass, keep the existing sharding behavior:
   138→
   139→- `bundled-reference.md` remains the stable index filename.
   140→- If a module exceeds the token limit, `bundled-reference.md` becomes an index
   141→  that points to `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` files.
   142→- `chunked-reference/` topic files are always present regardless of sharding.
   143→- The sharded parts sit alongside `bundled-reference.md`, not inside
   144→  `chunked-reference/`.
   145→
   146→### 3.5 Support-tree persistence
   147→
   148→V4a §14 question 2 asked whether `support-tree/` stays persistent.
   149→
   150→**Resolution:** `support-tree/` is ephemeral during CI runs and may be uploaded
   151→as a CI artifact for debugging. It is not a persistent on-disk contract. Its
   152→internal layout may change without notice.
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## 4. Complete Rework Map
   157→
   158→This section maps every file in the current codebase that contains
   159→old-contract names, the exact strings that must change, and what they become.
   160→
   161→### 4.1 Shared configuration — `lib/config.py`
   162→
   163→Current state: defines `OUTDIR`, `L1_RAW_WORKDIR`, `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR`, and
   164→`REPO_MANIFEST_PATH`. All paths are relative to `OUTDIR` which defaults to
   165→`openwrt-condensed-docs`.
   166→
   167→Required changes:
   168→
   169→| Current | New | Notes |
   170→| --- | --- | --- |
   171→| `OUTDIR` defaults to `openwrt-condensed-docs` | `OUTDIR` becomes the main output root (formerly "working output" only) | Internal stages still write here |
   172→| - | Add `RELEASE_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / release-tree` | New constant |
   173→| - | Add `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR` = `OUTDIR / support-tree` | New constant |
   174→| - | Add `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR` = `release-inputs/release-include` | New constant, relative to repo root |
   175→| - | Add public-name constants: `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`, `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME` | Central ownership of `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference`, `types` |
   176→| `L1_RAW_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L1-raw` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/raw` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   177→| `L2_SEMANTIC_WORKDIR` = `OUTDIR/L2-semantic` | Keep as working path, but under `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR/semantic-pages` in release assembly | Internal stages untouched |
   178→
   179→**Risk reduction:** Do NOT change `OUTDIR` semantics for early stages. Early
   180→stages continue to write `L1-raw/` and `L2-semantic/` under the existing
   181→`OUTDIR`. Only the late assembly stage reads from those locations and writes
   182→into `release-tree/`.
   183→
   184→### 4.2 Assembly stage — `05a-assemble-references.py`
   185→
   186→Current hard-coded strings and their required changes:
   187→
   188→| Line range | Current string | Required change |
   189→| --- | --- | --- |
   190→| Line 6-8 (docstring) | `OUTDIR/{module}/{module}-complete-reference.md`, `{module}-skeleton.md` | Update docstring to new names |
   191→| Line 36 | `L2_DIR = os.path.join(OUTDIR, "L2-semantic")` | Keep for reading; assembly output paths change |
   192→| Line 157 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` | `f"bundled-reference.part-{index:02d}.md"` |
   193→| Line 330 | `f"{module}-complete-reference.md"` | `"bundled-reference.md"` (from config constant) |
   194→| Line 331 | `f"{module}-skeleton.md"` | `"map.md"` (from config constant) |
   195→| Line 327 | `out_mod_dir = os.path.join(OUTDIR, module)` | `os.path.join(RELEASE_TREE_DIR, module)` |
   196→| Line 202 | `f"# {module} Complete Reference"` | `f"# {module} Bundled Reference"` |
   197→| Line 293 | `f"# {module} (Skeleton Semantic Map)"` | `f"# {module} Navigation Map"` |
   198→
   199→**New responsibility:** Script `05a` must also write `chunked-reference/`
   200→topic files. Currently, L2 semantic pages exist under `OUTDIR/L2-semantic/
   201→{module}/` but are not explicitly copied into a module-local page folder. The
   202→assembly stage must:
   203→
   204→1. Write `bundled-reference.md` (renamed from `{module}-complete-reference.md`)
   205→2. Write `map.md` (renamed from `{module}-skeleton.md`)
   206→3. Copy or link each L2 topic page into `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md`
   207→
   208→This is the most significant new code in the rework.
   209→
   210→### 4.3 Companion generators — `05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py`
   211→
   212→Current state: generates `AGENTS.md` and `README.md` with references to
   213→old paths.
   214→
   215→Required changes:
   216→
   217→| Current reference | Required change |
   218→| --- | --- |
   219→| `{module}-skeleton.md` | `map.md` |
   220→| `{module}-complete-reference.md` | `bundled-reference.md` |
   221→| `L2-semantic/` | `chunked-reference/` (or remove if not mentioned) |
   222→| `./openwrt-condensed-docs/` | remove from all visible text |
   223→
   224→Output location changes from `OUTDIR/` to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR/`.
   225→
   226→### 4.4 Routing index generator — `06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py`
   227→
   228→Current state: generates root `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, and per-module
   229→`llms.txt` files with hard-coded references to old names.
   230→
   231→Required changes:
   232→
   233→| Current reference | Required change |
   234→| --- | --- |
   235→| `{module}-skeleton.md` | `map.md` |
   236→| `{module}-complete-reference.md` | `bundled-reference.md` |
   237→| `L2-semantic/{module}/{topic}.md` references in module `llms.txt` | `chunked-reference/{topic}.md` |
   238→| `L2-semantic/` references in `llms-full.txt` | `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md` |
   239→
   240→Output location changes from `OUTDIR/` to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR/`.
   241→
   242→### 4.5 Web index generator — `07-generate-web-index.py`
   243→
   244→Current state: generates `index.html` with hard-coded `openwrt-condensed-docs`
   245→path prefixes and old filenames in visible labels.
   246→
   247→Required changes:
   248→
   249→| Current reference | Required change |
   250→| --- | --- |
   251→| `./openwrt-condensed-docs/` prefix in visible paths | Direct-root `./` prefix |
   252→| `L2-semantic/` labels and links | `{module}/chunked-reference/` |
   253→| `{module}-skeleton.md` labels | `map.md` |
   254→| `{module}-complete-reference.md` labels | `bundled-reference.md` |
   255→| Title referencing `openwrt-condensed-docs publish tree` | Update to product name |
   256→
   257→Output location changes to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR/`.
   258→
   259→### 4.6 Validator — `08-validate-output.py`
   260→
   261→Current state: validates old names and paths.
   262→
   263→Required changes:
   264→
   265→| Current validation | Required change |
   266→| --- | --- |
   267→| Checks for `L2-semantic/` existence | Check for `chunked-reference/` inside each module |
   268→| Checks for `{module}-skeleton.md` | Check for `map.md` |
   269→| Checks for `{module}-complete-reference.md` | Check for `bundled-reference.md` |
   270→| Checks for `openwrt-condensed-docs/` root paths in HTML | Check for direct-root paths |
   271→| - | Add: reject `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/` in `release-tree/` |
   272→| - | Add: reject `openwrt-condensed-docs` in any visible text |
   273→| - | Add: validate `release-tree/` vs `support-tree/` separation |
   274→| - | Add: validate release-include merge integrity |
   275→
   276→### 4.7 Test support — `tests/support/smoke_pipeline_support.py`

   278→Current hard-coded assertions that must change:
   279→
   280→| Line | Current assertion | Required change |

   286→| 277 | `L2-semantic/wiki/wiki_page-service-events.md` | `wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-service-events.md` |

   289→| 321 | `./L2-semantic/ucode/c_source-api-fs.md` | `./ucode/chunked-reference/c_source-api-fs.md` |

   293→

   295→contract. The exact changes mirror those in §4.7.

   301→
   302→### 4.10 Workflow — `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`
   303→
   304→Required new workflow steps (added after existing deploy steps):
   305→
   306→1. Create dual output root (`release-tree/` + `support-tree/`)
   307→2. Run late assembly into `release-tree/`
   308→3. Apply `release-inputs/release-include/`
   309→4. Apply `release-inputs/pages-include/` (for Pages target)
   310→5. Run gatekeeper validation on merged `release-tree/`
   311→6. Mint GitHub App installation token
   312→7. Push to `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io`
   313→8. Push to `corpus`
   314→9. Create/update dated release and ZIP on `corpus`
   315→
   316→Existing steps 1–5 (source-repo promotion, `gh-pages` mirror) remain unchanged
   317→per D13.
   318→
   319→### 4.11 Files not requiring changes
   320→
   321→These early-stage scripts do not need public-contract changes:
   322→
   323→- `01-clone-repos.py` — clones upstream, no public names
   324→- `02a` through `02h` — extractors write to `WORKDIR/L1-raw/`, internal only
   325→- `03-normalize-semantic.py` — writes to `WORKDIR/L2-semantic/`, internal only
   326→- `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` — enriches L2 frontmatter, no public names
   327→
   328→These scripts write into the existing `WORKDIR` which becomes part of
   329→`support-tree/`. They do not need renaming because their output paths are
   330→never public.
   331→
   332→---
   333→
   334→## 5. Phased Implementation With Rollback Gates
   335→
   336→Each phase has a go/no-go gate. If the gate fails, the previous phase's
   337→state is the safe rollback point. The pipeline continues to produce the
   338→current old-contract output until the phase that switches public output
   339→is explicitly activated.
   340→
   341→### Phase 0: Add shared configuration constants
   342→
   343→**Goal:** Centralize all public output names in `lib/config.py`.
   344→
   345→**Changes:**
   346→- Add constants for `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`, `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`,
   347→  `MODULE_MAP_FILENAME`, `MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME`,
   348→  `MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME`, `MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME`,
   349→  `RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR`, `PAGES_INCLUDE_DIR`
   350→
   351→**Gate:** All existing tests pass with no behavioral change. The new constants
   352→are defined but not yet consumed by any stage.
   353→
   354→**Rollback:** Revert the `lib/config.py` additions. Zero risk.
   355→
   356→---
   357→
   358→### Phase 1: Add release assembly stage (dual output, feature-flagged)
   359→
   360→**Goal:** Create a new late-stage script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` that
   361→reads from the existing pipeline output and writes the V5a `release-tree/`.
   362→
   363→**Changes:**
   364→- New script `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   365→  - Creates `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   366→  - Creates `SUPPORT_TREE_DIR`
   367→  - For each module:
   368→    - Copies/renames `{module}-complete-reference.md` → `bundled-reference.md`
   369→    - Copies/renames `{module}-skeleton.md` → `map.md`
   370→    - Copies L2 topic pages into `chunked-reference/`
   371→    - Copies optional `types/` files
   372→    - Generates module `llms.txt` with new paths
   373→  - Copies root files (`llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`,
   374→    `index.html`) with path rewrites
   375→  - Moves support-only artifacts to `support-tree/`
   376→- Feature flag: `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` env var (default `false`)
   377→- When flag is off, existing stages still produce old-contract output
   378→
   379→**Why a new script instead of modifying 05a:** This is the single most
   380→important risk-reduction decision. Script `05a` currently works. Inserting
   381→rename logic into it creates a change where a bug can break the existing
   382→pipeline. A new script that runs after `05a` and reads its output means:
   383→
   384→- `05a` continues to produce known-good old-contract files
   385→- `05e` produces the new contract files alongside them
   386→- Both can be validated before any consumer switches
   387→- If `05e` is broken, deleting it restores the old pipeline
   388→
   389→**Gate:** When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`:
   390→- `release-tree/` contains the full V5a layout
   391→- `support-tree/` contains L1, L2, telemetry, and manifests
   392→- All old-contract output still exists unchanged under `OUTDIR/`
   393→- All existing tests pass (they still check `OUTDIR/`)
   394→- A new parallel test suite validates `release-tree/`
   395→
   396→**Rollback:** Set `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false`. The script is skipped. Old
   397→output is unchanged.
   398→
   399→---
   400→
   401→### Phase 2: Add release-tree validation
   402→
   403→**Goal:** Create new tests and a validator that assert the V5a contract on
   404→`release-tree/`.
   405→
   406→**Changes:**
   407→- New test file:
   408→  `tests/pytest/pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py`
   409→  - Asserts every module has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`,
   410→    `chunked-reference/`
   411→  - Asserts no `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere
   412→    in `release-tree/`
   413→  - Asserts bundled-vs-chunked parity for at least one module
   414→  - Asserts `release-tree/` contains no `support-tree/` items
   415→- Extend `08-validate-output.py` with a `release-tree` validation mode
   416→  (behind feature flag)
   417→
   418→**Gate:** All new tests pass against the `release-tree/` produced by Phase 1.
   419→All existing tests still pass against old `OUTDIR/` output.
   420→
   421→**Rollback:** Delete the new test file and validation mode. Zero risk.
   422→
   423→---
   424→
   425→### Phase 3: Add release-include merge
   426→
   427→**Goal:** Implement the overlay merge from `release-inputs/release-include/`.
   428→
   429→**Changes:**
   430→- Create `release-inputs/release-include/` in the source repo (initially
   431→  empty or with a `README.md` explaining its purpose)
   432→- Create `release-inputs/pages-include/.nojekyll`
   433→- Create `release-inputs/release-repo-include/` (empty, reserved)
   434→- Add merge logic to `05e-assemble-release-tree.py`:
   435→  after generating `release-tree/`, copy `release-include/` contents on top
   436→- Add merge-precedence test
   437→
   438→**Gate:** Include merge produces expected results. A test file placed in
   439→`release-include/` appears in the merged `release-tree/`. A generated file
   440→replaced by an include-override contains the override content. Existing tests
   441→pass.
   442→
   443→**Rollback:** Remove the merge logic from `05e`. The `release-include/`
   444→directory stays inert.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→### Phase 4: Switch late stages to write into release-tree
   449→
   450→**Goal:** Modify scripts `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` to consume
   451→`RELEASE_TREE_DIR` when the feature flag is on.
   452→
   453→**Changes:**
   454→- `05a`: output paths use config constants; filenames use new names
   455→- `05b`: text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   456→- `06`: routing text references updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   457→- `07`: path labels and links updated; output to `RELEASE_TREE_DIR`
   458→- `08`: validation logic updated per §4.6
   459→- Script `05e` is retired (its functionality is absorbed into the modified
   460→  stages)
   461→
   462→This is the riskiest phase because it modifies working scripts.
   463→
   464→**Risk mitigation:**
   465→- Feature flag controls whether stages write to old or new paths
   466→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false` proves old contract
   467→  is intact
   468→- A full smoke test run with `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` proves new contract
   469→  works
   470→- Only after both pass green does the flag default change
   471→
   472→**Gate:** Full smoke tests pass in both flag states. The `release-tree/`
   473→output matches what Phase 1's `05e` produced. The old `OUTDIR/` output matches
   474→what the pipeline produced before Phase 4.
   475→
   476→**Rollback:** Reset feature flag to `false`. All stages revert to old output
   477→paths.
   478→
   479→---
   480→
   481→### Phase 5: Update test suite to new contract
   482→
   483→**Goal:** Modify `smoke_pipeline_support.py`, `pytest_02`, and `pytest_06`
   484→to assert the new contract as the primary contract.
   485→
   486→**Changes:**
   487→- Update all assertions per §4.7, §4.8, §4.9
   488→- Old-contract assertions are removed
   489→- Feature flag default changes to `true`
   490→
   491→**Gate:** Full test suite passes with the new contract as primary. Manual
   492→inspection of `release-tree/` output confirms correctness.
   493→
   494→**Rollback:** Restore old assertions and set flag to `false`.
   495→
   496→---
   497→
   498→### Phase 6: Provision public infrastructure and deploy
   499→
   500→**Goal:** Set up the external GitHub organization, repos, and deploy workflow.
   501→
   502→**Changes:**
   503→1. Create `openwrt-docs4ai` GitHub organization
   504→2. Create `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` repo (Pages)
   505→3. Create `corpus` repo (Releases)
   506→4. Create and install GitHub App
   507→5. Store secrets in source repo
   508→6. Add deploy workflow steps per §4.10
   509→7. First live publish
   510→
   511→**Gate:** Live Pages site serves direct-root content. Release repo has a
   512→fresh commit. ZIP expands into `openwrt-docs4ai/` with the V5a layout.
   513→No `openwrt-condensed-docs`, `L1-raw`, or `L2-semantic` visible anywhere.
   514→
   515→**Rollback:** Disable the new workflow steps. Source-repo deploy continues
   516→unchanged.
   517→
   518→---
   519→
   520→### Phase 7: Remove old contract and feature flag
   521→
   522→**Goal:** Clean up the codebase after live validation.
   523→
   524→**Changes:**
   525→- Remove `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` feature flag
   526→- Remove old-contract code paths
   527→- Retire script `05e` if not already retired in Phase 4
   528→- Update `DEVELOPMENT.md` and architecture docs
   529→
   530→**Gate:** Full test suite, CI pipeline, and live deploy all green without the
   531→old contract code paths.
   532→
   533→**Rollback:** Restore the feature flag and old paths. This is the only phase
   534→with a moderate rollback cost, which is why it is last.
   535→
   536→---
   537→
   538→## 6. Rework Summary By Risk Level
   539→
   540→| Risk | Phase | Files affected | What can go wrong | Mitigation |
   541→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
   542→| **None** | 0 | `lib/config.py` | Nothing; additive only | Constants unused until Phase 1 |
   543→| **Low** | 1 | New `05e` script | New script bugs produce bad `release-tree/` | Does not touch existing output; feature-flagged off |
   544→| **Low** | 2 | New test file, `08` extension | Bad test logic gives false confidence | Tests run against Phase 1 output; manual cross-check |
   545→| **Low** | 3 | `release-inputs/`, `05e` merge logic | Include merge overwrites wrong file | Merge is additive; test asserts exact behavior |
   546→| **High** | 4 | `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08` | Bug in modified script breaks existing pipeline | Feature flag; dual smoke test; rollback to flag=false |
   547→| **Medium** | 5 | Test support, pytest files | Wrong assertions pass bad output | Diff old vs new assertions for parity |
   548→| **Medium** | 6 | Workflow YAML, external infra | Deploy fails or publishes wrong tree | Deploy is additive after existing deploy; gatekeeper checks |
   549→| **Low** | 7 | Cleanup only | Premature removal of fallback | Only after live validation |
   550→
   551→---
   552→
   553→## 7. Final Release Tree Contract
   554→
   555→When all phases are complete, the published product tree looks like this:
   556→
   557→```text
   558→(repository root or ZIP root openwrt-docs4ai/)
   559→├── README.md
   560→├── AGENTS.md
   561→├── llms.txt
   562→├── llms-full.txt
   563→├── index.html
   564→├── luci/
   565→│   ├── llms.txt
   566→│   ├── map.md
   567→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   568→│   └── chunked-reference/
   569→│       ├── javascript-api-cbi.md
   570→│       └── ...
   571→├── luci-examples/
   572→│   ├── llms.txt
   573→│   ├── map.md
   574→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   575→│   └── chunked-reference/
   576→│       └── ...
   577→├── openwrt-core/
   578→│   └── ...
   579→├── openwrt-hotplug/
   580→│   └── ...
   581→├── procd/
   582→│   └── ...
   583→├── uci/
   584→│   └── ...
   585→├── ucode/
   586→│   ├── llms.txt
   587→│   ├── map.md
   588→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   589→│   ├── chunked-reference/
   590→│   │   └── ...
   591→│   └── types/
   592→│       └── ucode.d.ts
   593→└── wiki/
   594→    └── ...
   595→```
   596→
   597→### 7.1 Items guaranteed absent from the public tree
   598→
   599→- `L1-raw/`
   600→- `L2-semantic/`
   601→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
   602→- `corpus/`
   603→- `*-skeleton.md`
   604→- `*-complete-reference.md`
   605→- `cross-link-registry.json`
   606→- `repo-manifest.json`
   607→- `signature-inventory.json`
   608→- `changelog.json`
   609→- `CHANGES.md`
   610→- Any `.meta.json` sidecar
   611→
   612→---
   613→
   614→## 8. Delivery Surfaces
   615→
   616→| Surface | Root | Source |
   617→| --- | --- | --- |
   618→| Pages site | `https://openwrt-docs4ai.github.io/` | Validated `release-tree/` + `pages-include/` |
   619→| Release repo | `https://github.com/openwrt-docs4ai/corpus` | Validated `release-tree/` + `release-repo-include/` |
   620→| ZIP download | `openwrt-docs4ai-YYYY-MM-DD.zip` → `openwrt-docs4ai/` | Validated `release-tree/` (no target-specific overlays) |
   621→
   622→All three surfaces share the same validated `release-tree/` as their content
   623→source. Target-specific overlays (`.nojekyll` for Pages) are applied after.
   624→
   625→---
   626→
   627→## 9. Gatekeeper Rules
   628→
   629→Before any cross-repo publish, the pipeline must assert:
   630→
   631→| Check | Assertion |
   632→| --- | --- |
   633→| Root router exists | `release-tree/llms.txt` exists and is > 512 bytes |
   634→| Root index exists | `release-tree/index.html` exists |
   635→| Module count | At least 4 module directories exist |
   636→| No legacy leakage | No `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere in `release-tree/` |
   637→| Fixed schema | Every module dir has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/` |
   638→| Chunked non-empty | Every module's `chunked-reference/` has at least one `.md` file |
   639→
   640→Failure of any check blocks publish and fails the job visibly.
   641→
   642→---
   643→
   644→## 10. Document Supersession
   645→
   646→| Document | Status after V5a |
   647→| --- | --- |

# ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE Feature Flag Contract

## Purpose

This document defines the `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` environment variable that controls the transition from the current output contract to the V5a release-tree contract. It is introduced in Phase 0 of the V5a refactor and removed in Phase 7 after live validation of the new contract.

The authoritative implementation plan is `docs/plans/v12/public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`.

---

## Flag Definition

| Property | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Name | `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` |
| Type | Environment variable |
| Values | `true` / `false` (string, case-insensitive) |
| Default | `false` |
| Scope | Late-stage pipeline scripts: `05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08`, and `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` |

The default of `false` preserves the current output contract until the flag is explicitly activated.

---

## Behavior When `false` (Default)

- All pipeline stages produce output using the current contract.
- Output goes to `OUTDIR` (`openwrt-condensed-docs/`) with current filenames.
- Script `05e` is skipped entirely.
- All existing tests validate the current contract.
- No `release-tree/` or `support-tree/` directories are created.
- This is the safe rollback state for any failed phase.

---

## Behavior When `true`

The behavior changes by phase.

**Phase 1–3:** Script `05e` runs after existing stages and produces `release-tree/` alongside unchanged current output. Old output still exists under `OUTDIR/`.

**Phase 4:** Late stages (`05a`, `05b`, `06`, `07`, `08`) write into `release-tree/` instead of `OUTDIR/`. Script `05e` is retired.

**Phase 5:** Test suite switches to assert the new contract as primary. The flag default changes to `true`.

**Phase 6:** Deploy workflow publishes from `release-tree/`.

---

## Test Expectations Per Flag State

**`false`:** All existing tests pass. Release-tree tests either do not exist yet or skip gracefully if `release-tree/` is absent.

**`true` (Phase 1–3):** Existing tests still pass against `OUTDIR/`. New release-tree tests (`tests/pytest/pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py`) also pass against `release-tree/`.

**`true` (Phase 4+):** Old-contract assertions are removed. Tests validate `release-tree/` as primary.

---

## Flag Lifecycle

| Event | Phase |
| --- | --- |
| Introduced | Phase 0 |
| Default changes to `true` | Phase 5 |
| Removed | Phase 7 |

---

## Interaction With Other Variables

**`OUTDIR`:** Unchanged. Early stages (`01` through `04`) always write to `OUTDIR`. The flag controls only where late stages direct their final output.

**`WORKDIR`:** Unchanged. The scratch area under `tmp/` is unaffected by the flag.

**`VALIDATE_MODE`:** Unchanged. Validation severity is orthogonal to which contract is being checked. Both flag states respect `VALIDATE_MODE`.

```

# Release Tree Contract (V5a)

## Purpose

This document defines the published release-tree contract for the openwrt-docs4ai output product. It is the authoritative reference for the public output layout, replacing scattered descriptions across plan documents V1–V5a.

The release-tree is direct-root — no `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper. Modules sit at root. This contract is derived from V5a (`docs/plans/v12/public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`), specifically §3.1 resolution, §3.2, §3.4, §3.5, §7, §7.1, §8, and §9.

---

## Release Tree Layout

\```text
(repository root or ZIP root openwrt-docs4ai/)
├── README.md
├── AGENTS.md
├── llms.txt
├── llms-full.txt
├── index.html
├── luci/
│   ├── llms.txt
│   ├── map.md
│   ├── bundled-reference.md
│   └── chunked-reference/
│       ├── javascript-api-cbi.md
│       └── ...
├── luci-examples/
│   ├── llms.txt
│   ├── map.md
│   ├── bundled-reference.md
│   └── chunked-reference/
│       └── ...
├── openwrt-core/
│   └── ...
├── openwrt-hotplug/
│   └── ...
├── procd/
│   └── ...
├── uci/
│   └── ...
├── ucode/
│   ├── llms.txt
│   ├── map.md
│   ├── bundled-reference.md
│   ├── chunked-reference/
│   │   └── ...
│   └── types/
│       └── ucode.d.ts
└── wiki/
    └── ...
\```

---

## Module Schema

Every module folder contains the following files:

| File / Directory | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `llms.txt` | Per-module LLM routing index. Lists the module's chunked topics and key references. |
| `map.md` | Navigation map (semantic skeleton). Provides a structured outline of the module with cross-links to chunked topics. |
| `bundled-reference.md` | Complete bundled reference for the module. For oversized modules, becomes a sharding index (see Oversized Module Sharding). |
| `chunked-reference/` | Directory of per-topic Markdown files derived from L2 semantic pages. Always present. |
| `chunked-reference/{topic}.md` | Individual topic file. One file per semantic page from the L2 layer. |
| `types/` | Optional. Present only for modules that export TypeScript IDE schemas. |
| `types/{module}.d.ts` | TypeScript declaration file for IDE schema support. |

---

## Oversized Module Sharding

When a module exceeds the token limit for a single file:

- `bundled-reference.md` remains the stable, fixed index filename.
- `bundled-reference.md` becomes an index document that points to `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` files.
- Part files sit alongside `bundled-reference.md` in the module root — not inside `chunked-reference/`.
- `chunked-reference/` topic files are always present regardless of sharding status.

Example layout for an oversized module:

\```text
ucode/
├── bundled-reference.md          ← index, points to parts
├── bundled-reference.part-01.md
├── bundled-reference.part-02.md
├── chunked-reference/
│   └── ...
├── llms.txt
├── map.md
└── types/
    └── ucode.d.ts
\```

---

## Guaranteed Absent Items

The following items must never appear anywhere in the published release-tree:

- `L1-raw/`
- `L2-semantic/`
- `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
- `corpus/`
- `*-skeleton.md`
- `*-complete-reference.md`
- `cross-link-registry.json`
- `repo-manifest.json`
- `signature-inventory.json`
- `changelog.json`
- `CHANGES.md`
- Any `.meta.json` sidecar

---

## Root Files

The release-tree root contains exactly these fixed files:

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `README.md` | Human-readable introduction to the published corpus. |
| `AGENTS.md` | Agent navigation guide listing all modules and their entry points. |
| `llms.txt` | Root LLM routing index. Entry point for LLM-driven navigation across all modules. |
| `llms-full.txt` | Full expanded routing index with all topic links. |
| `index.html` | Web landing page served by the Pages site. |

---

## Support Tree

`support-tree/` is the internal pipeline staging area. It is ephemeral during CI runs and is not a persistent on-disk contract. Its internal layout may change without notice.

When present, it contains:

| Path | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| `support-tree/raw/` | L1-raw output (normalized source extracts and `.meta.json` sidecars). |
| `support-tree/semantic-pages/` | L2-semantic output (enriched Markdown with YAML frontmatter). |
| `support-tree/telemetry/` | `changelog.json`, `CHANGES.md`, `signature-inventory.json`. |
| `support-tree/manifests/` | `cross-link-registry.json`, `repo-manifest.json`. |

`support-tree/` may be uploaded as a CI artifact for debugging. It is never published to any public surface.

---

## Delivery Surfaces

All three delivery surfaces share the same validated release-tree as their content source. Target-specific overlays are applied after validation, before publish.

| Surface | Root URL / Path | Content source |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Pages site | `https://openwrt-docs4ai.github.io/` | Validated release-tree + `pages-include/` overlay |
| Release repo | `https://github.com/openwrt-docs4ai/corpus` | Validated release-tree + `release-repo-include/` overlay |
| ZIP download | `openwrt-docs4ai-YYYY-MM-DD.zip` → `openwrt-docs4ai/` | Validated release-tree (no target-specific overlays) |

---

## Gatekeeper Rules

All six checks must pass before any cross-repo publish. Failure of any check blocks publish and fails the job visibly.

| Check | Assertion |
| --- | --- |
| Root router exists | `release-tree/llms.txt` exists and is > 512 bytes |
| Root index exists | `release-tree/index.html` exists |
| Module count | At least 4 module directories exist |
| No legacy leakage | No `L1-raw/`, `L2-semantic/`, or `openwrt-condensed-docs` anywhere in `release-tree/` |
| Fixed schema | Every module directory has `llms.txt`, `map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/` |
| Chunked non-empty | Every module's `chunked-reference/` has at least one `.md` file |

---

## Release Include Overlays

Source-controlled overlay directories are merged into the release-tree after generation, before validation.

\```text
release-inputs/
├── release-include/          (common overlay, applied to all surfaces)
├── pages-include/            (Pages-only overlay, e.g. .nojekyll)
└── release-repo-include/    (release-repo-only overlay, reserved)
\```

- `release-inputs/release-include/` contents are applied to all delivery surfaces.
- `release-inputs/pages-include/` contents are applied only to the Pages surface (e.g. `.nojekyll`).
- `release-inputs/release-repo-include/` is reserved for future use.
- Include files take precedence over generated files of the same name.

---

## Name Mapping From Old Contract

Quick reference for names that changed between the pre-V5a contract and the V5a contract:

| Old name | New name |
| --- | --- |
| `{module}-skeleton.md` | `map.md` |
| `{module}-complete-reference.md` | `bundled-reference.md` |
| `{module}-complete-reference.part-{NN}.md` | `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` |
| `L2-semantic/{module}/` | `{module}/chunked-reference/` |
| `openwrt-condensed-docs/` | `release-tree/` (internal pipeline) / direct-root (public) |
| `# {module} Complete Reference` (H1) | `# {module} Bundled Reference` |
| `# {module} (Skeleton Semantic Map)` (H1) | `# {module} Navigation Map` |

---

## Relationship to Other Documents

- **Source:** This document is derived from V5a (`docs/plans/v12/public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`). For implementation phasing and rollback gates, see V5a §5.
- **Pre-V5a contract:** For the current pre-V5a schema in active use, see [`schema-definitions.md`](schema-definitions.md). That document will be updated as implementation phases complete.
- **Rework map:** For the per-file change map (exact strings, line ranges, affected scripts), see V5a §4.
- **Stage dependency:** For pipeline execution order, see [`execution-map.md`](execution-map.md).

```
