   130→- `{module}/types/{module}.d.ts` — IDE schema, moved into per-module `types/` subdirectory
   131→
   132→### L3 LLM Routing Contract
   133→
   134→The generated corpus is both a published documentation product and a navigation surface for tools and models. Its LLM-facing outputs must therefore be deterministic, link-safe, and self-describing.
   135→
   136→#### Root `llms.txt`
   137→
   138→Required characteristics:
   139→
   140→- single H1 title line for the routing index
   141→- introductory blockquote lines that point to `llms-full.txt`, state the version banner, and state the total underlying L2 token count
   142→- one or more H2 category sections
   143→- category bullet lines that point only to module indexes
   144→- bullet format:
   145→
   146→```text
   147→- [<module>](./<module>/llms.txt): <short description> (~<module_l2_tokens> tokens)
   148→```
   149→
   150→Rules:
   151→
   152→- root `llms.txt` is a routing tree, not the exhaustive catalog
   153→- the displayed token count represents underlying L2 content for the module rather than duplicated L3 or L4 helper files
   154→- descriptions should prefer `ai_summary`, then `description`, then a first-sentence body fallback
   155→- placeholder descriptions such as `No description` are not valid output
   156→
   157→#### Root `llms-full.txt`
   158→
   159→Required characteristics:
   160→
   161→- single H1 title line for the flat catalog
   162→- optional introductory blockquote lines are allowed
   163→- flat bullet list sorted by relative path
   164→- catalog entries must include all generated AI-facing helper files and all L2 markdown documents that belong to the published corpus
   165→- required catalog coverage (current, pre-V5a):
   166→  - `AGENTS.md`
   167→  - generated `README.md`
   168→  - each module `llms.txt`
   169→  - each `*-skeleton.md`
   170→  - each `*-complete-reference.md`
   171→  - each `*-complete-reference.part-*.md` when sharding is present
   172→  - each published `.d.ts`
   173→  - every `L2-semantic/{module}/*.md`
   174→- required catalog coverage (V5a):
   175→  - `AGENTS.md`
   176→  - generated `README.md`
   177→  - each module `llms.txt`
   178→  - each `{module}/map.md`
   179→  - each `{module}/bundled-reference.md`
   180→  - each `{module}/bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` when sharding is present
   181→  - each published `{module}/types/{module}.d.ts`
   182→  - every `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md` (replaces `L2-semantic/{module}/*.md`)
   183→- bullet format:
   184→
   185→```text
   186→- [<relative-path>](./<relative-path>): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, <kind>)
   187→```
   188→
   189→Typical `kind` values include `l2-source`, `l3-agent-guide`, `l3-generated-readme`, `l3-module-index`, `l3-skeleton`, `l3-ide-schema`, `l4-monolith`, and `l4-monolith-part`.
   190→
   191→(V5a) `l3-skeleton` entries reference `map.md`; `l4-monolith` entries reference `bundled-reference.md`; `l2-source` entries reference `{module}/chunked-reference/{topic}.md`.
   192→
   193→#### Module `llms.txt`
   194→
   195→Required characteristics:
   196→
   197→- H1 title in the form `# <module> module`
   198→- introductory blockquote lines for module description and total underlying L2 token count
   199→- deterministic H2 section order
   200→- allowed sections:
   201→  - `Recommended Entry Points`
   202→  - `Tooling Surfaces`
   203→  - `Source Documents`
   204→- empty sections must be omitted
   205→
   206→Preferred section semantics:
   207→
   208→- `Recommended Entry Points` lists the module navigation map (or skeleton), the stable bundled reference (or complete-reference) index, and any sharded part files when present
   209→- `Tooling Surfaces` lists generated `.d.ts` or other IDE-oriented helper files when present
   210→- `Source Documents` lists each L2 document for the module
   211→
   212→Bullet formats (current, pre-V5a):
   213→
   214→```text
   215→- [<module>-skeleton.md](./<module>-skeleton.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-skeleton)
   216→- [<module>-complete-reference.md](./<module>-complete-reference.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith)
   217→- [<module>-complete-reference.part-01.md](./<module>-complete-reference.part-01.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith-part)
   218→- [<module>.d.ts](./<module>.d.ts): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-ide-schema)
   219→- [<filename>.md](../L2-semantic/<module>/<filename>.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l2-source)
   220→```
   221→
   222→Bullet formats (V5a):
   223→
   224→```text
   225→- [map.md](./map.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-skeleton)
   226→- [bundled-reference.md](./bundled-reference.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith)
   227→- [bundled-reference.part-01.md](./bundled-reference.part-01.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l4-monolith-part)
   228→- [<module>.d.ts](./types/<module>.d.ts): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l3-ide-schema)
   229→- [<filename>.md](./chunked-reference/<filename>.md): <short description> (~<tokens> tokens, l2-source)
   230→```
   231→
   232→Note (V5a): module names are not repeated in child filenames inside the module folder. `map.md` and `bundled-reference.md` are fixed filenames, not module-prefixed. Source document links resolve relative to the module folder via `chunked-reference/` rather than `../L2-semantic/{module}/`.
   233→
   234→#### `AGENTS.md`
   235→
   236→`AGENTS.md` must remain consistent with the routing contract above.
   237→
   238→Required guidance points:
   239→
   240→- begin at root `llms.txt`
   241→- prefer module `llms.txt` once the target subsystem is known
   242→- (current, pre-V5a) prefer `*-skeleton.md` before complete-reference indexes or part files when context is tight
   243→- (V5a) prefer `map.md` before `bundled-reference.md` or part files when context is tight; `map.md` is the stable per-module navigation entry point
   244→- treat generated module indexes, navigation maps (or skeletons), bundled references (or complete-reference indexes), part files, and `.d.ts` files as published navigation surfaces
   245→- (V5a) `bundled-reference.md` and `map.md` are fixed filenames inside each module folder; they are not module-prefixed
   246→- avoid implying that a separate source-repo root `llms.txt` already exists
   247→
   248→### L4
   249→
   250→- Location (current, pre-V5a): `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/`
   251→- Location (V5a): `release-tree/{module}/`
   252→- Format (current, pre-V5a): one stable `{module}-complete-reference.md` per module plus optional sharded `{module}-complete-reference.part-*.md` files for oversized modules
   253→- Format (V5a): one stable `bundled-reference.md` per module plus optional sharded `bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md` files for oversized modules; sharded parts sit alongside `bundled-reference.md`, not inside `chunked-reference/`
   254→- Rule: one top-level YAML block per generated L4 file, not repeated L2 frontmatter sections
   255→
   256→### L5
   257→
   258→- Location (current, pre-V5a): `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
   259→- Location (V5a): `support-tree/telemetry/` — not published to any public surface
   260→- Outputs:
   261→  - `CHANGES.md`
   262→  - `changelog.json`
   263→  - `signature-inventory.json`
   264→
   265→## V5a Output Topology
   266→
   267→(V5a) The pipeline main output root contains two distinct subtrees. Only `release-tree/` is publishable. `support-tree/` is ephemeral CI state and must never appear in any public surface.
   268→
   269→### `release-tree/` — publishable output, direct-root layout
   270→
   271→The `release-tree/` subtree is the complete public product. It uses a direct-root layout where every module folder sits at root with no wrapper directory. The `release-tree/` root itself becomes the repository root for GitHub Pages and the ZIP expansion root.
   272→
   273→```text
   274→release-tree/
   275→├── README.md
   276→├── AGENTS.md
   277→├── llms.txt
   278→├── llms-full.txt
   279→├── index.html
   280→├── {module}/
   281→│   ├── llms.txt
   282→│   ├── map.md
   283→│   ├── bundled-reference.md
   284→│   ├── chunked-reference/
   285→│   │   └── {topic}.md ...
   286→│   └── types/           (optional)
   287→│       └── {module}.d.ts
   288→└── ... (more modules)
   289→```
   290→
   291→Items guaranteed absent from `release-tree/`:
   292→
   293→- `L1-raw/`
   294→- `L2-semantic/`
   295→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` (any reference or path segment)
   296→- `*-skeleton.md`
   297→- `*-complete-reference.md`
   298→- Any `.meta.json` sidecar
   299→- `CHANGES.md`, `changelog.json`, `signature-inventory.json`, `repo-manifest.json`, `cross-link-registry.json`
   300→
   301→### `support-tree/` — ephemeral CI support artifacts
   302→
   303→`support-tree/` holds all artifacts that are useful for debugging and CI inspection but are intentionally excluded from the public product. Its internal layout may change without notice and is not a stable contract.
   304→
   305→Typical contents:
   306→
   307→| Path | Former location (pre-V5a) |
   308→| --- | --- |
   309→| `support-tree/raw/` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/` |
   310→| `support-tree/semantic-pages/` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/` |
   311→| `support-tree/telemetry/CHANGES.md` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/CHANGES.md` |
   312→| `support-tree/telemetry/changelog.json` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/changelog.json` |
   313→| `support-tree/telemetry/signature-inventory.json` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/signature-inventory.json` |
   314→| `support-tree/manifests/repo-manifest.json` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/repo-manifest.json` |
   315→| `support-tree/manifests/cross-link-registry.json` | `openwrt-condensed-docs/cross-link-registry.json` |
   316→
   317→For the full V5a layout contract and phased implementation plan, see [release-tree-contract.md](release-tree-contract.md) and the source plan at `docs/plans/v12/public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md`.
   318→
   319→## Enumerated Origin Types
   320→
   321→- `c_source`
   322→- `js_source`
   323→- `wiki_page`
   324→- `makefile_meta`
   325→- `readme`
   326→- `uci_schema`
   327→- `hotplug_event`
   328→- `example_app`
   329→- `header_api`
   330→
   331→## Stakeholder Mapping
   332→
   333→| Stakeholder | Primary layers |
   334→| --- | --- |
   335→| Raw analyst | L1, L2 |
   336→| Context injector | L2 |
   337→| Human developer | L3, L4 |
   338→| IDE tooling | L3 |
   339→| Drift and telemetry tooling | L5 |
   340→| Pipeline maintainer | all layers plus local logs |
   341→
```

```text
     1→# openwrt-docs4ai Architecture
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This repository is a documentation production pipeline, not an application runtime. Its job is to collect OpenWrt documentation from multiple upstream sources, normalize it into stable intermediate layers, and publish compact outputs that are useful to humans, IDE tooling, and LLM workflows.
     6→
     7→The primary engineering goal for the current stage is maintainable correctness on Windows with verified remote publication behavior. Local validation remains the first gate, and GitHub Actions is now a confirmed verification and deployment layer rather than an unproven future target.
     8→
     9→The repository also has a dual-role documentation boundary: maintainer guidance lives in the source tree under `docs/`, while the generated AI-facing navigation surface lives under `openwrt-condensed-docs/`. The generated-corpus routing contract is authoritative for `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, module `llms.txt`, and `AGENTS.md`; a source-repo root `llms.txt` remains explicitly deferred. The project is transitioning to a V5a release-tree contract that separates the publishable output (`release-tree/`) from internal pipeline artifacts, and renames public-facing files to use generic names (`map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`) instead of module-prefixed names.
    10→
    11→## Repository Zones
    12→
    13→| Path | Role | Notes |
    14→| --- | --- | --- |
    15→| `.github/scripts/` | Numbered pipeline scripts | `00` is workflow orchestration. Whole numbers denote stage boundaries, and letter suffixes denote sibling scripts within a stage family. A bare stage id cannot coexist with same-family lettered siblings. |
    16→| `.github/workflows/` | GitHub Actions workflows | Remote execution only. Current stabilization work does not assume GitHub behavior is already verified. |
    17→| `lib/` | Shared Python support code | Shared config, file writing, extraction helpers, and other reusable logic. |
    18→| `tools/` | Non-numbered maintainer support tools | Local-only operator CLIs and helpers that are not part of the numbered hosted pipeline surface. |
    19→| `tests/` | Local deterministic tests and smoke runners | Local verification is required before remote workflow testing. |
    20→| `docs/specs/v12/` | Active v12 technical specifications | These are the current engineering references. |
    21→| `docs/archive/v12/` | Archived planning and review material | Historical context only. Not authoritative for implementation. |
    22→| `openwrt-condensed-docs/` | Published output root | Stable output tree for generated artifacts and local inspection. |
    23→| `tmp/` | Ephemeral working area | Never authoritative. Safe to delete between runs. |
    24→| `templates/` | Static content templates | Only keep templates that are actually consumed by the live pipeline. |
    25→| `release-tree/` | V5a publishable output root | Generated when `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`; direct-root layout with no `openwrt-condensed-docs/` wrapper. |
    26→| `support-tree/` | V5a ephemeral CI support artifacts | Not persistent; may be uploaded as CI artifact. |
    27→| `release-inputs/` | Source-controlled overlay directories | `release-include/` (common), `pages-include/` (Pages-specific), `release-repo-include/` (reserved). |
    28→
    29→## Layer Model
    30→
    31→| Layer | Meaning | Primary Location | Persistence |
    32→| --- | --- | --- | --- |
    33→| `L0` | Untouched upstream source inputs | `tmp/repo-*` | Ephemeral |
    34→| `L1` | Raw normalized markdown plus sidecar metadata | `tmp/L1-raw/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/` for inspection | Generated |
    35→| `L2` | Semantic markdown with YAML frontmatter and cross-links | `tmp/L2-semantic/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/` for inspection | Generated |
    36→| `L3` | Navigational and operational outputs | `openwrt-condensed-docs/` and module subdirectories | Published |
    37→| `L4` | Complete-reference index files plus optional sharded parts | `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/` | Published |
    38→| `L5` | Telemetry and drift outputs | `openwrt-condensed-docs/` | Published |
    39→| `L3/L4 (V5a)` | Under V5a, L3/L4 output moves from `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/` to `release-tree/{module}/` with renamed files (`map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`). | `release-tree/{module}/` | Generated when `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` |
    40→
    41→## Naming Conventions
    42→
    43→### Script numbering
    44→
    45→- `00` denotes orchestration or workflow entry points.
    46→- Whole numbers denote dependency boundaries and high-level stage order.
    47→- A letter suffix such as `05a` or `05d` denotes sibling scripts inside the same stage family.
    48→- A bare stage id such as `04` cannot coexist with `04a`, `04b`, or other same-family siblings.
    49→- Non-pipeline helpers must live outside the numbered stage surface, typically under `tools/`.
    50→- Stage-family siblings may run sequentially locally and may be parallelized in deployment when their direct dependencies are satisfied.
    51→- Local smoke tests may still run all scripts sequentially for simplicity and debuggability.
    52→
    53→### Directory names
    54→
    55→- Use `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic` without leading dots.
    56→- Avoid hidden directory naming for active pipeline outputs because the current project targets Windows development and public GitHub use.
    57→- Use lowercase, hyphenated filenames for human-authored specs in `docs/specs/v12/`.
    58→
    59→### Output philosophy
    60→
    61→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the stable output root for generated documentation.
    62→- `docs/` is for maintainers and project documentation only.
    63→- `tmp/` is scratch space and must never be treated as durable state.
    64→- Under V5a, `release-tree/` becomes the publishable output root. `openwrt-condensed-docs/` remains as the internal pipeline working directory but the name never appears in any public surface.
    65→
    66→## Execution Contract
    67→
    68→1. `01` prepares local source inputs and manifests.
    69→2. `02a` is an independent wiki extractor and can run in parallel with `01` in hosted workflow execution.
    70→3. `02b` through `02h` extract source-specific content into L1 and remain gated on `01` because they consume cloned repositories.
    71→4. `03` normalizes L1 into L2 and promotes stable intermediates into the output tree.
    72→5. `04` optionally enriches staged L2 files with AI summary metadata and performs its own AI-store preflight.
    73→6. `05a` assembles the publishable skeletons and complete-reference outputs, sharding oversized modules into smaller part files while preserving the stable index filename.
    74→6a. `05e` (V5a, feature-flagged) assembles the validated `release-tree/` from existing pipeline output, renaming files to the new contract (`map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`).
    75→7. `05b`, `05c`, and `05d` generate companion publication artifacts from the stabilized post-`03` snapshot.
    76→8. `06` generates routing indexes after `05a` has produced the publishable reference assets.
    77→9. `07` generates the HTML landing page directly from the staged publish tree, grouping files by top-level product area instead of deriving its structure from `llms.txt`.
    78→10. `08` validates the entire output tree.
    79→
    80→Current Option B hardening adds per-extractor status manifests, disables matrix fail-fast for repo-backed extractors, and emits extract plus pipeline summary artifacts for faster triage.
    81→
    82→## Local-First Verification Model
    83→
    84→- The required first-stage verification path is local and sequential.
    85→- Deterministic fixture tests are the core protection against accidental regressions.
    86→- Local smoke tests should isolate `WORKDIR` and `OUTDIR` so the repository is not corrupted during development.
    87→- GitHub Actions remains a second-stage verification target after local proof, and it is now also the normal publication path for generated outputs.
    88→
    89→## Remote Promotion Contract
    90→
    91→- The `process` job builds generated artifacts into `staging/` (`OUTDIR`) and uploads that tree as `final-staging`.
    92→- The `deploy` job promotes `final-staging` into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` with `rsync -a --delete`.
    93→- Generated-output commits use the `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD` format and are written by the GitHub Actions bot only when the staged tree changed.
    94→- The `deploy` job then mirrors `openwrt-condensed-docs/` into the dedicated `gh-pages` branch and writes `.nojekyll` there so GitHub Pages serves the same packaged tree, including `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic`, without Jekyll rewriting.
    95→
    96→Under V5a, deployment additionally publishes the validated `release-tree/` to external targets: `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` (GitHub Pages) and the `corpus` release repository. The existing source-repo deploy behavior remains intact regardless of V5a deployment status (locked decision D13).
    97→
    98→## Active Documents
    99→
   100→- `README.md`: short external overview.
   101→- `DEVELOPMENT.md`: maintainer quick start and local workflow reference.
   102→- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: durable architecture and naming contract.
   103→- `docs/specs/v12/`: active v12 specifications, current status, execution map, active bug log, and stabilization plan.
   104→- `docs/specs/v12/release-tree-contract.md`: V5a public output contract.
   105→- `docs/specs/v12/feature-flag-contract.md`: V5a feature flag semantics.
   106→
   107→## Archive Policy
   108→
   109→Documents under `docs/archive/v12/` remain useful as historical design context, but they do not control implementation. If an archived document conflicts with an active spec or with verified code behavior, the active spec wins.
```

     1→# openwrt-docs4ai v12 Execution Map
     2→
     3→## Transition Note
     4→
     5→This document is being updated for the V5a release-tree contract. When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`, late-stage scripts produce output into `release-tree/` instead of directly into `OUTDIR`. See [feature-flag-contract.md](../specs/v12/feature-flag-contract.md) and [release-tree-contract.md](release-tree-contract.md) for details.
     6→
     7→## Layer Locations
     8→
     9→- `L0`: `tmp/repo-*`
    10→- `L1`: `tmp/L1-raw/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L1-raw/{module}/`
    11→- `L2`: `tmp/L2-semantic/{module}/` during build, optionally copied to `openwrt-condensed-docs/L2-semantic/{module}/`
    12→- `L3`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/` root and per-module subdirectories
    13→- `L4`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/`
    14→- `L5`: `openwrt-condensed-docs/`
    15→- `release-tree/`: validated publishable output (V5a, when `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`)
    16→- `support-tree/`: ephemeral CI support artifacts (V5a)
    17→
    18→## Ordered Script Flow
    19→
    20→1. `01-clone-repos.py`
    21→2. `02a-scrape-wiki.py` (independent branch in hosted workflow)
    22→3. `02b` through `02h` (repo-backed extractors, gated on `01`)
    23→4. `03-normalize-semantic.py`
    24→5. `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` when AI enrichment is enabled; it performs its own AI-store preflight before enrichment
    25→6. `05a-assemble-references.py`
    26→7. `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` (V5a, when `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`) — assembles the release-tree from existing pipeline output; in Phase 4 this script is retired and its functionality is absorbed into the modified late stages
    27→8. `05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py`
    28→9. `05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py`
    29→10. `05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py`
    30→11. `06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py`
    31→12. `07-generate-web-index.py`
    32→13. `08-validate-output.py`
    33→
    34→Letter suffixes denote scripts that are parallelizable in deployment. Local smoke tests may still execute them sequentially.
    35→
    36→`tools/manage_ai_store.py` is the maintained local scratch-first helper.
    37→
    38→Hosted Option B topology now runs `02a` in a separate job without waiting on `initialize`, while repo-backed `02b` through `02h` remain in a matrix job that depends on `initialize`.
    39→
    40→## Execution Contract Matrix
    41→
    42→This matrix defines placement rationale, mutability boundaries, dependencies, and failure power.
    43→
    44→| Step | Mutability & atomicity | Primary dependencies | Failure mode in current workflow | Deploy impact |
    45→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    46→| `01` | Writes L0 clones and `repo-manifest.json` under `WORKDIR`; isolated to initialize job | Git checkout, network access | Hard fail (`initialize` fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    47→| `02a` | Writes only wiki L1 outputs and wiki cache under `WORKDIR` | Internet/wiki availability, `WORKDIR` | Hard fail (`extract_wiki` job fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    48→| `02b`-`02h` | Write module L1 outputs under `WORKDIR`; no publish writes | L0 clone artifacts from `01`, toolchain deps | Hard fail (repo-backed matrix leg fails) | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    49→| `03` | Converts L1 to L2 and promotes staged intermediates inside `OUTDIR`; atomic within process job sandbox | L1 artifacts, metadata sidecars | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    50→| `04` | Optional in-place L2 enrichment in staged `OUTDIR`; no direct publish writes | `SKIP_AI`, cache availability, optional token | If `SKIP_AI=true`, skipped cleanly. If enabled and it fails, hard fail in `process` | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    51→| `05a`-`05d` | Deterministic generation of publish companions inside `OUTDIR` | Staged L2 and registry outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    52→| `05e` (V5a) | Writes `release-tree/` and `support-tree/` from existing OUTDIR output; feature-flagged (`ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE`); retired at Phase 4+ when late stages write directly into `release-tree/` | Complete staged OUTDIR from `05a` through `07`; only runs when `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` | Hard fail in process (but only runs when flag is true) | No deploy if fails; existing OUTDIR output unchanged |
    53→| `06` | Builds routing/index artifacts inside `OUTDIR` | L2 metadata and reference outputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    54→| `07` | Generates `index.html` inside `OUTDIR` | Prior index inputs | Hard fail | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    55→| `08` | Validation gate over full staged `OUTDIR`; no external writes | Complete staged corpus | Hard fail on hard checks | No deploy; published output unchanged |
    56→| `deploy` | `rsync -a --delete` from staging into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` and optional bot commit | Successful `process` job and `final-staging` artifact | Hard fail in deploy only | Only step that can change published output |
    57→
    58→## Return-State Guarantees
    59→
    60→1. If any `process`-phase script fails, `final-staging` is not promoted and deploy does not run.
    61→2. Because promotion is isolated to the deploy job, partially generated outputs from `03` through `08` stay sandboxed in runner staging.
    62→3. This means a failed `08` cannot publish a mismatched `07` web index to the committed output tree.
    63→4. `SKIP_AI=true` is the explicit optional path for AI enrichment; AI failure is not ignored once the AI step is enabled.
    64→
    65→## Key Handoffs
    66→
    67→- `01` produces cloned repositories and any repo manifest data required by downstream stages.
    68→- `02*` scripts write L1 markdown plus `.meta.json` sidecars.
    69→- `02b` now exposes import-safe helper surfaces for fenced-block normalization and example fixups so targeted tests can import it directly.
    70→- `03` reads only L1 plus metadata and writes L2 plus a cross-link registry before promoting stable intermediates into `OUTDIR`.
    71→- `03` is also the correct place for bounded module-specific cleanup that should not mutate L1 raw retention; the current live example is wiki-only cleanup for residual DokuWiki and pandoc artifacts.
    72→- `04` mutates staged L2 files in place when enabled.
    73→- `05` consumes staged L2 files and emits skeletons plus monolithic references.
    74→- `05e` (V5a transition) reads from existing `OUTDIR` output and writes the V5a `release-tree/` layout with renamed files (`map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`); it also moves support-only artifacts into `support-tree/`. When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` at Phase 4+, `05a` through `08` write directly into `release-tree/` and `05e` is retired.
    75→- `06*` scripts consume staged L2 metadata and generated references to emit maps, agent instructions, IDE schemas, and telemetry.
    76→- `07` consumes the map outputs and writes the HTML landing page.
    77→- `08` validates the full `OUTDIR` tree and now exposes import-safe parsing helpers for direct unit coverage.
    78→
    79→## Observability Baseline
    80→
    81→Current hosted logs expose started/completed timestamps per job and per step via `gh run view --json jobs`.
    82→
    83→Example timing snapshot from successful run `22901854476`:
    84→
    85→- `initialize`: ~31s total, with `Clone repositories` ~9s
    86→- `extract_wiki (02a-scrape-wiki.py)`: ~211s
    87→- `process`: ~56s
    88→- `deploy`: ~18s
    89→
    90→Current workflow observability artifacts now include:
    91→
    92→- extractor-level status manifests (`extract-status-*`)
    93→- an always-generated `extract-summary` artifact
    94→- a `process-summary` staging contract artifact
    95→- an always-generated `pipeline-summary` artifact
    96→
    97→These artifacts are intended to be the first-stop troubleshooting surface before deep log forensics.
    98→
    99→## Scope Note
   100→
   101→The current AI-alignment slice keeps `04` as the single numbered AI stage and
   102→moves local review, audit, validation, and promotion into `tools/manage_ai_store.py`.
   103→Deeper model-selection and quality-scoring work remains deferred.
   104→
   105→## High-Value Debugging Paths
   106→
   107→- If the issue is stale or malformed wiki prose in committed outputs, inspect `02a` for source/cache behavior and `03` for semantic cleanup.
   108→- If the issue is misclassified ucode examples or false syntax warnings, inspect `02b` and `08` together because the extractor and validator now share regression coverage boundaries.
   109→- If the issue is missing, stale, or structurally invalid AI summaries, inspect `04`, `tools/manage_ai_store.py`, and the `data/base/` plus `data/override/` store roots together.
   110→- If the issue is missing or overwritten generated outputs, inspect the workflow `process` to `deploy` handoff because `staging/` is now the authoritative promotion source.
   111→- If the issue is only visible in committed `openwrt-condensed-docs/`, compare it against the focused corpus sanity snapshot from `tests/pytest/pytest_03_wiki_corpus_sanity_test.py` to decide whether the repo tree is stale or the normalization logic is still wrong.
   112→
   113→## Required Environment Variables
   114→
   115→- `WORKDIR`
   116→- `OUTDIR`
   117→- `SKIP_WIKI`
   118→- `SKIP_AI`
   119→- `WIKI_MAX_PAGES`
   120→- `MAX_AI_FILES`
   121→- `VALIDATE_MODE`
   122→- `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` (V5a; default `false`; when `true`, activates `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` and the release-tree validation path in `08`)
   123→
   124→## Required Schema Facts
   125→
   126→- L1 files are pure markdown and must not begin with YAML frontmatter.
   127→- L1 sidecars are `.meta.json` files stored beside the markdown file.
   128→- L2 files require YAML frontmatter with at least `title`, `module`, `origin_type`, `token_count`, and `version`.
   129→- L4 files use one top-level YAML block for the full monolith rather than reusing L2 frontmatter inline.
   130→
   131→## Verified Execution Priority
   132→
   133→The first required execution proof remains local and sequential. GitHub Actions is now a verified second-stage execution and publication layer, so pipeline debugging should distinguish between local logic regressions and remote promotion or cache-state effects.
   134→
```

     1→# Development Guide
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This file is the maintainer quick start for local development. The current engineering priority is to keep the Windows development path and the sequential smoke-test path reliable while preserving the now-verified GitHub Actions publication flow. The project is actively transitioning to the V5a release-tree contract; see [release-tree-contract.md](docs/specs/v12/release-tree-contract.md) for the target output layout.
     6→
     7→## Prerequisites
     8→
     9→| Tool | Version | Purpose |
    10→| --- | --- | --- |
    11→| Python | 3.11+ | All pipeline scripts and local tests |
    12→| Node.js | 20+ | `jsdoc-to-markdown` and JavaScript syntax checks |
    13→| pandoc | 3.0+ | Wiki conversion |
    14→| git | 2.25+ | Repo cloning and versioned refactors |
    15→| jsdoc-to-markdown | current npm release | API doc extraction |
    16→
    17→## Quick Start on Windows
    18→
    19→```powershell
    20→pip install -r .github/scripts/requirements.txt
    21→npm install -g jsdoc-to-markdown
    22→winget install --id JohnMacFarlane.Pandoc
    23→```
    24→
    25→After dependencies are installed, use the local tests in `tests/` rather than relying on GitHub Actions behavior.
    26→
    27→## Recommended Local Commands
    28→
    29→```powershell
    30→python tests/run_pytest.py
    31→python tests/run_smoke.py
    32→python tests/run_smoke.py --run-ai
    33→python tests/run_smoke.py --include-extractors
    34→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py
    35→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py --run-ai --keep-temp
    36→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py
    37→python tests/check_linting.py
    38→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review
    39→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option full --keep-scratch
    40→```
    41→
    42→The `--run-ai` path is cache-backed for local verification, so it can validate the placement and mutation behavior of script `04` without requiring a live model token.
    43→
    44→For real AI-summary generation, use `python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review` or the detailed scratch-first workflow in [docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md](docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md). Do not treat `--run-ai` as proof of live API generation or permanent AI-store promotion.
    45→
    46→For one-off terminal invocations, either activate `.venv` once before testing or call the workspace interpreter directly via `C:/Users/MC/Documents/AirSentinel/openwrt-docs4ai-v12-copilot/.venv/Scripts/python.exe`. Do not assume the system `python` on `PATH` is the repo interpreter.
    47→
    48→## Validation Runners
    49→
    50→The maintained local validation surface is now Python-first and cross-platform.
    51→See `tests/README.md` for the full layout and direct entry points.
    52→
    53→- `tests/run_pytest.py` runs the focused suites in `tests/pytest/` and forwards any extra CLI arguments to pytest.
    54→- `tests/run_smoke.py` runs the maintained smoke lane in serial order. It supports `--run-ai`, `--keep-temp`, `--include-extractors`, the individual `--skip-*` switches, and `--result-root`.
    55→- `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` is the maintained one-command sequential validation path. It runs the focused pytest lane first, then the enabled smoke stages, and writes per-stage logs plus `summary.json` under `tmp/ci/local-validation/`.
    56→- `tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py` runs one pytest lane and one smoke lane in parallel. Use it only for that supported split; do not treat it as permission to run multiple smoke scripts concurrently.
    57→- `tests/check_linting.py` performs a read-only Ruff, strict Pyright, and actionlint review and writes its bundle under `tmp/ci/lint-review/`.
    58→
    59→These runners are intentionally local-first. Remote GitHub Actions validation still depends on a pushed commit and the run-pinning procedure in `CI Operations`.
    60→
    61→## Terminal Testing Discipline
    62→
    63→When validating this project from the terminal, prefer deterministic, bounded commands over ad hoc shell control flow.
    64→
    65→1. Start in the repo root with the workspace virtual environment active, or use the explicit `.venv` interpreter path when running a single isolated command.
    66→2. Run the smallest proof first: `python tests/run_pytest.py`, then `python tests/run_smoke.py`, then remote workflow validation.
    67→3. When you want the full preferred local progression in one command, use `python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` instead of rebuilding the sequence ad hoc.
    68→4. Keep one purpose per command. Prefer a direct command invocation over inline retry loops or multi-purpose shell snippets.
    69→5. Capture durable local evidence when it matters, typically under `tests/test-results-*.txt` or `tmp/ci/`, so reruns can be compared without re-reading terminal scrollback.
    70→6. For GitHub Actions validation, pin the run to the pushed commit SHA, identify the matching `databaseId`, and wait on that exact run with `gh run watch <run_id> --exit-status --interval 15`.
    71→7. After the run finishes, inspect `pipeline-summary`, `process-summary`, and `extract-summary` artifacts before opening raw failed-job logs.
    72→8. Avoid open-ended polling loops, mid-run log scraping, and assumptions about "the latest run". They create noisy output and make it easy to validate the wrong workflow run.
    73→
    74→## Pre-Change Checklist
    75→
    76→Before editing pipeline scripts, generated outputs, or workflow behavior:
    77→
    78→1. Decide whether the change affects maintainer docs, generated-corpus contracts, workflow execution, or only local tests.
    79→2. Read this file, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/specs/v12/schema-definitions.md`, and `docs/specs/v12/execution-map.md` before changing numbered scripts or the workflow.
    80→3. If the change touches scripts `05b` through `08`, inspect the currently generated AI-facing outputs first: `openwrt-condensed-docs/llms.txt`, `openwrt-condensed-docs/llms-full.txt`, `openwrt-condensed-docs/AGENTS.md`, and at least one representative `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/llms.txt`.
    81→4. If the change touches `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`, map the intended behavior to a specific trigger path: push on `main`, monthly schedule, or `workflow_dispatch` with explicit inputs.
    82→5. Run the smallest local proof first. Only use remote GitHub Actions runs after local validation passes.
    83→
    84→## Dual-Role LLM Surfaces
    85→
    86→This repository has two LLM-relevant surfaces and they should not be conflated:
    87→
    88→- The source repository is the implementation and maintainer-doc surface. Its authoritative docs live under `docs/`, `README.md`, and `DEVELOPMENT.md`.
    89→- The generated corpus under `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the published AI navigation surface consumed by downstream tools and models.
    90→
    91→The strict routing contract for generated `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, module `llms.txt`, and `AGENTS.md` lives in `docs/specs/v12/schema-definitions.md`.
    92→
    93→Under V5a, the published AI navigation surface moves from `openwrt-condensed-docs/` to the external `release-tree/` layout with generic filenames (`map.md`, `bundled-reference.md`, `chunked-reference/`). The `openwrt-condensed-docs` name is internal to the source repo only and never appears in any public path or URL.
    94→
    95→A source-repo root `llms.txt` remains intentionally out of scope for the current maintenance tranche. Do not create one opportunistically while working on generated output behavior.
    96→
    97→## Repository Rules
    98→
    99→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the stable generated output root.
   100→- Under V5a (`ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`), `release-tree/` is the publishable output root. `openwrt-condensed-docs/` remains as the internal pipeline working directory.
   101→- `tmp/` is ephemeral and never authoritative.
   102→- `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic` are the standard intermediate layer names.
   103→- Script numbering denotes stage families and dependency boundaries. Letter suffixes denote sibling scripts inside the same stage family.
   104→- A bare stage id such as `04` cannot coexist with `04a`, `04b`, or other lettered siblings. A stage is either singular or lettered, never both.
   105→- Non-pipeline support tools belong outside the numbered stage surface, under `tools/`.
   106→- Local smoke runs may still execute the lettered scripts sequentially.
   107→- Local-only references that are out of scope for this repo (for example third-party bug notes) should stay under `tmp/legacy-backup/` so they remain durable locally and gitignored.
   108→
   109→## Script Families
   110→
   111→| Script | Role |
   112→| --- | --- |
   113→| `openwrt-docs4ai-01-clone-repos.py` | Prepare L0 source inputs and manifests |
   114→| `openwrt-docs4ai-02a` through `02h` | Source-specific extraction into L1 |
   115→| `openwrt-docs4ai-03-normalize-semantic.py` | L1 to L2 normalization and promotion |
   116→| `openwrt-docs4ai-04-generate-ai-summaries.py` | Optional AI summary enrichment |
   117→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05a-assemble-references.py` | Assemble publishable L3 skeletons and L4 monoliths |
   118→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py` | Generate AGENTS.md and the root generated README |
   119→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py` | Generate ucode IDE schema output |
   120→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py` | Generate API drift telemetry against the baseline inventory |
   121→| `openwrt-docs4ai-05e-assemble-release-tree.py` | Assemble V5a release-tree from existing pipeline output (feature-flagged) |
   122→| `openwrt-docs4ai-06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py` | Generate llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and module routing indexes |
   123→| `openwrt-docs4ai-07-generate-web-index.py` | HTML landing page generation |
   124→| `openwrt-docs4ai-08-validate-output.py` | Whole-output validation gate |
   125→
   126→## Local Tests
   127→
   128→- `tests/pytest/` contains the focused pytest suites for import-safe helpers, workflow contracts, fixture routing, corpus sanity, and wiki scraper behavior.
   129→- `tests/smoke/` contains the direct smoke runners; use `python tests/run_smoke.py` for the maintained serial sequence.
   130→- `tests/check_linting.py` produces read-only local review bundles for Ruff, strict Pyright, and actionlint.
   131→
   132→These entry points are maintained as first-class engineering assets. Use `tests/README.md` when you need the exact folder contract or runner/output mapping.
   133→
   134→## AI Summary Operations
   135→
   136→Real AI-summary work is intentionally AI-store first and scratch first.
   137→
   138→- `data/base/` and `data/override/` are the authoritative AI-summary surfaces.
   139→- `openwrt-condensed-docs/` is downstream generated evidence.
   140→- The permanent workflow lives in [docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md](docs/specs/v12/ai-summary-operations-runbook.md).
   141→- The only numbered AI stage is `.github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-04-generate-ai-summaries.py`.
   142→- Script `04` performs its own library-backed preflight against the selected AI store before applying or generating summaries.
   143→- The maintained one-command helper is `tools/manage_ai_store.py`.
   144→- `--option review` prepares scratch data, runs script `04`, then runs the same library-backed validation and audit checks against the scratch store.
   145→- `--option promote` copies reviewed scratch JSON into `data/base/` and immediately reruns validation and audit against the permanent store.
   146→- `--option full` runs the full review-plus-promotion path and can remove scratch data automatically unless `--keep-scratch` is set.
   147→
   148→Use the cache-backed smoke paths for regression proof, and use the runbook plus `tools/manage_ai_store.py` for real generation, review, and promotion.
   149→
   150→## Remote Publish Policy
   151→
   152→- The workflow builds generated artifacts into `staging/` first and only promotes them in the `deploy` job.
   153→- Hosted extraction now runs `02a` in parallel with `01`, while `02b` through `02h` remain clone-gated.
   154→- On push, schedule, and manual runs, the deploy job syncs `staging/` into `openwrt-condensed-docs/` with `rsync -a --delete`.
   155→- If the promoted tree changed, GitHub Actions writes a bot-authored commit in the form `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD`.
   156→- GitHub Pages publishes a `public/` copy of staging that excludes `L1-raw` and `L2-semantic`.
   157→- Workflow diagnostics now include `extract-summary`, `process-summary`, and `pipeline-summary` artifacts for first-stop triage.
   158→- Avoid hand-editing generated outputs if the next workflow run is expected to republish them.
   159→
   160→When `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true` and Phase 6 is active, the deploy job additionally publishes the validated `release-tree/` to external targets: `openwrt-docs4ai.github.io` (GitHub Pages) and the `corpus` release repository under the `openwrt-docs4ai` GitHub organization. Existing source-repo deploy behavior remains intact regardless of V5a deployment status.
   161→
   162→## Workflow Triggers And Manual Inputs
   163→
   164→- `push` runs only on `main` and follows the normal publish path.
   165→- `schedule` runs on the first day of each month at `13:00 UTC`.
   166→- `workflow_dispatch` exists for targeted verification and controlled reruns.
   167→- Manual dispatch supports four inputs: `skip_wiki`, `skip_buildroot`, `skip_ai`, and `max_ai_files`.
   168→- Manual dispatch can target a specific ref. Use `gh workflow run "openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml" --ref <branch>` when you need remote proof for a non-`main` branch.
   169→- Treat `workflow_dispatch` as the preferred remote test path for pipeline changes because it makes the intended skip knobs explicit in run history.
   170→
   171→## Dependency Policy
   172→
   173→- `.github/scripts/requirements.txt` is intentionally kept as a small direct dependency list rather than a blanket fully pinned lockfile.
   174→- Add new direct dependencies only when they materially simplify the pipeline or improve output reliability.
   175→- Do not add exact pins by default. Pin or lock only after verifying a concrete reproducibility or breakage problem.
   176→- When a dependency-related failure appears, record the exact failing version in the investigation notes and then decide whether targeted pinning is justified.
   177→- Keep local and CI bootstrap lightweight enough that a low-touch maintainer can rebuild the environment without a separate dependency-management project.
   178→
   179→## Optional Workflow Linting
   180→
   181→If you edit `.github/workflows/openwrt-docs4ai-00-pipeline.yml`, run `actionlint` locally when it is available on your machine. This is intentionally optional and is not a mandatory bootstrap dependency for the repo.
   182→
   183→## CI Operations
   184→
   185→### Waiting for a GitHub Actions run and reading its logs
   186→
   187→This pipeline runs CI frequently. Follow the procedure below every time you push a commit and need to verify the result. Do not improvise — the anti-pattern list below is based on real mistakes that have caused wasted debug cycles in this project.
   188→
   189→**Best method: pin-then-triage**
   190→
   191→**Phase A — wait for the right run to complete**
   192→
   193→1. Capture the exact commit SHA you pushed:
   194→
   195→   ```powershell
   196→   git rev-parse HEAD
   197→   ```
   198→
   199→2. List recent workflow runs and find the one that matches that SHA (never assume "latest" is yours — a deploy auto-update commit may have started a newer run):
   200→
   201→   ```powershell
   202→   gh run list --workflow "openwrt-docs4ai pipeline (v12)" --limit 20 --json databaseId,headSha,status,conclusion,url
   203→   ```
   204→
   205→3. Copy the `databaseId` of the matching entry and wait on that specific run with a bounded poll interval:
   206→
   207→   ```powershell
   208→   gh run watch <run_id> --exit-status --interval 15
   209→   ```
   210→
   211→   `--exit-status` returns exit code 1 on failure so the caller detects it without manual logic. `--interval 15` polls every 15 seconds. Block here until it exits — do not proceed to Phase B while the run is still in progress.
   212→
   213→**Phase B — triage from summary artifacts before opening raw logs**
   214→
   215→4. Download and inspect the structured pipeline summary artifact first:
   216→
   217→   ```powershell
   218→   gh run download <run_id> -n pipeline-summary -D tmp/ci/pipeline-summary
   219→   Get-Content tmp/ci/pipeline-summary/*.json | ConvertFrom-Json
   220→   ```
   221→
   222→5. If that implicates a specific extractor, also pull the per-extractor status bundle:
   223→
   224→   ```powershell
   225→   gh run download <run_id> -n extract-summary -D tmp/ci/extract-summary
   226→   ```
   227→
   228→6. Only if the summary artifacts indicate a failure you still cannot explain structurally, open raw failed-job logs:
   229→
   230→   ```powershell
   231→   gh run view <run_id> --log-failed
   232→   ```
   233→
   234→Quick whole-run status check:
   235→
   236→```powershell
   237→gh run view <run_id> --json jobs,conclusion,url
   238→```
   239→
   240→### Things to avoid when waiting for CI
   241→
   242→- **Do not poll "the latest run"** — always pin to the SHA of your push. After a successful deploy the workflow writes a bot-authored `docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD` commit which triggers a new run; the "latest" ID is no longer yours.
   243→- **Do not run an open-ended polling loop** — an unbounded `while ($true)` loop in a chat session consumes tokens, hides intermediate output, and looks like an infinite hang to both the user and the model. Always use `gh run watch` with `--interval` instead.
   244→- **Do not pull full logs before the run finishes** — logs are noisy and incomplete mid-run; wait for `gh run watch` to exit cleanly before any log inspection.
   245→- **Do not skip the artifact triage phase** — `pipeline-summary`, `process-summary`, and `extract-summary` exist precisely to avoid log forensics; read them first and fall back to raw logs only for unexplained failures.
   246→- **Do not assume a deploy auto-commit is your commit** — verify by checking `headSha` on the run, not by run order.
   247→
   248→## Environment Variables
   249→
   250→Defaults below are the direct local defaults from `lib/config.py` and the local
   251→AI tooling. The hosted workflow overrides `WORKDIR` and `OUTDIR`, and manual
   252→dispatch exposes `skip_ai=false` by default.
   253→
   254→| Variable | Default | Purpose |
   255→| --- | --- | --- |
   256→| `WORKDIR` | `tmp` | Scratch area for cloned repos and intermediate layers |
   257→| `OUTDIR` | `openwrt-condensed-docs` | Stable output root for generated artifacts |
   258→| `SKIP_WIKI` | `false` | Skip wiki extraction |
   259→| `SKIP_AI` | `true` | Disable optional AI enrichment by default |
   260→| `WRITE_AI` | `true` | Allow script `04` to create missing base records when AI is enabled |
   261→| `WIKI_MAX_PAGES` | `300` | Limit wiki traversal depth |
   262→| `MAX_AI_FILES` | `40` | Limit local or remote AI summary volume |
   263→| `VALIDATE_MODE` | `hard` | Validation severity mode |
   264→| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | empty | Remote-only integrations and telemetry fallback retrieval |
   265→| `LOCAL_DEV_TOKEN` | empty | Local override token for optional AI enrichment |
   266→| `AI_DATA_BASE_DIR` | `data/base` | Base AI summary store root |
   267→| `AI_DATA_OVERRIDE_DIR` | `data/override` | Override AI summary store root |
   268→| `ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE` | `false` | Controls V5a release-tree output generation; when true, late stages produce `release-tree/` layout |
   269→
   270→## Documentation And Reporting Conventions
   271→
   272→- Use relative Markdown links for navigational cross-references between maintainer documents and generated outputs.
   273→- When a file or path reference is meant to be opened by the reader, prefer a Markdown link instead of an inline code span.
   274→- Keep inline code for commands, environment variables, symbol names, filenames used as literals, and short syntax fragments.
   275→- Do not inject or rewrite cross-links inside code fences or inline code spans.
   276→
   277→## Adding or Changing a Scraper
   278→
   279→If a new extractor is added or a current extractor is materially changed:
   280→
   281→1. Keep it in `.github/scripts/` with the numbered naming convention.
   282→2. Make sure it writes only to `WORKDIR/L1-raw/{module}/`.
   283→3. Write `.meta.json` sidecars through shared helper logic instead of ad hoc metadata code.
   284→4. Update the deterministic tests and the sequential smoke runner.
   285→5. Update `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and active v12 specs if the contract changed.
   286→
   287→## Logging Convention
   288→
   289→Scripts should emit concise line-buffered messages using the numbered prefix convention, for example:
   290→
   291→```text
   292→[02a] OK: scraped 15 pages
   293→[05d] INFO: Baseline lacks module metadata; suppressing module drift sections
   294→[04] SKIP: AI enrichment disabled
   295→[08] FAIL: missing llms.txt
   296→```
   297→
   298→## Deferred Bugs
   299→
   300→- `luci-app-dockerman` still produces one truthful non-blocking standalone `ucode` validation warning in `docker_rpc.uc`. Keep it soft until a higher-fidelity LuCI runtime validation context exists.
   301→- Mermaid template promotion is still deferred. The repository has Mermaid source templates, but the exact publication targets and insertion rules are not constrained tightly enough to inject diagrams automatically without risking incorrect output.
   302→
   303→## Deferred Features
   304→
   305→- Extreme A3 renaming remains deferred. If revisited, prefer fully descriptive stage-prefixed or category-first filenames only after the current moderate stage-family contract is stable across the workflow, tests, and docs.
   306→- `signature-inventory.json` still does not emit explicit module metadata. The current `05d` fix suppresses false module drift against legacy baselines; richer schema expansion remains a later compatibility decision.
   307→- Curated example extraction still warns on partial read failures instead of failing the run. Hard-failing partial omissions is deferred until the curated app and file inventory is formalized.
   308→- Broader validation coverage beyond the current targeted fixes is deferred until the renamed stage contract and baseline expectations are stable.
   309→
   310→## Windows Notes
   311→
   312→- Windows is a required development environment for this project.
   313→- Path logic must remain cross-platform, but local development is the first validation target.
   314→- A small number of Linux-only checks may exist later for remote workflow validation, but they must be documented explicitly.
   315→
   316→## Current Focus
   317→
   318→The pipeline reached a stable, fully-tested state on 2026-03-11. All stabilization phases are complete. The next active engineering work is the V5a release-tree refactor, which introduces a `release-tree/` / `support-tree/` dual output model and deploys to external distribution targets.
   319→
   320→Ongoing monitored items:
   321→
   322→1. The `luci-app-dockerman` ucode warning (`REMOTE-008`) remains a deferred soft warning — intentionally preserved as a truthful signal, not suppressed.
   323→2. Mermaid diagram template promotion remains deferred until a concrete consumer exists.
   324→
   325→The pipeline is otherwise healthy: local tests pass, CI runs succeed with 0 hard failures, and published outputs are generated and committed correctly on every run. When returning to active development, run `python tests/run_pytest.py` locally first, then use `python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py` before following the CI Operations procedure above.
   326→
```

```text
     1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## What This Repository Does
     6→
     7→**openwrt-docs4ai** is a documentation production pipeline — not an application runtime. It collects OpenWrt documentation from multiple upstream sources (wiki, git repos, APIs), normalizes it through a staged layer model (L0→L1→L2→L3/L4), and publishes compact outputs for humans, IDE tooling, and LLM workflows. GitHub Actions is the verified remote execution path; Windows is the primary local development environment. The project is actively transitioning to a V5a release-tree contract that separates publishable output from internal pipeline artifacts and deploys to external distribution targets.
     8→
     9→## Prerequisites
    10→
    11→```powershell
    12→pip install -r .github/scripts/requirements.txt
    13→npm install -g jsdoc-to-markdown
    14→winget install --id JohnMacFarlane.Pandoc
    15→```
    16→
    17→Use the workspace interpreter directly when needed: `.venv/Scripts/python.exe`. Do not assume the system `python` on PATH is the repo interpreter.
    18→
    19→## Local Validation Commands
    20→
    21→Run the smallest proof first, then expand:
    22→
    23→```powershell
    24→python tests/run_pytest.py                              # focused pytest suites
    25→python tests/run_smoke.py                               # serial smoke lane
    26→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py                    # preferred full local validation
    27→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py --run-ai --keep-temp
    28→python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest_parallel.py           # parallel pytest + smoke
    29→python tests/check_linting.py                           # Ruff + strict Pyright + actionlint
    30→
    31→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option review         # AI store review (no promotion)
    32→python tools/manage_ai_store.py --option full --keep-scratch
    33→```
    34→
    35→`--run-ai` is cache-backed for regression proof only — it does not generate real AI summaries or promote to the AI store. Results land under `tmp/ci/`.
    36→
    37→## Running a Single Test
    38→
    39→```powershell
    40→python tests/run_pytest.py tests/pytest/pytest_01_workflow_contract_test.py
    41→python tests/run_pytest.py -k "test_name_pattern"
    42→```
    43→
    44→## CI Operations
    45→
    46→Always pin to your commit SHA — a successful deploy triggers a bot commit (`docs: v12 auto-update YYYY-MM-DD`) that starts a new "latest" run.
    47→
    48→```powershell
    49→git rev-parse HEAD
    50→gh run list --workflow "openwrt-docs4ai pipeline (v12)" --limit 20 --json databaseId,headSha,status,conclusion,url
    51→gh run watch <run_id> --exit-status --interval 15
    52→
    53→# After completion — triage artifacts before raw logs
    54→gh run download <run_id> -n pipeline-summary -D tmp/ci/pipeline-summary
    55→gh run download <run_id> -n extract-summary -D tmp/ci/extract-summary
    56→gh run view <run_id> --log-failed                       # only if artifacts don't explain it
    57→```
    58→
    59→## Architecture: Layer Model
    60→
    61→| Layer | Location | Purpose | Lifetime |
    62→|-------|----------|---------|---------|
    63→| L0 | `tmp/repo-*` | Upstream source clones | Ephemeral |
    64→| L1 | `L1-raw/{module}/` | Raw normalized markdown + `.meta.json` sidecars | Generated |
    65→| L2 | `L2-semantic/{module}/` | Semantic markdown + YAML frontmatter + cross-links | Generated |
    66→| L3/L4 | `openwrt-condensed-docs/{module}/` | Published references, skeletons, routing indexes | Published |
    67→
    68→`openwrt-condensed-docs/` is the **stable output root** — never hand-edit it if a workflow run will overwrite it. `tmp/` is ephemeral scratch, never authoritative.
    69→
    70→Under V5a (`ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true`), L3/L4 output moves to `release-tree/{module}/` with generic filenames: `map.md` (was `*-skeleton.md`), `bundled-reference.md` (was `*-complete-reference.md`), `chunked-reference/` (was `L2-semantic/{module}/`).
    71→
    72→## Architecture: Pipeline Stage Flow
    73→
    74→Scripts in `.github/scripts/` execute in numbered order. Letter suffixes (e.g., `05a`, `05b`) are siblings in the same stage family. A bare stage id (e.g., `04`) cannot coexist with lettered siblings.
    75→
    76→| Script | Stage | Role |
    77→|--------|-------|------|
    78→| `01-clone-repos.py` | L0 | Shallow-clone ucode, luci, openwrt repos; emit `repo-manifest.json` |
    79→| `02a-scrape-wiki.py` | L1 | Wiki extraction (runs in parallel with `01` on CI) |
    80→| `02b` – `02h` | L1 | Source-specific extractors (clone-gated); each writes to `L1-raw/{module}/` |
    81→| `03-normalize-semantic.py` | L2 | Add YAML frontmatter, cross-links, token counts |
    82→| `04-generate-ai-summaries.py` | L2 | Optional AI enrichment; reads/writes `data/base/` AI store |
    83→| `05a-assemble-references.py` | L4 | Build complete-reference monoliths (auto-sharded at 100k tokens) + skeletons |
    84→| `05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py` | L3 | Generate `AGENTS.md` and root `README.md` for the corpus |
    85→| `05c-generate-ucode-ide-schemas.py` | L3 | TypeScript `.d.ts` IDE schemas |
    86→| `05d-generate-api-drift-changelog.py` | L5 | API drift telemetry vs. signature baseline |
    87→| `05e-assemble-release-tree.py` | L3/L4 | V5a release-tree assembly (feature-flagged) |
    88→| `06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py` | L3 | `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, per-module `llms.txt` |
    89→| `07-generate-web-index.py` | L3 | `index.html` landing page |
    90→| `08-validate-output.py` | — | Whole-output validation gate |
    91→
    92→Shared Python libraries live in `lib/` (`config.py`, `ai_store.py`, `ai_enrichment.py`, etc.). Non-numbered maintainer tools live in `tools/`.
    93→
    94→## Architecture: Two LLM Surfaces
    95→
    96→This repo has two distinct LLM-relevant surfaces — do not conflate them:
    97→
    98→- **Source repo** (`docs/`, `DEVELOPMENT.md`, `README.md`): Maintainer docs and implementation.

```md
# V5a Implementation Prompt

Use this prompt to instruct an AI coding assistant to implement the V5a release-tree refactor. Copy the relevant phase section into your session. Do not give the AI the entire prompt at once — work one phase at a time and validate between phases.

---

## Context Block (Always Include)

\```
You are implementing the V5a release-tree refactor for the openwrt-docs4ai project.
This is a documentation production pipeline (not an application runtime) that collects
OpenWrt documentation and publishes compact outputs for humans, IDE tooling, and LLM
workflows. The project runs on Windows locally and GitHub Actions remotely.

The refactor introduces a clean public output layout (release-tree/) that separates
publishable product files from internal pipeline artifacts, renames files to use generic
names, and prepares for deployment to external GitHub organization repos.

MANDATORY READING before writing any code — read these files IN THIS ORDER:

1. docs/plans/v12/public-distribution-mirror-plan-2026-03-15-V5a.md  (THE PLAN — read all of it)
2. docs/specs/v12/release-tree-contract.md        (the output contract you are building toward)
3. docs/specs/v12/feature-flag-contract.md         (how the feature flag works)
4. lib/config.py                                    (shared configuration — you will modify this)
5. docs/specs/v12/schema-definitions.md            (current + V5a contract definitions)
6. docs/specs/v12/execution-map.md                 (pipeline stage flow)

SECONDARY READING when you reach the relevant phase:

- .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-05a-assemble-references.py  (assembly stage you model after)
- .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-05b-generate-agents-and-readme.py
- .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-06-generate-llm-routing-indexes.py
- .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-07-generate-web-index.py
- .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-08-validate-output.py
- tests/support/smoke_pipeline_support.py          (test assertions that must change)
- tests/pytest/pytest_02_fixture_pipeline_contract_test.py
- tests/pytest/pytest_06_warning_regression_test.py

HOW TO VALIDATE YOUR WORK after every change:

  python tests/run_pytest.py                       # must pass — run first, always
  python tests/run_smoke.py                        # must pass — run second
  python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py             # full local validation — run before declaring phase complete

Use .venv/Scripts/python.exe if python on PATH is not the repo interpreter.
\```

---

## Rules (Always Include)

\```
CRITICAL RULES — violating any of these will break the project:

1. DO NOT MODIFY SCRIPTS 01 THROUGH 04. They are upstream of the refactor and must
   not change. If you think they need changing, you are misunderstanding the plan.

2. DO NOT DELETE OR RENAME openwrt-condensed-docs/. It remains the internal pipeline
   working directory. The refactor adds a NEW output path alongside it. The old path
   must keep working when ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false.

3. DO NOT CHANGE THE DEFAULT VALUE OF ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE until Phase 5. It defaults
   to false. All existing tests must pass with it set to false at every phase.

4. DO NOT MODIFY EXISTING TESTS until Phase 5. Add new tests only. Existing tests
   validate the current contract which must remain intact until the switchover.

5. DO NOT HAND-EDIT files under openwrt-condensed-docs/. They are generated by the
   pipeline. If you need to change what's there, change the script that generates it.

6. DO NOT USE FORCE PUSH, git reset --hard, or any destructive git operation.

7. USE RELATIVE PATHS in all generated output. No absolute paths. No hardcoded
   Windows paths. All path construction must use os.path.join().

8. USE CONFIG CONSTANTS for all public output names. Never write a bare string like
   "bundled-reference.md" in a script — import the constant from lib/config.py.

9. LOG WITH THE STAGE PREFIX. Script 05e logs as [05e]. Follow the pattern:
   [05e] OK: assembled 8 modules into release-tree
   [05e] WARN: skipping module with no L2 content

10. EVERY PHASE MUST END WITH ALL TESTS PASSING. Do not proceed to the next phase
    if any test fails. Fix the failure first.

11. ONE COMMIT PER PHASE. Do not combine phases. Each phase must be independently
    revertable.

12. DO NOT CREATE A SOURCE-REPO ROOT llms.txt. This is explicitly out of scope.
    Do not create it even if it seems helpful.

STYLE RULES:

- Follow the existing code style. Read 05a before writing 05e — match its patterns.
- Use sys.stdout.reconfigure(line_buffering=True) at script top.
- Use from __future__ import annotations at script top.
- Import lib.config via sys.path manipulation matching the pattern in existing scripts.
- Keep functions small. The existing scripts average 20-40 lines per function.
- No type: ignore comments. Fix the actual type issue.
- No print() for user-facing output — use the [05e] prefix pattern.
\```

---

## Phase 0: Add Shared Configuration Constants

\```
PHASE 0 TASK: Add V5a constants to lib/config.py

Read lib/config.py first. Then add these constants AFTER the existing "Computed Paths"
section, in a new clearly commented section:

# V5a Release Tree Configuration
ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE = os.environ.get("ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE", "false").lower() == "true"
RELEASE_TREE_DIR = os.path.join(OUTDIR, "release-tree")
SUPPORT_TREE_DIR = os.path.join(OUTDIR, "support-tree")

# V5a Public Output Name Constants
MODULE_MAP_FILENAME = "map.md"
MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME = "bundled-reference.md"
MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME = "chunked-reference"
MODULE_TYPES_DIRNAME = "types"

# V5a Release Include Paths (relative to repo root)
RELEASE_INCLUDE_DIR = os.path.join(_REPO_ROOT, "release-inputs", "release-include")
PAGES_INCLUDE_DIR = os.path.join(_REPO_ROOT, "release-inputs", "pages-include")

DO NOT modify ensure_dirs() to create these directories yet. They should only be
created by the script that needs them (05e in Phase 1).

DO NOT change any existing constants or their defaults.

VALIDATE: python tests/run_pytest.py && python tests/run_smoke.py
Both must pass with zero changes to behavior.

GATE: The new constants exist but are not consumed by any script.
\```

---

## Phase 1: Create the Release Assembly Script

\```
PHASE 1 TASK: Create .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-05e-assemble-release-tree.py

This is the most important phase. Read the ENTIRE V5a plan §4.2 and §5 Phase 1
before starting. Also read 05a-assemble-references.py to understand the codebase
patterns.

THE SCRIPT MUST:

1. Check ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE. If false, log "[05e] SKIP: release-tree assembly
   disabled" and exit 0 immediately. This is not optional.

2. Create RELEASE_TREE_DIR and SUPPORT_TREE_DIR.

3. For each module directory found in OUTDIR (excluding L1-raw, L2-semantic, and
   non-module items like root files):

   a. Create release-tree/{module}/

   b. Copy {module}/{module}-complete-reference.md → release-tree/{module}/bundled-reference.md
      - Use the constant MODULE_BUNDLED_REF_FILENAME from config
      - If sharded parts exist ({module}-complete-reference.part-*.md), copy them as
        bundled-reference.part-{NN}.md and rewrite the index to point to new names

   c. Copy {module}/{module}-skeleton.md → release-tree/{module}/map.md
      - Use the constant MODULE_MAP_FILENAME from config

   d. Copy L2-semantic/{module}/*.md → release-tree/{module}/chunked-reference/
      - Use the constant MODULE_CHUNKED_REF_DIRNAME from config
      - These are the individual topic pages

   e. Copy {module}/types/ → release-tree/{module}/types/ (if it exists)

   f. Copy {module}/llms.txt → release-tree/{module}/llms.txt
      - REWRITE INTERNAL PATHS: replace old filenames with new ones in the text content
      - {module}-skeleton.md → map.md
      - {module}-complete-reference.md → bundled-reference.md
      - ../L2-semantic/{module}/ → ./chunked-reference/

4. Copy root files to release-tree/:
   - OUTDIR/llms.txt → release-tree/llms.txt (rewrite paths)
   - OUTDIR/llms-full.txt → release-tree/llms-full.txt (rewrite paths)
   - OUTDIR/README.md → release-tree/README.md (rewrite paths, remove openwrt-condensed-docs references)
   - OUTDIR/AGENTS.md → release-tree/AGENTS.md (rewrite paths)
   - OUTDIR/index.html → release-tree/index.html (rewrite paths)

5. Move support artifacts to support-tree/:
   - OUTDIR/L1-raw/ → support-tree/raw/
   - L2-semantic pages are already copied into chunked-reference/, do NOT also copy
     the L2-semantic tree into support-tree unless you are also preserving it for
     debug artifact upload
   - OUTDIR/cross-link-registry.json → support-tree/manifests/cross-link-registry.json
   - OUTDIR/repo-manifest.json → support-tree/manifests/repo-manifest.json (if present)
   - OUTDIR/CHANGES.md → support-tree/telemetry/CHANGES.md
   - OUTDIR/changelog.json → support-tree/telemetry/changelog.json
   - OUTDIR/signature-inventory.json → support-tree/telemetry/signature-inventory.json

6. Log a summary: "[05e] OK: assembled N modules into release-tree"

PATH REWRITING — this is where bugs hide. Rules:

- Use simple string .replace() for known patterns. Do NOT use regex unless the
  replacement is ambiguous without it.
- The replacements for module llms.txt are:
    ./{module}-skeleton.md        → ./map.md
    ./{module}-complete-reference.md → ./bundled-reference.md
    ./{module}-complete-reference.part-  → ./bundled-reference.part-
    ../L2-semantic/{module}/      → ./chunked-reference/
- The replacements for root llms.txt are:
    {module}/{module}-skeleton.md → {module}/map.md
    {module}/{module}-complete-reference.md → {module}/bundled-reference.md
    L2-semantic/{module}/         → {module}/chunked-reference/
- The replacements for llms-full.txt are similar to root llms.txt.
- For AGENTS.md and README.md: also replace visible text references to
  "openwrt-condensed-docs" — but be careful not to corrupt URLs or code blocks.
- For index.html: replace ./openwrt-condensed-docs/ prefixes and old filenames in
  href attributes and visible text.

IMPORTANT: Use shutil.copy2 for file copies to preserve metadata. Use os.makedirs
with exist_ok=True. Handle missing optional files gracefully (log a WARN, don't crash).

REGISTER IN SMOKE SUPPORT:
After creating the script, add it to the POST_EXTRACT_PIPELINE list in
tests/support/smoke_pipeline_support.py — insert it after 05d and before 06.
The entry is: "openwrt-docs4ai-05e-assemble-release-tree.py"

VALIDATE:
  ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false python tests/run_smoke_and_pytest.py   (must pass, 05e skips)
  ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true python tests/run_smoke.py               (must pass, 05e runs)

Manually inspect tmp/ci/ output to confirm release-tree/ was created with the
expected layout. Check at least:
  - release-tree/llms.txt exists and contains no "openwrt-condensed-docs" or old filenames
  - release-tree/ucode/map.md exists
  - release-tree/ucode/bundled-reference.md exists
  - release-tree/ucode/chunked-reference/ has .md files
  - release-tree/wiki/chunked-reference/ has .md files

GATE: All existing tests pass with flag=false. Script runs and produces correct
output with flag=true. Old OUTDIR output is untouched in both cases.
\```

## Phase 2: Add Release-Tree Validation Tests

\```
PHASE 2 TASK: Create tests/pytest/pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py

This test file validates the V5a release-tree contract. It should ONLY run when
a release-tree/ exists (i.e., after a smoke run with ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true).

Read the gatekeeper rules in docs/specs/v12/release-tree-contract.md §Gatekeeper Rules.

THE TEST FILE MUST:

1. Skip gracefully if release-tree/ does not exist:
   pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
       not os.path.isdir(RELEASE_TREE_DIR),
       reason="release-tree not present (ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE was false or smoke not run)"
   )

2. Find RELEASE_TREE_DIR. It will be under the smoke output root, which is
   typically tmp/ci/ somewhere. Look at how pytest_02 finds OUTDIR for the pattern.
   You may need to accept a --release-tree-root CLI arg or discover it.

3. Test: root llms.txt exists and is > 512 bytes
4. Test: root index.html exists
5. Test: at least 4 module directories exist
6. Test: every module dir has llms.txt, map.md, bundled-reference.md, chunked-reference/
7. Test: every chunked-reference/ has at least one .md file
8. Test: NO file or directory named L1-raw anywhere in release-tree/
9. Test: NO file or directory named L2-semantic anywhere in release-tree/
10. Test: the string "openwrt-condensed-docs" does NOT appear in:
    - release-tree/llms.txt
    - release-tree/llms-full.txt
    - release-tree/AGENTS.md
    - release-tree/README.md
    - release-tree/index.html
11. Test: no file matching *-skeleton.md anywhere in release-tree/
12. Test: no file matching *-complete-reference.md anywhere in release-tree/

Also extend 08-validate-output.py with a release-tree validation mode, but ONLY
when ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE is true. Add checks matching the gatekeeper rules. When
the flag is false, skip release-tree validation entirely.

VALIDATE:
  ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false python tests/run_pytest.py   (09 skips, rest pass)
  ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true python tests/run_smoke.py     (builds release-tree)
  ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=true python tests/run_pytest.py    (09 now runs and passes)

GATE: All tests pass in both flag states.
\```

## Troubleshooting Reference

\```
COMMON MISTAKES AND HOW TO AVOID THEM:

1. "Tests pass but release-tree has wrong content"
   → You forgot to rewrite paths inside the copied text files. Grep the release-tree
     for old patterns: grep -r "skeleton.md\|complete-reference.md\|L2-semantic\|openwrt-condensed-docs" release-tree/

2. "05e crashes on a missing file"
   → Some modules may not have types/ or may not have sharded parts. Always check
     existence before copying optional files. Use os.path.exists() guards.

3. "Smoke tests fail after adding 05e to the pipeline list"
   → The smoke runner executes scripts in order. 05e must come AFTER 05d and BEFORE 06.
     Check the exact position in POST_EXTRACT_PIPELINE.

4. "Tests fail with ENABLE_RELEASE_TREE=false after Phase 4 changes"
   → You broke the old code path. Every modified script must have a clean if/else
     branch. The false path must produce EXACTLY the same output as before your changes.
     Diff the output before and after.

5. "Path separator issues on Windows"
   → Always use os.path.join(). Never write "release-tree/ucode/map.md" as a path.
     In generated TEXT content (llms.txt, AGENTS.md), forward slashes are correct
     and expected — they are URLs, not filesystem paths.

6. "shutil.copy2 fails with permission error on Windows"
   → Target directory may not exist. Always os.makedirs(target_dir, exist_ok=True)
     before copying.

7. "Module discovery picks up L1-raw and L2-semantic as modules"
   → Filter your module list. A module is a directory in OUTDIR that contains
     {module}-skeleton.md (current) or map.md (V5a). L1-raw and L2-semantic are
     not modules. Neither are files (llms.txt, AGENTS.md, etc.).

8. "index.html has broken links after path rewriting"
   → index.html uses href attributes. Your replacement must handle both
     href="./openwrt-condensed-docs/..." and visible link text. Read the current
     index.html before writing replacements — understand its structure first.

FILES YOU SHOULD NEVER NEED TO TOUCH:
  - .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-01-clone-repos.py
  - .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-02a-scrape-wiki.py through 02h
  - .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-03-normalize-semantic.py
  - .github/scripts/openwrt-docs4ai-04-generate-ai-summaries.py
  - lib/ai_store.py, lib/ai_enrichment.py
  - data/base/*, data/override/*
  - tests/pytest/pytest_00_pipeline_units_test.py
  - tests/pytest/pytest_03_wiki_corpus_sanity_test.py
  - tests/pytest/pytest_04_wiki_scraper_test.py
  - tests/pytest/pytest_05_manage_ai_store_cli_test.py

IF IN DOUBT:
  - Read the file before changing it.
  - Run tests after every change, not just at the end.
  - Check both flag states.
  - Ask the human operator rather than guessing.
\```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 03:56:51Z)**_

Written to `docs/plans/v12/v5a-implementation-prompt.md`.

The prompt is structured as **copy-paste blocks** — one per phase, each self-contained:

- **Context Block** + **Rules** go into every session
- **Phase N** section gets added when you're ready for that phase
- **Troubleshooting Reference** goes in when things go sideways

Key design choices for a less capable model:

1. **Explicit file lists** — tells it exactly what to read, what to modify, what never to touch
2. **Literal string replacements** — spells out every `old → new` pattern instead of leaving it to infer from the plan
3. **Validation commands after every phase** — with both flag states
4. **"If you think X needs changing, you're wrong"** guardrails — prevents the common failure mode of over-reaching into upstream scripts
5. **One commit per phase** — keeps rollback clean
6. **Troubleshooting section** — covers the 8 most likely failure modes based on the codebase structure (Windows paths, module discovery, optional files, text rewriting)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 04:00:20Z)**_



The prompt follows the project's naming convention correctly. Specifically:

- **`05e-assemble-release-tree.py`** — uses the `05` stage family with letter suffix `e`, placing it after `05d` and within the same stage family as `05a`–`05d`. This is correct because all `05*` scripts depend on the same upstream (`03`/`04` output) and are parallelizable siblings.

- **`pytest_09_release_tree_contract_test.py`** — follows the existing `pytest_{NN}_` numbering pattern, placed after the current highest (`pytest_06`).

However, there's a subtlety worth calling out: **`05e` is not truly parallelizable with `05a`**. It reads `05a`'s output (the complete-reference and skeleton files). By the project convention documented in `ARCHITECTURE.md`, letter suffixes denote siblings that "may be parallelized in deployment when their direct dependencies are satisfied." In Phase 1, `05e` depends on `05a` through `07` — it's a post-assembly step, not a sibling.

Two options:

1. **Keep `05e` as a transitional name** — it's temporary anyway. In Phase 4, `05e` is retired and its logic is absorbed into the modified `05a`–`08`. The name only lives for Phases 1–3, then disappears.

2. **Rename to a true post-assembly position** — something like `05z` to signal "runs after all other `05*` siblings" or use a new stage number entirely, but the plan explicitly avoids bare `05` coexisting with `05a`, and `06` is taken.

The prompt as written takes option 1 — the script is explicitly documented as transitional and gets retired in Phase 4. The naming convention violation is bounded and short-lived. Want me to change the approach, or is the transitional compromise acceptable?
